review_text: “Amazing stay!!” I booked the room through orbitz.com, and got a very surprising rate. Upon check-in I discovered that the room was directly next to an elevator, and microscopic in size. Honestly, there is no way two people could have stayed in this room. Make sure you tell them that you do not want this type of room. I walked back down to the front desk, and explained that I was on vacation with my girlfriend and the room was far too small. Without hesitation, they upgraded us at no charge to an absolutely amazing room. We could not have asked for more. The room was on one of the higher floors with a south view facing the downtown skyline. The bathroom was extremely spacious (about the size of the room we had initially) with a large walk in shower and separate tub that was as deep as a swimming pool (amazing!). Room service was prompt and the food was definitely impressive albeit a bit on the expensive side. Then again, room service isn't cheap anywhere. The hotel is within walking distance of countless bars and restaurants in the downtown area. The hotel has a shuttle service that was very convenient as well. One of the employees, Tank, took us to a number of destinations in the hotel's car. Absolutely the nicest and most helpful person at the Icon. If you're looking for a place to eat near the hotel, ask to be taken to the Last Concert Cafe which is roughly a mile away on Nance St. It is a historic restaurant in Houston with great Mexican food. I cannot say enough about the Icon Hotel in Houston. Honestly I don't have a single complaint, and can't wait to stay there again. I've stayed in just about every nice hotel in Houston, and since the Warwick Hotel is no longer, I wouldn't recommend many other places than the Icon.

“Leaving so soon?” I had stayed at the Park Savoy two years ago and had no problems with it--while fairly barebones, the room I had then was comfortable with a huge closet and a decent sized bathroom.
Based on past history, I booked the Park Savoy again on very short notice and got one of the smallest rooms. No problem there--the full bed and small bathroom (shower only) were fine, but someone had left the bathroom window partially open and it was freezing in there for virtually my complete stay since I could never figure out how to work the radiator.
The big problem was related to checking out. Apparently, on the day you leave, your electronic key will not work past a certain time despite the fact that checkout is 11 am. I had gone out around 9 to sightsee and get some breakfast and when I got back at 10:30 to pack up and leave, my key would not work. It took nearly 20 minutes to get the whole thing sorted out and in retrospect, it seems kind of stupid since all I wanted to do was get back in the room and grab my suitcase. Why can't they wait until 11 to shut off the key?
That said, the Park Savoy is still a decent (and clean) budget option, but the last 30 minutes of my stay did leave a bad taste.

“Must have caught them on a bad day, I guess!” It started bad from the check-in. The person at the front desk was clearly new and took 3 times as long checking me in as it should have (there was no one else checking in.) When I asked him if, as this was my first time at the hotel, if an upgrade was "in my future" for the night, he laughed and said "no, maybe on your second visit". He then proceeded to put me in a room that was one floor above the bar. (More on this later). He then took forever to scan my ID and then gave me a room key that would not work in the elevator. When I brought it back down, a different clerk gave a key that worked. 
Now, I booked the room thru Priceline and the confirmation clearly stated, "Queen deluxe...this room has a separate seating area". The separate seating area consisted of a designer chair with what amounted to a miniature TV tray next to it at the very corner of the queen bed. I guess they must have considered this combination to almost act as the work area as there was no other work surface in the room. When I noticed my remote control wasn't working, I informed the front desk on my way out for the evening and they said they would have it fixed when I got back.,
Fast forward and its now 11:30pm, I notice the bar disco is going at FULL volume and sure enough, when I get to my room, I am serenaded by the not so melodic super bass from the bar RIGHT BELOW MY ROOM. And, of course, the remote control still does not work. I call down to report this and again, get a very sweet "I'm sorry" and "I'll send someone right up". The night bellman diagnosed and fixed the problem immediately and I was now free to watch a little TV and go to sleep.
Not so fast! Somehow, the music below got even louder, so I called down to the front desk and asked if my future was to be awake all night. They gave me a very nice "I'm sorry" and offered to move me to a higher floor which I immediately accepted. When the bellman arrived with my new key I asked him how they could book a room right above a bar that plays LOUD music until 2am! He informed me that it is a common complaint and that the hotel DOES NOT OWN the bar and have no control over its operation. I find it unconscionable that a hotel as highly rated as this one would book people into a room with this kind of known condition. 
The final touch to my experience was the wake-up call. I was leaving on a 5:45am flight the next morning and asked for a 3:30 wake-up call for a 4am car. (I even made it twice due to switching rooms). My eyes popped open at 3:40am with that panicked feeling we all know and love. Ten minutes later I receive a cheery call, "...good morning, this is your wake-up call...". When I inform the clerk that it was 20 minutes late I receive the well trained "I'm sorry" and then I hang up. I didn't even bother to check-out for fear of being further delayed, but if the manager is reading this, I hope they will have the decency of at the very least refunding the $10 wi-fi charge. Service recovery goes a long way.
I have learned in my 30 years of travel to never say never, but I can safely say it will be quite some time before the 60 Thompson sees me again.

“Horrible hotel” This is the worst ever hotel I have stayed at in NYC. Visited the hotel in December. The room was very old and extremely cold. Crowded with youngsters - tourists. The building itself - seen from outside has a Nice architecture, but the innsiden is not Nice.

“clean, but issues with service” I understand it is always hard to meet expectations in a service industry, but too many strikes against the marriott on this visit.
I reserved 3 rooms. After being told that they could all be on the same floor, only two were on the same floor. 
Having arrived at 3pm, I could only get into one room.
They messed up one of my relatives reservations, and would not honor the original room rate (only a small nightly difference).
I called for my car, which was valet parked ($45/day), and it took 35 minutes to get it, even though it was 6 in the morning. 
I went downstairs to get a luggage cart to get my suitcases out of our rooms when leaving. When I got back downstairs, I was instructed that I wasn't allowed to take the carts. 
Other items to note: No lobby to speak of. No food other than breakfast. "room service" is delivery from a nearby restaurant. 
As you can read, none of these were serious offenses. The rooms were clean. Shower had plenty of hot water. Rooms were a perfectly reasonable size. Breakfast buffet was fine.
I'll stay elsewhere the next time I am in NYC. At $200/night, this would have been fine. At $375, I expected more.

“One night's stay” Stayed one cold night in December. Room was nice and well decorated with a fancy shower but no tub. Lobby and bar were really nice, the room came close but was relatively small and unfortunately was supposed to be non-smoking but from the burns on the floor and the smell on the phone was obviously for smoking. A bit noisy too, caused by an adjacent occupant. Overall, pretty good.

“For expense-account travelers and the wealthy” The Westin is an excellent hotel if you are OK with spending $500/night for your room (including taxes) or having someone spend it on your behalf. The $500 is just for a start, though, since continental breakfast is $20, wired Internet access is $15, the exercise facility is $10, designer water in the room is $7, and even printing your airline boarding pass is $1.50. If you upgrade (higher room rate) to the Executive room, then you get a 17" iMac in the room (nice touch!), a 32" LCD TV, and access to the lounge, which includes a continental breakfast.
As for the location, you are literally on top of 9 subway lines, a block from 40 movie theaters, close to the Port Authority Bus Terminal, and within walking distance of all of the Broadway theaters. If you like the activity of the Times Square neighborhood, this hotel or the nearby Hilton and Marriott is for you. However, the Millennium Broadway and the Muse (Kimpton) are not far away, and they are considerably quieter. (I've stayed in all of these hotels over the years, but personally prefer to stay on the quieter Upper West Side near Broadway and the number 1 subway line).
As I'm an "urban survivor" by nature, I didn't mind the frequent sirens on 42nd Street or 8th Avenue, but that might be an annoyance for some visitors. On the second night of my stay, we were awakened at 4:30 AM by a fire alarm and a sequence of announcements broadcast over a speaker system into the guest rooms. After 20 minutes, we were told that it was a false alarm. From what I could tell, the hotel staff treated this event as a routine occurrence, so make sure that you bring pajamas and a robe with you for a possible room evacuation.

“An Elegant Past for a Modern Present” We spent two nights at the flagship St. Regis hotel in New York City in February 2007. What a wonderful stay – we’d go back again in a minute. 
This hotel boasts a kind of hushed elegance that strikes just the right counterbalance to the hustle and bustle of New York City. The location, on Fifth Avenue in the mid-50s, is far enough away to avoid the crowds of the theater district but close enough to make getting around simple and straightforward. The lobby and public spaces are both grand and intimate and the hallways are hushed and elegant. The flowers and fine decoration everywhere make it seem the height of luxury. The hotel’s layout, design, service and amenities are all as top-notch as you would expect. 
The highlight of our stay was the room. We were upgraded to an Astor Suite and greatly enjoyed the extra space. The rooms are traditional, but with modern amenities and the result is a nice blend of both elegance and technology that is not an easy thing to pull off in a hotel room. The St. Regis succeeds very well in this regard, probably because it has always been a high-end hotel and changes made during the renovation simply updated what was already there. We’re not sure if they added soundproofing during the renovation or if the hotel has always been so quiet: either way it’s a truly relaxing escape, with no outside sounds to interfere with the wonderful ambiance of the rooms. 
The front desk service, housekeeping and concierges are all excellent. We particularly enjoyed the food and beverage presentations, complimentary pressing and shoe shines and “no problem” attitude of the wonderful butler service. The only drawback to our stay was the service in the restaurant and bar. We’re Four Seasons people and we’re accustomed to service in the bar and restaurant being one of the highlights of our stay at those properties. Despite the elegance and luxury of the St. Regis, the bar service was perfunctory at best and the breakfast service was slow and mostly unfriendly. That’s a shame, because the space (even with the current construction in the restaurant) is beautiful. We didn’t sit in the bar itself because the small space was overflowing with people (we sat in the lower bar/restaurant area instead), but it looked very nice and we’d recommend at least seeing it during your stay at this hotel. 
This is not a hotel for families on vacation in New York City: it’s a more refined and elegant space than the typical tourist hotel. It’s probably also not a hotel for serious business travelers who need service beyond their rooms: the business center is a dark room off the staircase with what looks like discarded furniture and very little privacy. This is a hotel for people looking for an elegant, luxurious, relaxing place to stay in the heart of New York City. If that’s what you want, and you’re willing to splurge to get it, this is the hotel for you.

“Love this Hotel !!!” The rooms are cozy and comfy, the staff is courteous, kind and thoughtful and the location is ideal. The only problem I have ever had here is some parking issues, but they were always addressed immediately and resolved on the spot. I will come back again and again and again.
PS. - the bar is great and food is good.

“Marriott Frequent Guest's/Business Folks, you will be upset” This property has not been renovated. Old rooms, very small, you need to close the door to the bathroom to move around in it. As a platinum member of Marriott, it is not up to par with all other full service Marriott's. I have stayed at the Courtyard near by, which was much nicer. The lounge on the 12th was tiny, standard soda/water, but very small. My bathroom sink did not drain, no wired internet, only wireless. Nice staff, but there is nothing they can do... No platinum parking. Worked late one night and ordered room service, the wings are no good...

“Nothing special” The great advantages are location and price. It's located in the heart of Theater District, a few steps from Time Square. The price is fair for a well located hotel with a standard room. They don't have free wireless internet. I paid a fortune to use internet and print a sheet. I would go back there and recomend to a friend.

“The nice Thompson Hotel” I've stayed at other Thompson hotels and this one is probably the best. The hotel is fairly new so all the rooms/amenities are quite nice and clean. The hotel bar/lounge was a great spot for people to meet up for a drink before we headed out and the staff were super nice.
We got a good rate ($240 per night) right after fashion week in late Feb, but I hear this hotel is normally about $100 more. Either way I recommend searching online for better deals than calling the hotel. Also, get a deal with breakfast included. Its a nice convenience and the food is better than the breakfast you'll get at the Deli next door.
If you stay here you'll LOVE the shower (rains down on you from above) and you'll have to check out Meatball, right around the corner. That restaurant does this great meatball trays and they have the best Ice Cream sandwiches late-night. Yep - it's horrible for a diet but it's a great way to feel like a kid again! =)

“Huge difference!” I've stayed at The Plaza several times before they renovated and downsized. What a huge improvement they've made! I was a little surprised for a weekend in August the hotel didn't have the usual busy buzz, but I much preferred that than sifting my way through a busy lobby with lots of tourists snapping pictures. The hotel did a fantastic job keeping the beautiful aspect of the iconic hotel's old world charm and infusing the new at the same time. I spent a great deal of time in the bathroom, how can one not when the tub is so massive. The one small problem, it's not like the Four Seasons where the tub fills up in 60 seconds. It took a very very long time for it to fill. The gold plated faucets didn't look so tacky offset with the white. Bathrobes were plush and comfortable.
I didn't take advantage of the vast array of services the hotel offers, but then when you're in NY you want to do more outside than hide in a hotel. I would definitely check out Todd English's food outlet in the basement. It's a great place to grab something quick to eat. The concierge recommended a French Bistro that we were not overly impressed with. 
It's a very pricey hotel, I would only recommend it for those who want to stay for a special occasion.

“No Complaints; No Raves” One night business trip, Location worked and rate was quite reasonable. Complimentary upgrade to 1 bdr w/kitchen (thank you!). Staff was pleasant; room clean. Decor was almost depressing in the lack of style. All beige and dated - felt dirty even though it was a clean room. Insufficient electric oulets and lighting. Shower was weird -- required instructions (helpful) but still no way to turn it on w/o getting wet. Equipped kitchen (lost on me), but no coffee maker (?). Hotel makes a big deal out of the linens. Don't know why. Bed was at best a 5 on a scale of 1-10. Didn't eat in restaurant. Went next door instead to decent pub (loud; great beer selection; food surprisingly good). I'm not disappointed with the stay, but I won't hurry back either because I can't think of a reason to do so.

“Recent Renovations Need Another Renovation” I am a frequent visitor to NYC and have stayed at many of the larger hotels in the city. The Flatotel was a property I had wanted to stay at for some time...finally got the opportunity in April, 2005. The service was terrific. After an early morning flight, I arrived at the hotel just after breakfast, expecting to be told I could store my luggage until check-in time. What a surprise when they allowed me to check into my room. The staff, from the front desk to the bar staff to the women working in the small gift shop were all pleasant and exceedingly friendly. The room, and the hallways, although recently renovated, already look like they are due for another renovation. The bathroom was large and clean...but the room itself looked a bit rough around the edges. Had a cheap kettle in the room that never warmed up enough to make tea/coffee, nothing in the bar fridge and beds were not particularly comfortable. Location can't be beat.

“Good stay but could have been better” My daughter and i visited Chicago to see the city and go to a couple of cubs games from 8 May -13 May and were using reward points. i will do a pros and cons for this stay:
PROS-
1. early checkin- big thumbs up to be able to drop bags off and not worry about coming back later to checkin.
2. nice breakfast-had just about anything you would have wanted.
3. location-i will put this in the pros and also the cons. really like how close the "L" was to the hotel.
4. really close to some good resteraunts like the Weber Grill and Rosebud Trattoria.
Cons:
1. using points i was hoping for a bigger room with a somewhat decent view...room 1021 had a great view of the huge crane next door and the construction of the building or some structure going up. not the hotels fault but...
2. the extremely loud club next door which was banging techno music until at least 3am. again not the hotels fault but be warned if you are a light sleeper. i would suggest to get on the front side of the hotel on the street with Ruth Chris to help drown out the noise
3. Parking-we took the "L" everywhere but if you have a car it is $54 a day for parking. ouch! 
4. elevators-had some minor issues. i am all for security but i had trouble with my room key using the key pad to get the elevator. i kept getting a yellow light and had to have the card changed out twice to get it to work. one day the key pad was not working and the elevator was done and some people were walking up 10-20 stories to get to their room. finally you may want to allow time on Sundays for getting to the lobby for checkout. took about 15 minutes to get an elevator with what seemed like everyone coming down at the same time (again this is not the hotels fault, just a friendly warning.
normally i stay at the Residence Inn on East Walton Street but chose this hotel from the reviews. i would stay here again but i would make sure that the room is on the other side of the hotel. if i couldn't then i would go back up 1 stop on the "L" to the CHicago stop and stay in a quieter area of town at the Residence Inn.

“Dark Rooms dated decor but wonderful staff” You have to pity the excellent staff at this hotel. Their good work is let down by the quality of the facilities. Every encounter with the staff was pleasant and all my requests were fulfilled. 
The decor was at one time excellent - 1988. 
The room i got was dark and the bathroom was small and smelled musty.
There were no light fixtures in the room - only lamps. The room felt worn and dated. I could see the dust in the carpets around the doors. 
The elevators had worn call buttons and the corridors leading to the rooms, although wide were dark.
I was there on a business conference. The conference facilities were very good. The staff managed food and beverage service very efficiently. The quality of the food was also quite nice.
Stay here if you get a really cheap rate or your company is paying for it. There could be no complaint about the location.

“Great place to stay” This a great place to base yourself for a trip to New York. The hotel is located on a relatively quiet street just out of the hustle and bustle. It's just a short walk away from Penn Station (we got the train to and from Newark Airport, The Meadowlands and Prudential Center), Madison Square Garden, Empire State Building and others. There's also the option of a subway station right on the doorstep, but NY is best experienced on foot. The hotel is relatively new so everything is in good condition. Our 23rd floor city view room was nice and clean but quite small (but apparently above average for NY according to other reviews). We were looking North which is superior to the rooms which have a view of the Empire State Building. The beds were comfortable, but there is no in-room safe which is unsatisfactory. The Business Center has 4 computers which are free to use and there is also free wi-fi internet access in your room. We didn't have any problems with the lifts mentioned in some reviews. We only had breakfast once in the hotel due to the excessive cost. Staff were friendly and helpful when required. We were very happy with our choice of hotel.

“Great location and stay” This hotel is more of a boutique one and very nicely located within Times Square, a close subway stop and Broadway. The staff couldn't have been nicer and more helpful. We enjoyed drinks here but never a meal so I can't comment on the food. The only thing I wish they had in the rooms was a coffee/tea service for those times when you just wanted to stay in your room and relax in the morning. We had a Superior Room with an incredible view of the Paramount. Nice twin beds with comfy sheets. The bedspread was beautiful but with it's satin lining, I had a heck of a time keeping it on the bed during the night. An extra blanket would have been nice on the bed instead (with the spread folded back). The room wasn't large but suitable for what we needed. The bathroom could have used a bit of bleach on the tile but otherwise everything was clean and shiny. Thanks for a wonderful stay in an incredible city!

“Wanted to Love But Ended Up Loathing & Checking Out Early!” We spent Thanksgiving in NYC, and used American Express Travel Rewards, and we had planned on staying for three nights. I contacted the hotel prior to arrival and told them it was our anniversary, and requested a room on a higher floor, and was pleasantly surprised upon check-in that we had been upgraded to a junior suite, we had originally booked a premier king sized room.
Our room was on the 10th floor, and we stayed in room 1015. The hallways are very, very dark and narrow. It was an odd configuration, as you descended down the hallway, there are three steps that lead to three rooms, with a service elevator outside. This became an issue when food service trays, and laundry were left in the hallway for hours at a time, the pictures tell the story.
The room while on appearances, looked nice, I didn't know why it was classified as a junior suite, usually there is a partial wall, divider, separate sitting area, and there wasn't, just a couch against a wall. The nightstands had clearly seen better days, as both were heavily battered, and had chipping paint, so did the wall in the bathroom.
One of the main reasons I booked this hotel was the Kimpton Hotel chain prides itself on being environmentally responsible and eco-savvy, and that is something I strive for in my personal life, and given the option when travelling, I will always book the eco option. Kimpton's website boasts of awards, accolades, and efforts the chain takes, so imagine my surprise when I turned on the lamp on the nighstand, and it was a regular lightbulb, there wasn't one compact fluorscent lightbulb in our room. Hotel chains such as Holiday Inn's don't tout their policies, and they have cfl's. Rule one of being environmentally responsible is cfl's. 
The cleaning products used by housekeeping eminated the worst odor ever, that gave me a sickening headache, and I have never experienced that before in a hotel, ever. Again, website touts non-toxic cleaning products, but this stuff stunk and certainly smelled toxic.
Our room faced Park Avenue, and as other reviews have noted, the soundproofing is non-existent. Its to be expected in NYC to experience noise, but this was just unbearable, and its not the busiest part of Park Avenue. I didn't even bother requesting a move since I heard a man complaining about it at the front desk, and the clerk told him there was nothing else available.
Thanksgiving Day, we got up, after two nights of no sleep, we got up to go to the parade, and I practically tripped over a very large bag of laundry outside the service elevator, and there was no one around, and there was a room service tray that looked like it had been out for hours. Coming back from the parade two hours later, the items were still in the hallway, tired, not wanting to navigate anymore, we decided to check out.
Went downstairs, bags packed, no clue where we were going, too tired to argue, plan or think. The same man that was also sleepless was again asking if an inside room was available, and it wasn't. I told him we were getting out, and he said he had decided to move next door to the Kitano. I wanted out of the neighborhood. The clerk was surprised we were checking out a day early, since it was an award, and I knew we wouldn't get those points back from American Express, nothing was worth me not sleeping. We ended up at the W Times Square, paying $350 a night for a gorgeous, quiet room that to us was priceless and perfect, and we slept through Thanksgiving, but were thankful for sleep, and not having to navigate through narrow hallways.

“Spacious and really nice hotel” I've stayed in Manhattan hotels quite a bit and this is the first hotel I've stayed in that had a normal sized room, with normal sized closets and with a normal sized bathroom! And that last one was the biggest surprise! Everything in my room was really nice, the lobby is pretty cool looking and the bar was hopping at night and the bartender Mike really had it under control. Don't let the one elevator being remodeled keep you away...be patient or take the stairs and get your fitness level up! Speaking of which, they had a really nice fitness facility with a few weight machines, weighted balls, stability balls, treadmills, free weights, benches and mats for your ab work. I was really happy about that! I stayed a few extra days because the hotel was so nice as was their staff and of course to hang around with my friends in the city. It's not in the middle of all the tourist mayhem but it's definitely within walking distance if that's what you're looking. I highly recommend this hotel and can't wait to go back!

“Déceptif” Un hotel cher (pres de 350 USD la chambre par nuit) pour :
- une chambre en etat tres moyen et daté
- un coffre de chambre ne fonctionnant pas
- une climatisation défaillante (pres de 24 degre celsios avec la clim poussé à fond)
- un service de pressing qui froisse les chemises
- un ménage à 14 heures ! 
- une réception très impersonnelle et blasée
Cet hotel s'est endormi sur ses lauriers bien passé avec des couts excessifs pour un service très moyens !
L'hotel meriterait une renovation generale et un personnel plus attentif.
Je n'y retournerais pas et vous conseille de rechercher ailleurs.

“Ditto, bring your earplugs” I recognize New York is noisy, and while you're up high in the Hilton Millenium, construction at ground zero can be pretty loud, even on a weekend. The rooms are very clean and for business travellers to downtown, the location couldn't be better. It's a bit overpriced, but which hotel in New York is not.

“Rooms are too small for families” We stayed at this hotel for one night. I reserved two rooms hoping for adjoining rooms. The hotel does not have any rooms that connect. The rooms (even by NY) standards are way too small. With luggage, and a stroller, we were having a difficult time just walking around the room. This is a new hotel so the rooms and common areas are very clean. If you are a business man looking for a clean place to stay then I recommend this hotel. If you are a family, then I would not recommend this hotel. Apart from the rooms being small, the location isn't that great. you are technically a block and a half from Time Square, but that half block seems a bit too "sketchy." Again, a business man by himself, not a problem. Walking around with the kids, I didn't feel that comfortable, especially at night.
The staff were okay. They were friendly and accommodating. The check-in process could be better as it seemed like there was only one person checking in guests.

“Great location and good value for what we paid” Booked a two night stay on Priceline and paid only $110/night. Arrived at the hotel very early and requested check in at 9AM. Desk staff was able to find us a room in 45 minutes and we were happy to take whatever we got. Room was small with a Queen bed but very comfortable. Based on the rate we paid there was nothing to complain about. Be warned however that this is a large convention based hotel that caters to groups. There were always long lines at check in and some dude in the lobby tried to sell us a timeshare when we asked for a map. The thing that really left a bad taste in our mouth was after checking out we left our bags with the bellman as we had a late flight. The charge to hold the items was $3.50 per piece. (This was on top of the tip to the bellman for taking the bags) Maybe this is just a New York thing but we did not appreciate it. All in all we got what we paid for.

“Bedroom nice, Bathroom bad.” Never having stayed in the Galleria area before, I was very confused by the two Westin's essentially on the same block. Yes, there are two. The Westin Oaks Galleria and The Westin at the Galleria. The Westin at The Galleria has better reviews by and large than The Westin Oaks. I thought I was making reservations at The Westin Galleria but in fact was booking at the other Westin. My transportation from the airport was even confused by it all. 
Staff at The Westin Oaks was very friendly and seemed responsive to my needs. I complained about smoking on my floor within the first 24 hours and the problem was addressed. This is a non smoking property but I never would have known that when I checked in-the smoke smell in the hallways was very thick. 
One advantage of this property is the elevator system-one hardly has to ever wait for an elevator and the elevators don't stop at every floor on the way up or down. 
My bedroom was spacious and clean for the most part. Mattress seemed brand new and very comfortable. Linens on bed were high quality. Nice views of Post Oak area and beyond. 
And then there was the bathroom. I was in room 1708, by the way because I read other reviews of water issues and the manager wanted to know which room the guest was in. I reported one of the problems when it occurred. A repair man came up after 30 minutes claiming that someone above me must have overfilled the tub. Hah! Just looking at the bathroom ceiling when I checked in, was evidence that there has been chronic water problems in the ceiling. The tiles were covered with furry stuff and corroding. Large spaces between ceiling tiles. What happened was water pouring in great quantities, including pieces of the ceiling from the 18th floor bathroom above me into the bathtub. Lucky for me that I was not in the bathtub when this happened. By the time the repair man arrived the water had stopped. I can't imagine this would be very healthy. 
But there was another issue with the bathroom and that was the very hot water in the toilet. Every time I went to the bathroom it was like a sauna on my bottom. I didn't see steam rising up but it sure felt like it and one time I thought I had been burned. I am not kidding about this. I think I could have roasted marshmallows over the john! It was very disconcerting. I never put my hand in the water because I thought I would freak out. The toilet storage tank was even hot to the touch. 
When you turn on the water in a bathtub it usually runs cold and turns warmer the further you turn the handle. With this bathtub it was the opposite. It starts out hot and turns cold the further you turn the handle.
The wallpaper in the bathroom was stained and dirty-one area could have possibly been blood stains. Look behind the painting hanging over the toilet.
So what's the deal with even renting out rooms in this condition? Does the Hotel hope to get lucky and hope the guests won't make a fuss? Does the front desk think that guests of a certain age will just suck it up? I should have asked to change rooms but sometimes the hassle of packing everything up & moving is just overwhelming. Plus I was not in the room that much. 
I would have had a beautiful stay if it were not for this issue. It was a major issue. Because of my experience here, I will think twice before I even book at another Westin.

“This place is a dump.” This place is dated and decrepit. Furniture is ready for Goodwill. Unattractive lobby. Chipped paint and tiny bathroom. Depressing. Good location does not make up for the run-down quality of the hotel. Stay somewhere else and use the good Chicago public transportation to get to the sights.

“Nice hotel, not your ordinary W” We had a nice stay at the W. The hotel is not laid out like a normal W. It has more light and not the groovy ambiance of what I expect from W hotels. The location is great. Sort of in the center of Manhattan. Lots of restaurants and shops and a short walk to Greenwich Village. On Saturdays there is a green market at Union Square which is pretty amazing. The bar downstairs always seems to have a lot people. We had a mega room which is bigger than their standard room, so room size was not an issue. The first room we had was showing some wear an tear. We had to move because apparently some one had gotten sick on the carpet and the smell was lingering. The front desk was very nice and moved us immediately. Our second room was in very good condition. There was nothing spectacular about the decor. Very standard earth earth tones. The bed was very comfortable. We had no problems with the service and would definitely stay there again.

“OK, but does not meet Hilton standards.” OK, but does not meet Hilton standards. Right on U. of Texas which has +'s and -'s but is aprox. $100 less then the Hilton Med. Center which is about 5 miles away. There's no gym on site, but you can use the campus gym. There's a good game room and fast food directly across from the hotel at the student center and the campus is nice, but the hotel rooms are old and don't meet the standards of the upgrades Hilton has been making. OK option for a business stay, but I wouldn't chose this a get away destination.

“Terrific x 2” Just completed my second stay at The Royalton. This is a terrific hotel. Nothing has changed near as I can tell from my prior visit, but it remains in good order and very well maintained. What stands out from my second experience is the staff. I cannot fault one of them. Unfailingly friendly, helpful, cheerful. I was only there two night but I could tell they were making an effort to remember their guests by face, at least. Can't think of a single staff member who wasn't friendly and motivated. They are running a terrific hotel.

“Spacious & Spacious” I've stayed here once before on business. I remember not being too pleased. I had to wait til like 5-6 to check in... then the room was messed up in some way so they ended up upgrading me. This time the room was perfect. I choose the hotel because of the great rates but was a little unsure because of the way the first stay went. My fears were thankfully unwarranted. The room was very spaces. I was in town to hang out with friends so the big double bed room seemed huge given the usual tiny rooms you find in NYC. The location is ideal if you wanna be close to times square but not right in the middle of all the hassle and bustle. 
Oh I can't forget to mention one thing that bothered me... I know its not the hotels fault... But when I used the shower for like a second or two the water came out some weird rusty color and eww it freaked me out. It happened twice. I wanna assume its because it has old pipes or something I dunno.

“Just a Bad Stay” We were attending a function during the week and our main objective was to find a hotel that was near Penn Station to catch a 6:00am train home the next morning. The location of the Doubletree was only a few blocks from Penn Station and seemed like a good fit.
I can't even begin with the list of things that were not up to the Doubletree standard.
First although the location is near Penn Station, it's on a dowdy side street that mostly has business that are out of business. It's a very depressing street.
When we arrived the lobby was a nightmare. It was tiny and jammed with people. The front desk is just a podium for two people. There were no lines just clusters of people all fighting and arguing who was next. Each person seemed to have a problem that needed fixing now. It was chaos and bedlam. There was a woman sprawled out on the lobby couch sound asleep.
When we got our turn we too had a problem.
Originally I had booked one king room, however later it was determined that our daughter would join us and we required a room for three people. I called the hotel direct. I was told they did not have rollaways at this hotel, but we settled on a double (two beds). Somehow the day before we left I pulled the reservation and called a second time to check on the correct bedding (as I never received a second confirmation. They did not have me in a double room - still a king. So again I asked for a double room and they said it was taken care of. Well of course we get to the hotel and I still have a king bed for three people! The woman at the desk made it into a huge deal. She blamed me for calling reservations and not the hotel (I DID call the hotel direct), she said I called the wrong Doubletree (so how did they pull my reservation each time), she accused me of lying that I never called. Well finally it turned out they did still double rooms open - so why instead of all the hassle and words with us did she just not change it to a double room.
I think she was mad at us as she made sure we didn't get the Doubletree cookies at check-in. She was short tempered and rude.
When we got to the room it was tiny - postage stamp tiny. The room just fit two bed and no space to unpack or move around. When we took out the ironing board to iron our clothing the other people had to sit on the beds as there was no other room. Our view was of a building across the street and two massive water towers. The room stunk of old cigarette and cigar smoke. 
When we left for the function we could not get a cab as it was raining and no cabs come down this depressing side street. Nobody at the hotel was willing to get us a cab. We had to go back to our room and arrange a private car pick up. The staff was amazingly unhelpful.
The hotel was just shabby, small, poorly run and the staff rude. There was a aura of things going wrong as most guests in the lobby were complaining about something.
The worst part was that the room was $350.00 per night. I could have gotten a much nicer hotel a bit farther from Penn Station for around $199.00. We paid about $150.00 per night to stay in one of the worst hotels I have ever stayed in!

“Budget Travelers Only!” This is a European style hotel (think small) in a superb location that is suitable only for a budget traveller. It's location near the Hancock building makes it an ideal location. But it is very shabby, and everything has a very worn appearance. If you want a great location, can get a bargain rate (we paid about half of what any other hotel in the area cost), and just need a place to sleep, this might be your place. If you want to impress someone with luxury, great service, and nice furnishings, or can't get a great rate, choose another location.

“Excellent Stay, Excellent Service” The Four Seasons was an excellent stay for a short two night trip. The location was excellent, right in the downtown area, very close to the light rail that goes through town, maybe a 5-10 minute walk. The staff was very polite, free shoe shining was very nice and turn down service always seemed to occur when I was out at dinner which was really perfect, service never got in the way. 
The bed was amazing, one of the most comfortable that I have ever stayed in. Shower was a bit strange, not the best shower in the entire world, but certainly livable. Only complaint I had really was that the razor they brought for me was a 2 blade razor... guess I just shouldn't have forgotten my own.
An excellent hotel, and I had a great deal with the 2nd night of my stay for free.

“cheap for a reason” if i could give this hotel 2.5 stars I would. It is not a dump but was clean for my stay. The room was small but not the smallest i've stayed in. It was in a decent neighborhood one block from the subway and the desk clerk was efficient. That said, the hotel has no ice machine, you have to buy it by the cup across the street for $.75 a cup. They have limited TV and no Fox News. The elevator (1) was always too busy to pick me up since it has an attendant and I had to walk up and down 3 flights of stairs with my bags. You can do worse than this hotel but when its rates are below $!20 a night, it sure beats the Ganesvoort across the street at $400 a night.

“Horrible, really horrible!” This hotel is right in the heart of North Michigan Avenue. A great location, but a crummy hotel. When you enter the hotel you have to take an elevator to the third floor to check in. It can be confusing. Then you have to switch over to another bank of elevators to get to your room. Parking is $40.00 a day. 
The lobby of the hotel is beautiful. Too bad the rest of the hotel doesn't measure up. When we were waiting in line we watched and overheard another customer arguing with a check-in clerk. The problem was that she needed a room with two beds, not one. The clerk told the customer "Sorry you booked on Priceline we don't have to honor ANY special requests. You get what we give you" !!!! HUH? We were floored as we had booked on Priceline ourselves. We didn't ask for anything special, we quietly checked in. We usually ask for a nonsmoking king and 99% out of 100 times hotels accomodate us. We have never encountered such rudness.
Not saying anything to the person checking us in was a huge mistake. We were checked into room 623. We have had rooms on cruise ships larger than this room and it was right across from the elevator bank so we could hear everything. The room was the size of a walk in closet with a only a double bed. The carpet was dirty. The linen on the bed was worn. If the linens on the bed were clean or not was questionable. So much for that "comfort bed" they advertise. If this hotel had them we didn't see it in our room. Our phone didn't work and our windows didn't have a darkening shade. You could see the hooks for them, but the window didn't have one. We called the front desk, no chance of changing rooms because we booked on Priceline, however the said they would send some one up to see about the window shade. They never showed up.The windows were filthy. The furniture was nicked and worn. The wallpaper was worn. The water trickled out of the showerhead. Ugh, what a nightmare! 
Thank goodness we were only spending one night there. NEVER AGAIN! We and others were treated horribly because we booked with priceline. When are hotel employees going to figure out online booking portals are here to stay and if they don't like it, then don't release the rooms to be booked through online travel websites. This hotel does not deserve the three stars listed on priceline and other travel websites.

“Largest rooms in New York!” With the subway system out the front door this is a great hotel and a must stay. Embrace the subway you will love new York! Beds are perfect, windows from top to bottom and full wall looking out on the world trade center it's amazing and a must see!

“We loved this hotel !!” We loved the hotel – great customer service, always met with a nice friendly greeting and a smile when we left or came back to the hotel and the concierge, David, was the best. If he didn’t know, then no-one would know !! And above all, a great rate for a junior suite. 
I had orginally asked for a junior suite with a great view however when we arrived, it was raining, I was cold and wet and had bronchitus and my other half had the flu so we asked if there was anything available right away so we could get out of our wet clothes and have a hot shower. We were given a junior suite right away - no great view but in the end that didn't matter.
Location
We stayed 10 nights at the Affinia Dumont in late May / early June as we wanted to be in a neighbourhood which had more of a flavour to where “real” New Yorkers live. The hotel is located on East 34th Street between Lexington and 3rd Ave. The subway (green line) is only a block or so away and the good thing about this location is that the station at Union Square is great for changing to other lines. Cross town buses were easily picked up – from west to east, alight just beyond Park Ave or at 3rd Ave. You can also catch a bus to the East River Ferry in 34th Street too so it is quite a handy location.
Adjacent to the Affinia Dumont is a restaurant “the Barking Dog”, and a Baskins Robbins ice creamery. There’s also a Thai restaurant and a Deli on the corner too. Opposite the Affinia Dumont is a Duane Reade pharmacy and market which is handy if you need to top up medications or other items or see a doctor (weekdays only).
We bought fruit at the street vendor cnr 34th / 3rd Ave as his fruit was nicer and cheaper than in the Deli / Duane Reade. Nice bread (as opposed to plastic wrapped varieties) can be bought at any of the Zabars bakeries in grand Central.
Along 3rd and Lexington Aves there are many other restaurants and cafes and shops and on some weekends, Lexington Ave has a street fair which runs from 34th to about 23rd Street.
Around 8 or so blocks north of 34th Street is Grand Central station which is easily walkable from the hotel. 
There’s a bank with a couple of ATMs on the corner of 34th and Lexington too … really the location has everything at your fingertips.
The room
Our room was a junior suite and had plenty of room and amenities for us. It was decorated in dark browns (carpet and woods) with light grey, beiges and light olive green and cream accents. We had a double bed and a huge flat screen TV, a large desk and a sofa and tub chair. Our kitchenette had a full size fridge and stove and washing up facilities too. Our room was nicely maintained by housekeeping and always spic and span when we came back from being out and about.
If there was one small critiscm, there were no cooking utensils such as eg flips and large spoons suitable for stirring a pot etc and only one large bowl which we used to put fruit in. However it wasn’t a great concern as we ate out most of the time or brought back cooked food from nearby restaurants / delis after all, we were on holidays.
Laundry facilities
There is a guest laundry in the basement with about 4 machines and dryers as well as a soap dispenser. The washing machines take 8 Quarters as do the dryers and the soap powder costs $1.
Internet access
Internet access is available throughout hotel and during our stay, the cost was $13.95 per day. I didn't see any nearby cafes apart from one on 28th/Lexington Ave that actually had a notice that free WiFi was available and I certainly didn't feel like lugging my Notepad around NYC to go to a Burger King just to get free WiFi. The way I figured it was that I could pay $13.95 for internet access every now and then or go to a cafe and order a couple of coffees which with tip would probably be similar to the cost of access through the hotel.

“Could be handled a lot better” We were there the weekend after the black out.It was very hard to find the hotel. The street outside the hotel was under construction and you could see huge rats running around. The rooms were small but nice and clean. Unfortunately, we were not warned that there was no hot water because of the black out. We would have been prepared or cancel our trip. No compensation was given to us.Overall, if you dont mind staying in lower Manhattan, the hotel was very nice for the price.

“Showing Her Age” This used to be an elegant hotel but it is in need of some renovations. The lobby is beautiful but it stops there. The carpetin is stained and shold have been replaces years ago. The room layout was good for the 80's but not today. People need a big desk for a computer and the room is too small for that. There is little benefit from their membership program. No executive lounge and no free breakfast. I prefer the Marriot if going to Greenspoint.

“everything was just ok” I'm not usually a Hilton guy, but attended a conference at this property so stayed there recently. Staff was invariably very nice, which was a big plus. They also have abundant free parking. That was about all that was above average, at least for me. Bed was decent, but not nearly as comfortable as the beds in the new Holiday Inn Expresses or the Sheraton brand beds.
The A/C was not working properly when I got to the room. Called down and they sent someone to fix it. 
I also found that the bathroom was strangely laid out and cramped. Could not lay a bath mat down and open the door as the door would move the mat across the floor. The toilet was very close to the shower, so when sitting on the toilet a knee is pressing against the tub.
The biggest problem I had was the Wifi. It's done through a third party, not Hilton. So I paid my $13 to find out that it was dreadfully slow. So slow that it took perhaps 45-60 seconds to load a single webpage. I'm on a business trip and trying to log into my office from this awful connection and was, literally, unable to work. The connection was not even strong enough to use gotomypc. I called the front desk who sent me to tech support for the third party company. I spent about an hour on the phone with them and they were of no help at all. Said they'd check more and call me back but they did not. I called them back later and again, they did not help and said, again, that they would escalate it and call me back. The definitely did not do the latter. I have no idea about the former.
My download speed was averaging about .08 mps. That's really, really slow.
So in order to do work on a business trip I was relegated to going to the Starbucks up the road. This is not very conducive to getting work done, or having a relaxing stay.
I will definitely not stay here again. As a business traveler I don't need much, and I spend a lot of time on the road. A comfy room, a decent Wifi connection, and a friendly staff. I got only 1 of the 3 here.

“Decent Location but........” This hotel has supposedly been recently renovated, but other than a flat screen TV and a motion activated lighting system that turned itself on and off randomly, there was little evidence of this. Our carpets were heavily stained. The bathroom has no vent/fan and steams up; there is an ever present mildew odor. The shower was useless and the plumbing has clearly not been touched in decades - in fact we were told at the gym that we should use the showers there as they were the best in the buidling. The door staff did not offer to help us carry our bags in. We waited for 20 minutes after calling to have our bags picked up as we were leaving and no one came. However, the beds were very comfortable.

“Dark does not equal Hip” this is a strangely designed hotel. reminds me of a comfort inn where all the rooms face into a central internal courtyard. and, they keep the hallways incredibly dark. almost impossible to find the room numbers. once inside, the rooms can be small, but functional. lots of dark browns and greens. it's very 70s. 
the location is decent and you can easily walk to some of TriBeCa's best restaurants. as a business traveler, i stay here when the rate slips under $200, as at that price point it's a good deal based on the location -- and it's nice to mix it up and explore some downtown neighborhoods. 
the bar is awful, however. horrible servers, no energy. just tired and old. they should re-do it.

“Location! Location! Location!” Was initially concerned about staying here after reading other reviews. My husband is a diamond member @ Hiltons so I was expecting to be pleasantly surprised. & I was! Service great, although the breakfast wasnt great it was nice to have a Starbucks on the premise & you can NOT beat the location!

“Long Wait for Airport Pickup” I booked a Park and Stay reservation at this hotel and strongly recommend against anyone else making that mistake. The hotel itself was fine though the "suites" are not much bigger than an average Marriott hotel room. It is new and clean and everything worked save for the shuttle back to the hotel upon my return. I had to call twice and wait more than a half hour to be picked up at the international Terminal E. If the hotel were not less than ten minutes from the terminal I might understand but when I first called they told me a shuttle was already on the way.
As a Gold level member I stay at a lot of Marriott hotels but will never again make the mistake of purchasing a park and stay rate at this Marriott. They did not apologize for the delay at the time nor have they responded to my survey responses on their service.

“Affordable Comfort” I really enjoyed the stay. It is located at 41st/Broadway, which is like 3 blocks away from Times Square - super close. Also within 10 blocks of Top Of The Rock (a must see!). 
Pros
- Very comfortable bedding
- Quick response by Housekeeping staff
- Coffee/Tea maker in room and in lobby
- Room is not too small - just right
- Plasma TV
- Clean 
- Good service/hospitality
- Free internet (wireless with good connectivity)
- Lots and lots of good restaurants and small restaurants nearby, since this is afterall Times 
- Convinence Store nearby
- Fitness Center
- Good air conditioning
- privacy - room key to enter hotel at night, and elevator floors at all times
- iron board
Cons
- Phone call to Reception can take over 5 minutes, so if you want something, either call Operator or House Keeping, much faster
- Water pressure quite slow at showers
- $7 Fiji water by table side, don't buy it, it cost $1.5 at the convinence store nearby
- not enough table - only one small working desk
- not enough chair - only one chair and no sofas
Overall, this is a great and very comfortable hotel. It's no non-nonsense value with its affordable pricing is very good for business travelers and leisure travelers like me. I will recommend it to everyone. It may not be as grand and huge as Marriott Marquis, or have the beautiful atrium, it is way cheaper and very comfortable. Note that it is not grand or luxurious, it is excellent for value.

“Best located hotel in new york.” Have stayed here at least six times since 2004.you could not ask for a better location in new york city.walk out the hotel and your are in the middle of times square.the hotel itself is very big and very busy which is the way i like it as you are anonymous.rooms are large,clean and when you shut your door it is complete silence.have been lucky and had a view basic priced rooms with a stunning view of times square,but you can pay more to guarantee this.staff are friendly,helpful and efficient.all in all a great place to stay.not cheap but worth every penny.

“Great hotel! Great bed! Will stay again.” Had a fantastic stay at the Omni. Particularly loved the comfortable bed, attentive staff and the outdoor pool area. The location is fantastic, very easy to get in and out. The room had a big, flat-screen TV, which was great - although the signal reception was a little bit poor so having such a good TV with that signal is a bit of a waste. I would definitely recommend the hotel to anyone who can afford to stay there. It's not the cheapest option in the area, but I'll be booking into the Omni again on my next trip.

“Spacious Suite but toilet dirty on arrival” The Eastgate Tower is about a 10 minute walk from the nearest subway and not in one of the main tourist areas. The suite we had was spacious with a huge living room and separate bedroom, kitchen and bathroom. Apart from the Reception area which appears quite new the hotel is very run down, paintwork is chipped and faded and the furniture is old and worn. However the beds are very comfortable, there is plenty of wardrobe space and the shower and hairdrier are good enough. The iron and ironing board are also very welcome. We could clearly hear the street noise (honking horns etc) but this did not disturb us as much as the very noisy fridge. We did not use the cooking facilities but they seemed adequate; we were however surprised to see sugar grains scattered over the bottom of one of the kitchen cupboards. As the hotel is not four star we did not mind the shabbiness and general run down feel of the place but the dirty toilet that greeted us on our arrival just wasn’t acceptable. The back of the toilet was encrusted with old urine stains and pubic hair; evidently we were the only people to put the lid down and see this. We asked for it to be cleaned and it was done when we went out for the evening, however on our return I noticed another hair on the rim of the bath in the corner, I decided to see if it was cleaned away the next day. It wasn’t and was still there when we left. Unfortunately something like this makes you wonder just how clean the rest of the suite is and we were not sorry to leave. Apart from the cleanliness issue the hotel would be OK for the price.

“Stay Downtown Chicago if your at a convention, not worth the savings” Booked a hotel room online for the Ramada. It was a little less expensive then staying downtown. First problem I had was when I called the hotel and asked how to get to the hotel from the ORD airport using public transporation. The lady said it was not possible and to take an airport shuttle or taxi. So off I went and took the blue line from the airport and got off and took the #6 bus, and got off where the driver told me to, and walked 3 blocks. total price was $2.00 for the subway and $.25 transfer to the bus.
next problem was the hotel room. It was really old and dated, kinda like furniture you would see at goodwill. Decor was not the nicest. This was livable, although my girlfriend did not like it and felt "dirty" during our stay. Good thing we were only here one night.
next problem was that on their web site it states, "The Ramada Lake Shore, a great Chicago Hotel located just five minutes from McCormick Place, offers a free shuttle service to and from the hotel to the Chicago Convention Center on a daily basis." which would lead one to believe that they have a constant supply of shuttles running to and from the McCormick Place convention center, a reason why I booked the room. Well we get there and then they say "our shuttle runs downtown" and we can drop you foff at Mccormick. I booked this hotel because of the statement on their web site because I was going to a convention while in town. So they tell me they have two shuttles, one at 9:30 and one at 11:30 to drop me off at Mccormick, but can PROVIDE NO PICKUP from McCormick. and to take a taxi back. for one, my events started at 8:30am at McCormick, and there was no pickup! My girlfriend was not happy about navigating public transportation to get back to the hotel at night.
Another problem was with the internet connection. On the ramada corporate web site, when I booked the room it states, HIGH SPEED INTERNET. When I got to the hotel, there was no internet available in the room, and I asked the front desk, and they said it was only available in the lobby. 
Those were all not that significat problems, and were able to be worked around. The biggest problem was that the hotel door did not latch without pulling up on the door, and then the door looked as it had been broken in, and repaird, by putting a piece of wood infront so someone would not be able to stick a screwdriver or credit card to jimmy open the door. When my girlfriend left the room, she did not realize you had to do this, and when I got back we pushed the door open to get in, without using the key or even turning the door handle. All our stuff in the room was at risk to be stolen or worst have a breakin where we could be personally harmed. In addition, the neighborhood this hotel was in didnt look like the best neighborhood in Chicago.
So I went to the front desk to report the problems with the door lock and to get into a different room, and before me heard the front desk lady tell two other people the hotel is sold out and there are no rooms available. so we just "delt" with it.
In addition, the hotel is under renovation, and the lobby was under construction, and the retaurant on property was closed.
If I were you, I would avoid the Ramada in Chicago. I thought I would save some money by staying here, but it was far away from everything (shopping, etc) and was not worth the savings.

“Ideal Location” If you want Zen type tranquility, this hotel provides it. Traffic is not heavy here. There are no tour groups at this hotel. The lobby is always quiet. The staff is very helpful. Our room of adequate size was very quiet. Never heard any outside noise. You are only a short 10 min. 
walk to Gramd Central Station, so within any destination in Manhatten. 
Times Sq. is also within walking distance and has been cleaned up, we felt safe. Try the Garden Cafe on the ground floor. The chef and service is outstanding. Internet rates for this hotel is reasonable for NYC.

“Great location, clean, comfortable room” We had a one night stay recently during a college tour visit with our daughter. We had booked a room with 2 double beds and were upgraded to to a suite upon our arrival. The suite had a separate sitting area, TV and desk that was separated from the bedroom with french doors. The double beds were very small - not sure if they were actual 'doubles', so it makes for a tight squeeze for two people in the bed. Bathroom was clean with nice amenities. No coffee maker in the room. We asked about parking and were told to go to the lot near the museum. When we left the parking garage they asked us for a 'ticket' from the hotel to get the discount rate of $25 vs. $40. We were not given any such ticket so paid the full $40 rate, which was annoying.

“VERY nice stay!” I was VERY hesitant to stay at this hotel after the reviews, just goes to show how different our experiences can be.
My original reservation was made through Hotels.com at a VERY good rate for an executive room. My plans were then changed and I need to add an additional 4 nites to the orig. 2. The hotel honored my rate from Hotels.com, which they did not have to do and I was able to stay in my FABULOUS room! other friends staying at the same time thought I had the best room in the hotel - it was great. Very roomy, (by NYC standards), the Sleep Number bed was great, bathroom although a bit cramped, was CLEAN! Housekeeping was great, the breakfast on the 17th floor was super, the front desk was especially helpful, friendly and efficient!! and the doormen were fun, too! 
All in all, I had a great stay - thank you Radisson!

“Not impressed and poor attention to cleaning detail” This hotel has nice touches and amenities, but not impressed with the room as a business traveler. The room was very small (yes, even for NY), but nicely laid out with modern decor. The bathroom was separated from the bedroom (hard to really call it two rooms) by a glass wall. There was an electric shade with a mural that could be lowered for privacy. Cool design, but too small for working in the room. The only place to sit was a small table with a tiny aluminum chair or a padded bench (see photo). The room was annoying, but I probably could have put up with it for the week. It was the room upkeep that pushed it over the top. The room had a single rug under the table, which was badly stained. And there was a hair clog in the shower drain - yuck. Not what I expected from a luxury boutique hotel. I checked out after one night.

“Definitely below par” I spent 4 nights at this property during a recent business trip. The hotel is conveniently located in Midtown and the service was generally good. However, this place has some work to do to get back to expectations. 
My room was small, not properly lit, and cramped. There was wallpaper coming off the walls and the plumbing was barely adequate.
I needed to send laundry out and found that you can only do that Monday to Friday. No Saturday service.
The fitness center is medium sized but the equipment is in terrible shape. Half of the treadmills did not work and you only have room for free weights if you are the only one there.
The Sheraton is a just a couple of blocks away and there are so many other options in this neighborhood that I recommend trying another place.

“Large comfortable room, nice stay” This hotel looks like an apartment building, which I think it used to be. We stayed three nights in a studio--bed, bath, and kitchenette. The bed was comfortable, and the room was clean. A lot more space than is usual in a NYC hotel. Front desk service was extremely pleasant. The location, on E34th Street near Lexington Avenue, is outstanding. There is an all-night deli/grocery on the corner and many decent restaurants nearby in all directions.

“Second time - NIGHTMARE!” Had a semi-bad experience about a year ago and the general manager phoned me about that and PROMISED me the next time I booked to contact him and he would get me the best suite available at the time of booking. Therefore, this review will mainly consist of my EXPERIENCE this time around rather than describing the hotel amenities, etc.
I went ahead and booked it (two rooms in fact with the referral I gave my Aussie friends) and contacted the GM IMMEDIATELY after booking the room- Christopher Hosmer. He was out of town and I booked a non refundable rate. I was told a suite would be unavailable but I would receive a junior suite (with french doors), which I was happy with as this would suffice for my purposes. 
Upon check in I was told I had a "junior suite" but when I entered it was indeed the large room, no divider walls after countless calls and a written confirmation (on Marriott's site). I went downstairs and spoke with Richard (the evening manager) who insisted the room assigned was a junior suite (which did not meet the specifications if you look at the room details of the junior suite - two televisions, french doors, etc on their site under room details). My battery was dead on my phone and I was unable to pull up the confirmation and stumbled through luggage to look for the printed version for my two night stay. 
Finally, Richard emerged with a confirmation stating it was a "larger room." I was beside myself and ready to move to another property. As I was calling other properties in desperation and considering leaving the city, Richard finally came back out and stated he would be able to give me the junior suite for the two nights booked under my name and apologized, shook my hand, etc. I happily went to the new room, which was absolutely fine.
We ended up having a great time but after such a delayed flight from LAX and then meeting my friends, we ended up sleeping in until pretty late to get a late start on the only full day that I had there. 
After check out, my friends advised me the Marriott would be charging me an additional night in fees and said I did not fully prepay this, which I did (even have the confirmation and charge made in July) when I was already back in LA. I contacted them and will be disputing this with my credit card company. 
Never again will I trust this hotel and there is no way this can be made up. I was completely lied to and mislead and this is not the way to treat customers - ever! This is my own opinion and I truly dislike writing reviews that reflect a negative opinion, but the truth should be out there and people should not have to deal with crooks who run a hotel. 
I think Tony Soprano would do a better job then the persons managing this property.

“Brand New, Great Value, Would Stay Again” I stayed here from June 8 - 10 and was pleasantly surprised at check in when I learned that the hotel had just opened a few days prior, and I was the first person to stay in the room. I got a rate of about $189/night, which I think is pretty good given the proximity to times square, the fact that you free get high speed internet, bottled water, and a great little room. The staff were all excellent, the room nicely appointed (but standard NYC small), and the gym was wonderful as well. The hotel is in a bit of a "transitional" area, meaning that there are some adult DVD shops a few hundred feet up the street, but you are also right there by the NY Times building, and 8th ave. I will definitely be back to this modern, affordable, boutique style hotel.

“Tripadvisor gets it right” I stayed at this hotel 12/25 to 12/27 and found it to be
a very nice hotel which was quite luxorious for the
price. Staff were excellent in every respect. The room
was very clean and well lit with bedding that can
compete with the best hotels. Very quiet with excellent
sound proofing. Location is perfect for subway travel
to all parts of the city. I had to go to Soho and did so
in less than a half hour including the one block
walk to catch the number one subway to Franklin. Same
ease of travel for going to the upper east side
arround Madison. My only complaint is the $45 for
valet parking, but they got my car for me in twenty
minutes when I had to leave. Very easy to get to from
the Lincoln Tunnel, just go straight up 40th street
 four blocks and you are there.

“This place should be shut down!” Do NOT stay here! Any positive review cannot be legitimate. I made the mistake of using an awful opaque site (Lastminutetravel) and thought I was getting a three star. Sure enough this place lived up to its 1-star class as listed here. So much was wrong I'm not sure where to start. The room was lime green with florescent lighting that made the place feel like a prison waiting room. Repairs had been done to the walls and ceiling, yet they had not repainted. There was NO HEAT! I asked about it and all they could offer was an extra blanket. From other reviews below, their lack of heating is a permanent situation. The cable TV was on the fritz. Only three channels came thru clearly. The bathroom had a nasty odor, probably from the mildew. There was an ironing board, but no iron. I requested one and they gave me a corroded iron that left a copper stain on the tissue I wiped against it. It would have ruined my shirt had I used it. The bed was extremely hard and uncomfortable. The tiny elevator must be powered by a hamster wheel as it takes forever to move from floor to floor. There's a view of the park... BUT the windows are so filthy you can barely tell. Oh, and you'll have to listen to the people in the bathroom next door when they do their bizness cuz the walls aren't sound-absorbing in the slightest.
The Astor on the Park is the place to recommend to your enemies. Seriously, I cannot stress enough that this place is worse than setting up a tent in Central Park. And please be wary of using opaque sites when booking in NYC. (I just checked out of the place two hours ago and I'm so upset by my experience that I had to write this immediately to blow off some steam.)

“Great hotel in Times Square!” We traveled to NYC for a week and stayed at the Marriott. We have traveled multiple times over the years to NYC, and this is our favorite hotel in the city of all the different hotels that we have visited. The location to Times Square is excellent and the overall quality and comfort of the rooms is excellent. The rooms are very large and well appointed. The bathroom is fine. My only negative about the room is the quality of the matress. It is not as comfortable as other hotels in this class. We stayed recently in NYC at the Westin and the matress and quality of sleep was superior, but overall the Marriott is our favorite and we will return! The city has strong reputation for many of the hotels having bed bugs and we are very cautious and concerned about this when booking hotels in NYC. We checked our room for any signs and our room was very clean and we found zero signs of any bed bugs. I highly recommend this hotel for your visit to NYC if you are wanting to stay near Times Square.

“We Will Be Back!” We enjoyed our weekend at the Renaissance. We chose the hotel because it offered a package deal for the King Tut exhibit at the Houston Museum of Fine Arts (the deal included exhibit "fast-track" tickets and one free breakfast). Our room was on the second floor. The noise from the air-conditioning was the only problem (pack ear plugs). The hotel staff was friendly and helpful. And the free breakfast was delicious. We also enjoyed dinner in the lounge Friday night. The hotel's location is hard to beat, so we are already planning a return trip.

“Great Hotel!!” Stayed here for two nights, and was very impressed with our stay. Nice clean rooms, great resturants. The desk help was very helpful, anything we needed was taken care of. Staff was extremely helpful. Would stay again.

“Would not stay again” I booked the room for a weekend with my mother. Usually I stay at the W but this was available for a good rate and had good reviews. First impression, the lobby and common areas are worn and need fresh paint, carpet cleaning, etc. The room was small and dark, no minibar, and the safe did not work.
We enjoyed the location for it's proximity to Grand Central, 5th Ave, and Bryant Park. The Wheeltapper Pub's outdoor area is a nice spot to have a drink or casual meal.
Overall, the hotel is nothing special and I would not stay here again.

“Nice historic hotel, quiet” The Waldorf is a nice hotel with lots of history and it was fun to stay there once. The rooms were clean and quiet, but a little worn around the edges (e.g., cracked sink in the bathroom). The location was convenient to many mid-town attractions. My main complaint is that there wasn't much of a selection of eating places near the hotel for a quick breakfast. The area also has a very "business-like" feel because of big insurance and financial offices nearby so the restaurants seem to cater to the office crowd who grabs breakfast or lunch on the way to work or to eat at a desk.

“Fantastic Location, Average Hotel” My husband and I recently went to Chicago for the weekend to enjoy the holiday festivities, and chose to stay at the Westin Michigan Avenue. The location of the hotel is FANTASTIC - right on North Michigan Avenue, close to shopping, dining, and many activities.
Other trip advisor members note that the decor needs updating, however I would suggest that the hotel spend their renovation dollars on staff coaching.
Shortly after booking our room, I received an email asking if I would like to upgrade to a room with a view of Michigan Avenue for $20/ night - I said yes. When I checked in, I was told there was no upgrade, and given the room I reserved (with a view of the windows of the Hancock Building across the street). This was not a problem until I received my bill which had the additional $20 charge per night applied for both nights (total of $40). When I went to the hotel desk to discuss the matter they gentleman at the desk said, "No big deal, it's only 40 bucks, I'll remove the charge from the bill." Given that this is a premium hotel, I would have expected a more gracious response (including an apology) for this oversight.
My advice to you? If you are to stay here, check your bill. And don't expect an apology from the staff.
Also, the view from the rooms vary greatly - Ask for a good view: There are 2 towers: East and West:
In the East tower - Higher-floor rooms on the North side have a view of Oak Street beach & the lake, all South side have views into the John Hancock building (you see a black window out your window).
In the West tower - Some North side rooms have a view of Oak Street beach, most have a view into the Palmolive building. Some South side rooms have a fantastic view of Michigan Avenue, others have a view of the Hancock building (as the West tower does).

“Don't spend more than $79 a night...” I only had a week to find a place and couldn't get anything downtown for 3 nights in my price range. Despite the other reviews I decided to take the chance-- besides I got a discount for 3 nights, about $79 a night. Upon reflection I probably overpaid.
The hotel website does not advertise that it's only two blocks from the Hyde Park 51st/53rd St metra stop. Which considering how rundown the place is, you'd think they want to mention the convenience. 
When I got there my heart sank, it looks really bad from the outside. I checked in with no problems, but on the way to my room the hallway smelled awful. Like a mixture of standing water, mold, and funky feet. It turns your stomach at first but you get used to it. 
Surprise! My room wasn't that bad. It looked and smelled clean, I thought the carpet would be the same horror out in the hall. There were a few problems: coffee grounds were smeared on the table near the pot and some outlets didn't work. My neighbors looked a little shady, since I was alone I made sure I kept the door locked. But overall I was satisfied with the room.
Biggest problem: unreliable wifi. I brought my laptop hoping to get some work done and on the second night it completely stopped working. 
The restaurant was okay. Stay away from the french fries, they're little grease traps.

“Fire Alarm at 3am in the morning, no coffee anywhere in the hotel and staff which just has no manners” The summary of the yotel in NYC is short - do not expect what you are used to the yotel brand in Europe or from Yo! sushi's Brand Equity.
- Fire alarm went off twice in a night (8.30 pm and 3.am) 
- No coffee available (not at the breakfast and not in one of the 13!?! Coffee Machines I visited on any floor
- Continental Breakfast is Muffin and Tea (as coffee was not available ;-) - nothing else
- Free night has been offered for compensation (you had to ask and there was a dispute about it so we were given a 2nd free night as voucher)
