- Safe was blocked - 'Security Guy' left litter (Coke Can) in our room and has general code for all safes in his Iphone (2124XX)
The rooms are nice, Internet a bit slow for NYC but the washrooms are not functioning - take a shower and your bathroom is flooded.
Very superficial place - you would be good to hire some professional staff who has been working in the industry!

“Einfaches Hotel mit guter Lage, jedoch mit relativ unterdurchschnittlichem Preis-Leistungsverhältnis” Das Hotel ist nur ein paar Blocks vom Central Park entfernt, und liegt direkt gegenüber Carnegie Hall - damit also hervorragende Lage, die das Hotel vor allem bei Touristen sehr beliebt sein lässt. Angesichts des relativ hohen Zimmerpreises enttäuschte jedoch insbesondere die Zimmerausstattung etwas: kein kostenloser Internet-Zugang verfügbar, das Zimmer war eher klein und das Bett relativ unbequem. Aufgrund der offenbar relativ dicken Gebäudemauern war der Mobilfunk-Empfang miserabel.

“Leider nur Durchschnitt” Das Wellington ist von der Lage sicher ideal, direkt am Central Park und ganz nahe der 5th Avenue. Viele Restaurants und Coffee Shops sind in unmittelbarer Nähe, so dass man ohne weiteres morgens frühstücken kann, wenn man nicht im Hotelrestaurant essen möchte! 
Leider lässt das Hotel ansonsten eher zu wünschen übrig. Für den ersten Aufenthalt in New York kann ich es nicht empfehlen, auch wenn es in vielen Reisebüros immer wieder gerne angeboten wird. Man kann leicht einen falschen Eindruck von der Stadt bekommen, obwohl es so viele andere und bessere Hotels gibt, die vom Preis ähnlich sind. 
Am schlimmsten finde ich die Zimmer, die langsam eine Renovierung gebrauchen könnten! Man kann das Wort "schlimm" auf jeden Fall verwenden, denn der Stil ist veraltet, so wie die ganze Inneneinrichtung! Die Betten werden mit wenig Liebe hergerichtet, die Tagesdecken hätte man schon vor Jahren austauschen müssen. Die Bäder sind klein- wie fast immer in New York! Die Sauberkeit ist ok, aber manche Fliesen sind kaputt, was sehr unschön ohne Schuhe ist! Die Handtücher werden aber auch hier jeden Tag gewechselt- immerhin! 
Wenn man das Hotel endlich sanieren würde, könnte es zu einem viel besseren werden, denn die Lage ist unschlagbar! Doch wer einen schönen Urlaub in dieser Stadt verbringen möchte und abends in ein sauberes und gemütliches Hotel kommen will, der sollte lieber eine andere Unterkunft wählen! Drei Sterne bei uns ist in Amerika leider was ganz anderes. 
Ein kleiner Tipp: Direkt gegenüber befindet sich das Park Central Hotel, was etwa die gleiche Preis.- und Sterneklasse besitzt und um längen besser ist! Einfach mal dort vorbei schauen oder im Reisebüro direkt nach diesem Hotel fragen! 
Wäre das Wellington nicht in der Rundreise inkludiert gewesen, wären wir auf jeden Fall in ein anderes Hotel gegangen!
“Disgusting.” Foul smelling... even for a non smoking room. Upon further inspection there was mold growing on the mattresses. There was also hair all over the sheets.

“Good Value” The quandary: visiting NYC during the holidays with no advance planning -- with two adults and two adult children. The solution: YOTEL.
So we needed three beds and wanted to stay near the theatre district and we wanted to spend less than a fortune. For less than $200 per night we stayed in a bright, clean room with unique pull up bunk beds and a comfortable queen. The only real complaint is the silly glass bathroom door that insists on slamming every time - and does not go all the way to the ceiling, making it . . uh . . difficult to . . . uh . . . USE when nature calls and there are others in the room. The pluses are many: the windows actually open, the shower is huge, it is well-insulated for sound and feels very secure. The staff are genuinely friendly and seem concerned that you will like their place.
As for the bland restaurant, main lobby and bar . . . who needs 'em. This is NYC and anything you want is steps away.

“Nothing but Praise!” We booked our 5 night stay for my 50th birthday celebration. I had read ever Trip Advisor review on NYC hotels and thought this would be the best place for our stay with my family. I booked the stay with my Hilton Honors points. We were completely blown away by everything this hotel had to offer.
A week before our arrival I received an email from Estela Lopez asking me to complete a questionnaire so the hotel could make our stay a pleasant one. I thought this was a nice touch and something that you don't see in large hotel chains in one of the busiest cities in the world. I completed my questionnaire with my requests hoping that maybe one or two of them would be met.
We arrived at the hotel early. Since the room wasn't ready we were given a breakfast voucher to enjoy a complimentary breakfast at the hotel restaurant, Pigalle’s. The bellman, Charles, loaded our luggage onto a cart and secured it for us. Charles engaged my 6 year old son in conversation which our son loved! It was at that time that we met Estela in the lobby. She made us feel welcome. The front desk agent who checked us in, Talbert, was very friendly and efficient. He took my cell number and told me he would call when the room was ready. After we enjoyed the breakfast buffet we took to the streets.
About an hour later, me cell phone rang. Our room was ready. In the meantime, we couldn't find a store we were trying to locate on Madison Ave. I asked Talbert if he could please google search the store for their address and phone number. He located it for me, even offered to call the store to get directions for me. I told him that wouldn't be necessary but was excellent customer service.
We were upgraded to a king room with a pull out futon for our son. One of my requests! The room was on the 11th floor with a great view of 8th Ave. Another one of my requests!The room was large and comfortable. Charles brought up our luggage and let our son ride on the luggage cart! 
As we were unpacking, we heard a knock on our door. It was Estela making sure our room was ok. She had a box of cupcakes and a note to acknowledge my birthday! We were blown away. I was also having trouble getting WiFi access in the room. Estela made sure I had a good connection before she left. 
We had breakfast in the hotel restaurant daily with our breakfast vouchers. The buffet offered lots of variety. Since I try to eat healthy, I loved the steel cut oatmeal that was there daily. They also had a good variety of hot and cold items. The restaurant was never crowded and we had a good view of 8th Ave. 
We asked Larry, the concierge, help with subway and bus information. He was very helpful and more importantly, accurate with his directions. He always gave us a city map with his handwritten instructions. He offered to help us with show tickets if we couldn’t find them on our own. 
The hotel location is perfect. It’s a short block to the subway, restaurants and Times Square. The hotel is located 8 blocks south of Columbus Circle. We didn't have a problem with the street noise. 
The housekeeping department kept our room clean and stocked with clean towels. We used the mini fridge when we had leftovers from the restaurants. We used the microwave to warm the cookies that were available at the front desk. Another nice touch. Even though the room had a coffee pot stocked with coffee and tea, we didn’t use it. And we didn’t use the complimentary Starbucks coffee that was located in the lobby every morning. For snacking, we walked to the Food Emporium one block north. The deli section is located at the street level and downstairs is the grocery section.
We purchased a gift for a friend who lives in NYC and realized we didn't have tissue paper for the gift bag we bought. I called the front desk and asked if they had some that I could use. Five minutes later, Laura, had a beautiful supply ready for us. Theses folks are always on the ball!
Whenever we walked through the lobby we were always met with lots of smiles, Hello's, and have a nice day. It really felt like home.
On our last day, as we were checking out, Estela was in the lobby. We told her we had a great time and it was hard to leave. She went behind the front desk and gave our son a plastic red apple filled with chocolate truffles. Another unexpected treat.
We cannot wait to book our next trip to the Hilton Garden Inn at Times Square. I will never stay at another NYC hotel again.
Thanks again to all of the employees at the Hilton Garden Inn for a wonderful stay and for creating life long memories

“I am going to be biased” I stayed here with my partner and his family for Thanksgiving 2011. It is really centrally located which was great for the black friday sales. The room we got was tiny, I know this is what people say about every hotel room in NY but the door touched the bed when it opened. My in-laws room was much more spacious and bright so they aren't all small rooms here. I am picky about hotel rooms but I found it comfortable as you would need, maybe a little on the dark side, we couldn't tell if it was raining, snowing or if the sun was shining as the view out the window was obscurred by air ducts. But on the plus side there was little or no external noise. On one of the days during our stay we got engaged, when we arrived back to greet our in-laws with the news in the lobby the receptionist noticed and sent over a bottle of champagne. Suddenly everything looked much better, go figure ;) Overall no complaints with the hotel. I would stay here again.

“Feel at home” In the midst of the hustle and bustle in New York City, on the south side of Times Square lies the closest thing to "home" you can get! The Courtyard typically seems to have a good rate, is very clean, and makes you feel at "home." That feeling is hard to achieve in a typical Manhattan hotel. There were no disappointments with this hotel besides the fact that they do not offer room service.

“Some staff are great, housekeeping is so/so” There are 4 hotels right together: Marriott, Courtyard, Drury and Spring Hill. Drury was sold out, so I booked here since my meeting was very close. Front desk staff, bellman/shuttle drivers, and especially the employee who runs the p.m. Concierge Lounge are fantastic! Room was comfortable and well designed. My concerns (addressed by manager): Housekeeping. I stayed here for 3 nights. Wasn't done by 3:30 the next day. Also, they didn't restock adequately. Had to ask for toilet paper. They didn't restock all towels needed two large ones and didn't have them) and large bottle of bottled water. Sundries shop didn't have toothpaste! They sent me up one sample packet. I had to ask for 4 more. Food service: Hit and miss. Great huevos rancheros w/salsa verde. Lunch buffet was just "OK." I would go elsewhere if possible, but not lots of choices of you don't have a car. If I had to stay in this area again, I would stay here or at Drury, but it doesn't always represent Marriott well, although they have some great people.

“Good size rooms, good location, bad staff, noisy hotel” I have mixed feelings about this hotel….First of all, it doesn’t reflect the enthusiastic comments on Tripadvisor and I was misled by them…We arrived around 10pm and the lady at reception (I think her name was Noemi) was so miserable-looking, not even a smile or “how are you” or “welcome” it was not a great start at all…Also I believe at one point she said “here are your 2 complimentary water bottles coupons. you can collect them at the small shop as the guy has been waiting for you for hours”….I couldn’t believe she said that, but I didn’t want to get mad as I was recovering from a veeeery long flight so I just took the coupons and went to the small pantry to get my bottles. The guy there was so nice, I asked him if he had been waiting for us or something and he told me he was going to be there all night as the pantry was open 24 hours….Anyway we get to our room on the top 15th floor and it was quite large. But as soon as I entered I could hear a constant noise, like an air/con generator, I think it came from the roof. I told the guy downstairs the next day and he gave me a strange mocking look, like I was mad or something. So I did not want to get mad at this point as it was quite clear the staff was not the “forte” of this hotel (so I really can’t believe all the positive comments about the staff of this hotel on TA reviews, or maybe I was just unlucky…?) Anyway we arrived on a Sunday and all went ok until Wednesday as I started hearing loud house music in the evening…I thought it was the room next to ours so I called reception and asked them to check what was going on as it was ongoing for 1 hour…after half hour no improvement so I called reception again and the guy told me that one of the staff had come upstairs and said all was ok. I told them it was not ok, the music was still going on and loud, so they sent a guy to the room to check it out…The guy came in and told us that it was the open club on the roof….A CLUB ON THE ROOF OF A HOTEL??? I understood it’s not a club owned by Hilton. I couldn’t believe it! And it was only Wednesday so this situation would have been the same for the rest of the weekend!! He then phoned reception from our room and the guy apologized, told me that we would have been granted 1 free night and they would have moved us to another room the following day….It was nice of them but it was hard to sleep that night…The next day another guy from reception seems annoyed by the working load of having to move a guest to another room, he gave me a room key but I told him I wanted to see the room first…fortunately I did as it was a connecting room while my requests specify I do not want them! I go back to reception and he gives me another key to floor 11 which was a good room, similar to our original room and facing the back (we were facing the back on the top floor too, the noise from the fire department is heard but not too often). The house music in the evening could be heard anyway but not as loud as the on the top floor. I believe the issue with the club is only in the summer time. The location of this hotel is a good one, short walk to Town Square and Hell’s Kitchen’s restaurant area, and several metro stops just outside the hotel. We had breakfast in Pigalle one morning but was not worth it..it’s too expensive and food quality is not great (pastries are terrible). Pigalle is a good restaurant for evening meals though: we had dinner there once and liked it, nothing special but reasonable pricing and food. There is free-wifi and it's definately a plus. But overall the experience was not a very good one, I would not go back to this hotel if I went to NYC again, it is too expensive for what you get, I guess you pay for location but there are better options in NYC.

“Great Location and Friendliest Staff” The property has been renovated and is in great shape. The rooms and bathrooms are small but are adequate with flat panel televisions and comfortable beds.
Besides the great location, the other thing that really stood out at this hotel was the staff. I'm not usually one that cares about friendliness but these guys were top notch - everyone from the doorman, to the maids, to the service staff and the front desk. 
I was there for 6 days and never experienced anything but friendly smiles and kind comments. 
We were greeted every time we walked in and out the doors or through the lobby. 
The service personnel were courteous and always put you, your path of travel and needs before theirs. If you came across your maid, she would always ask when it would be convenient to clean your room and if there was anything you needed. 
I just can't say enough about this! 
No, the hotel isn't the InterContinental but the staff performs and treats you as if it is - Better actually since there is nothing pretentious about them.

“Buon hotel, colazione così così” Ho soggiornato in questo hotel per due notti nel mese di agosto durante il mio tour degli Stati Uniti. L'hotel è piuttosto centrale e si raggiunge facilmente tutti i punti principali della città sia a piedi (ma è un po'lunga) sia in pochi minuti di metropolitana. L'hotel è un po'piccolo rispetto agli altri Doubletree nei quali ho soggiornato, ma lo standard delle camere è lo stesso. Infatti sono sempre molto grandi e molto pulite. L'unica cosa che non mi ha soddisfatto molto è la colazione che era un po'scarsa. Per il resto è consigliatissimo!!

“Time warp” Weather delays forced me to spend the night at IAH. The next flight out was EARLY the next morning. I didn't want to deal with getting to the airport at 5:30 so despite the 225 a night rate I grabbed a room. Sure, getting there from the terminals is easy via the underground trains. Nice to know where the movie Future World was filmed... on these underground trains. Not sure but I think I saw Blythe Danner and Peter Fonda down there. The rest of the hotel hasn't changed much from the 70's. VERY dated and showing dirt and stains. The room was basic and less than clean. Room service was painfully slow. Check in and out was S L O W and there were no other guests around. Ancient systems from what I could tell. Staff is very stiff and uninviting. As you can tell, I didn't like this place and hope to never have to stay there again.

“Disappointing experience” Stayed at the Barclay Intercontinental NY November 4-6 for 2 nights. Definitely not a 5 star hotel - cannot compare with any Ritz Carlton or Four Seasons that I have ever stayed at. Here's why:
Got a cab from Penn Station to the hotel (cost - $10/rush hour traffic on a Friday), was met at the door with an offer to assist with luggage. Fine check-in, nothing special. Lobby is impressive, nice, beautiful fresh flowers, bustling with activity. We were in Room 1017 - standard room with a king sized bed. Room is small and outdated. Needs repairs on a multitude of levels. The chair at the desk was coming apart at the seams and the carpet was spotted in a number of areas. There is also a sitting chair by the window that looks outdated as well. Entertainment center has a TV and a coffee maker. There is also an honor bar as well. No bathrobes. Bathroom is tiny, barely room for one person and nothing special. It was very clean and had nice amentities. Housekeeping was good and discreet.
My issues began on the first night I got almost no sleep. The bed is comfortable and the lines were OK (although nothing near the Frette linens that the Ritz Carlton uses in their beds.) However, there was something seriously amiss with the heating/ac units in the room - it went from hot to cold to hot to cold in a matter of minutes. Impossible to regulate and highly uncomfortable. And very loud as well. Coupled with that extreme annoyance was the fact that there was a dump truck on the street and for at least a half hour around 1:30 a.m. All I could hear was the truck dumping something, backing up, doing the same thing over and over. 
The second night we were there, my husband and I went to the bar in the Barclay for a late night drinks with friends of ours who were also staying at the hotel as well. Our friend ordered our drinks for us -3 Baileys on the rocks and an Irish Coffee. The bartender misunderstood and began pouring the Bailey drinks as triples. Now, that is fine, anyone can make a mistake, but as soon as we realized what had happened and brought it to his attention immediately, he was completely indifferent and did not even offer an apology. He then proceeded to charge us for the "triple" Baileys - and the bar bill for one round of 4 drinks was over $60. Ridiculous. I thought the least he could do since it was his error would be to just charge us the normal charge of what a single Baileys would cost. Needless to say, there was no more drinking at the bar at the Barclay. They lost our business.
And I had the same issues with the HVAC system the second night as well, thus resulting in little to no sleep for the second night in a row. This just should not happen in a hotel that supposedly rates at 5-stars in terms of quality. 
Location is good - close to 5th Avenue, walking distance to Times Square, just a few blocks away from Grand Central Station. Took a short cab ride to Greenwich Village ($8) and ate at a really cute Mexican place called La Palapa - excellent margaritas and Mexican food, and great guacamole as well. Went to breakfast one morning at the Comfort Diner on 45th Street - excellent breakfast food, enormous portions. Highly recommend. Did not eat the breakfast at the hotel, it seemed overpriced at $20-some dollars a head.
Check-out was uneventful. There were offering complimentary bottled water and power bars in the lobby in the honor of the NYC marathon that was happening this weekend. Nonetheless, I just don't consider this place to be of 5-star caliber. I just didn't get the personalized service and attention to detail that would be typically prevalent that kind of a hotel. For $450/night, I expect much, much more. I'll give this one a miss next time we are in New York.

“I felt like I'd had a spa treatment, just by staying in the room” Wow, a real hidden gem. As a bloke, it felt a little strange staying above a beauty treatment establishment, but wow what a good decision. The best accommodation I've stayed in for a long while. Relaxation is the key, beautiful un-cluttered room, excellent bed and bedding and a steam shower that was perfect.
This is a B&B so you have to arrange a time to pick up keys etc, but you are not staying in someone's house where you may feel uncomfortable, this is much more like an apartment experience.
I cannot recommend this place highly enough, right in the heart of the wicker park area, close to the metro, surrounded by some of the most cool places to go with plenty of car parking on the side streets.
Don't waste your pennies on an expensive hotel in Chicago, stay here!
Note: Not that I wanted to watch TV, just to note there is no TV in the room. WiFi and a CD player was more than enough for me.

“おすすめできません” 仕事で自動的に予約されていたホテルです。
外観はそれなりに良く、グランドセントラルに接している点は評価できます。
しかし、ホテルのサービスは悪く部屋は狭く汚く騒々しい。
本当にハイアットブランドなのか？と驚かされる程がっかりなホテルでした。
バックパッカー用だと考えるといいのですが、金額はそれなりにするので改善の必要は結構あるように思いました。

“Good Stay” Stayed here for a one night stay. Hotel open for about a month in a converted office building. Desk service good and friendly. Did not seem to have many guests. Stayed on a top floor. Hallways very narrow, but room was a good size. Room decorated in Ikea-style furniture and comfortable. Although the hotel was open a month, the furniture was already showing signs of wear. Generally, a good stay in a very convenient location near the stock exchange and WTC site.

“You can do better in Chicago” The web site describes a luxury hotel but it may have been that when it opened but not today. The lobby is dismal and the check in desk area is tight, not staffed well and in need of a major redo. The rooms are ok but as with the standards today are not up to a great room/ bath experience. The elevators have the most unusual way to get to your floor. You need TO get used to the lack of buttons in the cars. This is not a flagship property for this brand.

“Won't stay again” October 15th we spent the night at the Tremont in Chicago. Never again! Staff was nice enough, but the room was dirty and falling apart. Paint chipping, soiled chairs, mold in the shower, not enough towels, etc. Web site is very misleading, absolutely nothing like what you get. It shouldn't be rated as a 3 star, it barely makes it as a 1 star. Traveler be ware.

“Horible hotel not worth the price” So i got this hotel through an opaque provider, at about 40% off.
The hotel is in a nice location, close to Grand Central, Rockefeller, Times square, and transit.
The lobby is nice.
It's close to transit.
Now for what is bad...
They claimed they had no non-smoking kings, even though they were still selling them on their website, as they were not sold out! They offered us a smoking king, or a room with 2 doubles. We chose the doubles.
But this is not why I am upset at this hotel.
While they put some money into the upkeep of the common areas, lobby/hallways, the room is atrocious! The headboards were disgusting, the carpet stained and dirty looking. The tub was deplorable. There were stains on both the outside and inside, and I wonder if it has had a good clean in a long time. The wallpaper was peeling from the walls.
The water in the sink comes out with such force, and is difficult to regulate, so you end up splashing yourself, as well as the entire bathroom.
The room "felt"dirty. It was just not up to Doubletree, or even Comfort Inn standards! They claim to be doing a renovation in 2012. Until that time, I would advise staying FAR away from this hotel!

“Spoiled after staying at the Ritz at Central Park” Just got back from three nights at the Ritz. They had a special for this time of the year otherwise it's beyond my normal price range. However, now I'm so spoiled it will be hard to stay anywhere else. I usually stay at the Marriotts or the W, which are very nice and work fine, but the Ritz's service and luxury really makes you feel special. I also stayed there because I was able to bring my two dogs. It's really hard to find dog-walking areas in NYC that have grass (my dogs are used to the country) and Central Park is THEE place to walk your dogs. We were able to get a double upgrade to a high floor Central Park view which was amazing. The staff is really attentive and all call you by name. I have to extend particular kudos to Jenny who booked our room, Ender who is a guest relations person, and the bell hop/doormen staff. They were very attentive and helpful and had to open the doors for the dogs many times! The room itself is so beautiful and comfortable. I do wish the Ritz's had ice machines though....while they would be most friendly about delivering it, I find I don't want to call each time I need ice, but I believe this is standard for Ritz's and I understand why. I also LOVED this area of NYC.....it's not commercialized like Times Square and you feel a part of the "neighborhood", yet it's still so convenient to getting places. If you ever have the chance to stay at the Ritz, do so. I was wondering if I should spend the extra money, and while I won't do so every time I visit NYC, it's really something you need to experience. It would be my choice for a special event for sure! And like I said, I feel spoiled now....everything else will likely pale in comparison.

“Near Medical Center” An okay hotel, room clean, bed comfy,pricing was high. Lobby decor was strange, parking fencing indicated we were in a high crime area, Has a good subtle service to nearby for 5 miles,
The name Crown Plaza implies more up scale quarters. Breakfast was so s.

“Great location and service” This was my second trip to NYC and loved staying in Times Square. It is close to everything we needed and to transportation. We had a small slice of a view of the Hudson. We requested a room with a view of the water and that is what we got, so I would suggest being specific about what you want. The bed was comfy and room was clean. Although the hotel is nice enough, keep in mind this is not a super lush hotel, just clean, comfy and convenient.

“Sehr empfehlenswert!” Das Hotel liegt sehr zentral im nördlichen Büroviertel von Manhattan. Vor der Haustür befindet sich die U-Bahn, mit der man in den Süden (Südspitze Manhattan) und Norden (Richtung Central Park/Harlem) fahren kann. Die 5th Ave ist in 10 Min. zu Fuß erreichbar. Das Hotelpersonal ist sehr freundlich und zuvorkommend, das Frühstück war sehr gut. Es gab heiße WAffeln, Omelett, frische Früchte. Joghurt, Kuchen - alles, was das Herz begehrt. DAs Zimmer war sehr sauber und gut ausgestattet, die dicken amerik. Matratzen ausgezeichnet. Zum Essen ein Tipp: In unmittelbarer Nähe befindet sich das tgl. bis in den späten Abend geöffnete Selbstbedienungsrestaurant "Azure" (3rd Ave. Ecke 51.) mit einer riesigen Salatbar und einer langen Theke mit unterschiedlichen warmen Gerichten. Da kann man prima zwischendurch essen!

“Nice, Clean, Quiet Homewood Suites - I loved it!” Stayed at this hotel for one night with three other colleagues. The rooms were large and very clean. jThe bed was extremely comfortable. My room was on the first floor adjoining the fitness room. I was afraid that it would be noisy; however, no sound came through the wallsl This hotel is set back from the highway and it is a quiet, peaceful oasis in the middle of the hustle and bustle of Houston! Dinner was good - meatball sandwiches. My friends had stayed the night before and said the pepper steak was really good too. Breakfast was also good with french toast, eggs and sausage. The front desk staff and the lady that prepared and served the food was also very nice. I did not meet one unprofessional person in this hotel. Do not hesitate to stay here if you are in the area!

“Good Location” Stayed 4 nights in October. Great location for the Metro and main attractions.
The room was a good size, with a large comfortable bed, nicely decorated and clean. The bathroom again very clean with adequate towels and shower accessories. A room safe a good touch.
The lobby always appeared busy with coach loads of visitors appeared to be arriving daily. Didn't use the restaurant,but had breakfast next door for less than half the price. A bit surprised when they charged $7 to store the cases for a few hours after checkout.
Overall a good hotel and would stay again. Watch out for the mini bar ( very pricey) and top tray. Items picked up will be charged to your room if not replaced within 20 seconds.

“Hotel da consigliare per spazio, pulizia e ..vista!” C'è molto da dire su questo Hotel , apparentemente anonimo dall'esterno ma molto confortevole all'interno.
SPAZI delle camere ampi, di molto superiori agli standard tipici di New York che prevede in genere camere molto piccole. Doppio letto matrimoniale (un lusso a New York) , Tv Lcd Frigo. Bagno sufficientemente spazioso e funzionale ricco di asciugamani. 
PULIZIA buona anche qui con standard molto più alti rispetto alla media Newyorkese. Molte camere hanno poi la possibilità di godere di una vista fantastica sulla Skyline della città con effetto molto suggestivo sopratutto la sera.
Hotel con sala fitness, piscina coperta all'ultimo piano, sala colazione e ristorante interno anche con servizio in camera. NO WI-FI Gratuito
Parti comuni pulite, personale cortese , tranne qualche piccola eccezione di poco conto..
POSIZIONE: a due passi dalla zona dei teatri, a 15 minuti da Times Square a 20 da Rockefeller Center. Questo è sicuramentre un vantaggio perchè si è in zona centrale ricca di ristoranti e locali ma già sufficientemente lontani dalle zone davvero molto incasinate e trafficate 24 h su 24. Riposo quindi possibile senza sentire continuo traffico per tutta la notte!!! 
Zona Tranquilla e sempre frequentata anche alla sera tardi , mai avuto problemi o sensazioni strane...
Metropolitana a due isolati di distanza + altra linea facendo un isolato in più...
Buon rapporto qualità prezzo...che dire ancora....CONSIGLIATO!

“Fine” Amazing lobby, very grand. Rooms were ok, very small and small beds. Also didn't get much sleep with a loud group next door partying and trying to get through the conjoining door. The manager did go up and sort them out though. Was ok, perhaps i would stay somewhere else next time though.

“Nothing less than pure SATISFACTION” I have stayed at this hotel more than three times & the stay is always pleasant. 
PROS:
The hotel security is always circling the parking lot.
The hotel is very quiet. VERY QUIET.
If you are a starwoods member, you more than likely will be upgraded for free.
You will receive a complimentary bottle water upon arrival.
The hotel offers movies (for a fee) that are currently still in the theaters.
The hotel provide free breakfast and it it pretty good, with a wide variety of options.
The hotel provide "HAPPY HOUR" 
The location could not be better, you are across the parking lot from the movie grill, Walmart is 24 hours and less than 5 mins away, Pappadeaux is in walking distance and Memorial City Mall is less than a mile away. 
You can easily find a room for less than $100.00 a night here. 
Free Wifi
CONS:
I don't have any.

“Probably the worst hotel i've ever used...” Shoddy decor, unloved uncared for - left to decay, After 24 hours of tepid water in my room, I went to reception to see if there was a problem, yes you have to run the taps for 20 minutes for hot water.. room service ? didn't you look at the black book in your room, explained no black book - one was requested but never arrived.
Breakfast staff tried but couldn't keep up, thing's missing and people walking out after bored of waiting.
Sad as place could be great, but tired and worn.
Expensive for what you get, and as my card had already been charged fully upon booking online, I couldn't see me getting a refund and had to stick it out.
I didn't eat here as everything else was too depressing to even try.

“Very Good Hotel” Very good hotel. Staff very friendly. Rooms exceptionally clean. Beds great. Bathrooms "spic and span".A few blocks off Broadway. Close to many of the shows. Would stay again next time in NY.Check in and check out quick, friendly and 100% accurate. Stay on top floors and very good breakfast and cocktail hour included in room rate.

“Nice on outside and below average rooms.” Nice staff and grounds, lobby, and restaurant are in excellent condition.
Rooms are ok, but seem old and lack quality for the rate we paid

“A midtown gem!” I had stayed here a few years ago, but it never got to stay here again until this past week. Booked a king room on booking.com, and was very pleased with the overall setup.
The hotel is in a great midtown location. On 55th St between 6th and 7th, it is steps away from the mid block arcades, now dubbed by the City of NY as 6.5 Av. To get people to use these mid-block passages, they have installed stop signs on the streets between 51st and 57th. I'm sure drivers don't like the stop signs, but it assures life and limb for those using the passages.
The hotel lobby is small, but efficient. Our King Room was quiet and spacious for a midtown hotel, with a great bed, blackout shades, a comfy reading chair. It faced 55th Street, with the City Center right across the street. Nicely decorated ( I'd say freshly, but I don't know). Marble bathroom, with tub and great shower. Sufficient dresser and closet space, and free wireless internet 
The bellmen, and concierge were helpful throughout, and the housekeeping staff was not intrusive, and respected the 'do not disturb' sign. 
The free continental breakfast was provided by the restaurant downstairs, and ran until 1030am .It included coffee, tea, three types of cereal, lots of ripe cut fresh fruit (pineapple, watermelon, strawberries and melon), english muffins, bagels and toast, orange and grapefruit juice. This breakfast would easily go for $20 per head elsewhere. The only negative is that the restaurant staff started cleaning up and taking food away around 1020am and I hated rushing my vacation breakfast. I do understand that they had to get ready for the lunchtime business they have. 
The hotel was busy, fully of traveling families from around the US and Europe. All in all, a MUCH better experience than many larger, chain hotels in midtown.

“Walk in closet” The smallest room I ever stayed in. So small the tv was placed on a stripper pole at the base of the bed. The bathroom was a joke. I had to step outside the bathroom to put my close on. My car had more room in the garage than we did and was much cheaper than our room.

“Great location, but beware...” Recently stayed for one night @ Hotel Mela as I was in town attending a conference. Check-in was prompt and efficient. The room was clean and small, but not unexpected. The bed and linens were perfectly fine and comfortable.
My only beef is two-fold. One, the wireless internet is complimentary which is nice, but the reception in my room was very poor to the point that it was unreliable and ultimately useless. I couldn't do any work at my desk and had to go down to the lobby to get my emails. Two, my room faced 44th Street and there was construction work going on across the street at 5/5:30 in the morning - that's not the hotel's fault, but beware, it's noisy and then some.

“Loved the Alex! Great Staff, Great Rooms, Great Price too!” I recently stayed at the Alex for a weekend, and I have to say I was pleasntly surprised by how enjoyable my stay was. The building is really nice, the lobby is small but well decorated. My check-in experience was fine. They did not give me the room I wanted and instead gave me one with two beds, but that wasn't such a big deal. The entire staff during my stay was extremely friendly and helpful, a big difference from many hotels in NYC. They didn't have an attitude, and were very willing to help or just be social.
The room was very nice, quite large for NYC standards and very fresh. The beds were comfortable, 4 pillows on each. There was a large LCD TV and entertainment center in the bedroom and an additional small LCD in the bathroom. There was two closets, a dressing area, minibar, and several nighstands. The only problem was that there really isn't a desk. There is a large piece of furniute which has three sections, one being a closet, the second being the TV cabinet, and the third is a desk, however it is so incredibly tiny that it's less deep than an airplane tray table. Other than that, the room's great. Also, the hotel is very quiet, I was on a really low floor and didn't hear any outside noise.
I didn't eat in the restaurant, it looked like it would be pretty nice, although I was there Friday and Saturday night and I never saw it busy when passing by.
Another great ammenity is the business center. They took a guest room and converted it to the center, however I still loved it. It has hardwood floors and three large contemporary desks. Each desk has a Dell computer and HP laser printer. There is no charge for using the center (or the high-speed internet in the rooms) which is really really nice. It's all self-service, so you don't have to sign in, pay anything, you just sit down, use the computer, and print stuff if you like. There was a sign indicating that copy and fax services were available at the front desk.
There is a fitness center in the hotel, however I did not use or see it.
Overall, I was really impressed with the Alex, I would defiantely go back, especially if they're offering competitive rates (which they definately were when I was there).

“Hard to believe hotel has not been condemned!” My husband and I needed to visit the city on a day hotel availability was scarce. We usually stay at 2.5 -3 star hotels in Manhattan (for example, Holiday Inn and Hilton Garden Inn). Unfortunately, all hotels ranging from $175 - $350 per night were booked that evening. Thus, we had a choice between $130 for a one star and $350 for a 3.5 star hotel. The HOTEL CARTER was SO DISGUSTING we left at 8 pm without checking out. Though we're courteous people, we didn't bother to do so as we had prepaid anyway and the lobby was littered with the following: a jumpy looking street hood making a rather obvious drug transaction and a sadly young prostitute whose infectious state was apparent from the lesions on her face. We ended up at the Marriott (the lowest price available room spur of the moment) charging on our credit card $380 for a box-like room. We're not at all wealthy -- are middle class parents of college aged kids living frugally. However we ended up spending over $500 that night for the two hotels because the small mountain of bugs in our room noshing on on some previous client's piece of banana in the garbage (yes -- they were red-tinted and appeared to be blood suckers) , the brown-stained bed cover (it looked as if someone with uncontrolled diarhhea had sat on the bed previously) and the rat racing in the hall outside had us thinking we'd need medical attention the next day if we stayed. Please don't stay here.........perhaps if they lack clientele they'll shut down. Honestly, even if my husband and I were homeless, we'd prefer to sleep in Port Authority than here.

“素晴らしいホテルでしたが・・・” 立地、部屋とも素晴らしかったです。部屋から公立図書館はもちろんエンパイアも見えました。バスタブは大きく、シャワーブースも広くて使いやすかったです。インテリアも洒落ています。ニューヨークで最高級のプラザやフォーシーズンズなどと比べても、快適に過ごせると思います。ただし、請求書に誤りがあり、不快な思いもしました。海外のホテルでは、たまにあるので、チェック・アウトの際はもちろん、クレジット・カードの請求書もよく確認しておいた方が良さそうです。

“A tremendous value” As previosuly said, The Salisbury is perfectly located and very clean. Yes, the furnishings are dated and yes, the elevators are slow. But the room was HUGE (1712 and 1718 on the 17th floor) by New York standards. And the $209 rate was excellent. The $3 breakfast is just what one should expect for $3. It's an easy, quick way to grab something before heading out to see the sights. Our location was quiet and the beds were comfortable. My request for more hangers was never filled, but it didn't matter after we found another closet!

“Wow, the room was small!” The hotel was in a good location; however, I am happy that I was traveling alone due to the rooms size. I always stay at 4 star hotels,(Morgans, Muse, Sofitel, etc), but this has been one of the smallest rooms I have ever seen. In addition, it was plain. Upon looking out the window, I was greated with a brick wall. Not worth the price. 
The breakfast in the hotel was fantastic ($15 pancakes)!

“Great Location, Good view, friendly Staff” I've been to New York City many times for business and vacations but never stayed in this hotel. I was headed to Canada on business when American cancelled my flight at the last minute. Frustrating after checking my bags in and then getting the flight cancelled. While waiting for my bags I was looking for a hotel to book at the last minute. 
Many hotels were full. I saw that this one was available so I booked it. There weren't too many options and I was frustrated as I didn't expect to have to book a hotel in NYC. I just flew in for a quick meeting and then was expecting to head to Montreal on another meeting that day.
I called the hotel and they confirmed a suite on a high floor. $400 + tax rate. I took a taxi and promptly checked into the hotel. Everything was quick and easy at the front desk and they had my room waiting. 
A bit strange since you have to take an elevator from the ground floor up to the lobby and then another set of elevators up to the rooms. I had a room on the 35th floor and it was very spacious with an amazing view. Surprisingly it was quiet even though it faced Times Square. 
The location couldn't be better as you are in the heart of the action. There is an entrance from Time Square or another entrance on 44th Street as well. There is a bar off the front desk area. Nothing super high end but it was busy when I checked in.
The room was comfortable with a King bed that was comfortable, a good sized bathroom, LCD TV and a couch and desk. View was great. 
Friendly staff with no problems at all. Pay to play internet that was $21 per day and it worked glitch free. There are higher end hotels in NYC but this location is great if you want to be in the heart of the action.

“Rooms Need Renovation” We spent a week here due to its proximity to the UN, and while the staff was for the most part friendly (except an especially unhelpful and rude concierge member), our room could use a good renovation. The bathroom in particular suffered from dirty and stained grout, scuffed/marked/peeling wallpaper, fingerprinted mirrors, a broken showerhead and broken bulb on the magnifying mirror. The room had a small but nice walk-in closet and two very comfortable double beds, but we had to call about broken hinges on the desk cabinet and the guest chair had filthy looking/stained pillows You get the impression that no one does any quality control or inspections on the rooms whatsoever unless and until a guest calls to report it. This is not how hotels should be operated. 
On our first night we asked the concierge for a restaurant recommendation and he handed me a preprinted restaurant list and simply said that those were the recommended places. The list was basically just names and addresses organized by type of cuisine, so when I asked whether he had menus for any of the Italian restaurants, he huffed and reluctantly grabbed me the menu for the place directly across the street. When I discovered that it was more of a steak place and asked for another Italian menu, he said flat out "You should just go google them yourself." Imagine that!!! The concierge telling me to go figure it out myself! What a joke. Just around that point another guest arrived at the desk, overheard our conversation and was as shocked as I was! This concierge was simply unwilling to provide any help whatsoever, and on top of that he was incredibly rude. We avoided him until the last day when I kindly asked if he wouldn't mind checking online to confirm that my flight was departing on time. He snapped "We only help with restaurants, taxis and theater tickets, not airline reservations." When I pointed out that I didn't need him to book a flight or print a boarding pass, I simply needed his assistance to verify departure time, he then flat-out lied and said he didn't have internet access and told me to go do it myself in the business center. Yes, there's a big sign on the concierge desk stating that they are not hotel employees but rather oursourced from another company. Perhaps the hotel should rethink this service as this one guy was a terrible representation of the hotel. Presumably other members would have been friendly and helpful but who knows. Based on this kind of service and the lack of standard maintenance of the room, we will likely not stay here again.

“Close to office. Good for one person” Stayed here for one night for a biz trip. King sized bed, tiny bathroom(typical NYC fair). 
Lobby was teensy and jam packed with people. 
Room was nice and perfect size wise for one person (not sure about 2.) Raucous guests woke me up several times in the middle of the night and early am so i left tired and cranky but would return again and hope that i would have a peaceful night of sleep. 
Internet worked well and they gave you two choice of pillows (soft and hard) which i liked a lot.

“A disgrace to the Morgan's brand” I don't know why they haven't sold this place and let it become a youth hostel. The Hudson gives "boutique hotels" a bad name and what's odd to me is that they've done nothing to improve it in the decade or so it's been open. I'm sorry but in a city like New York, you need to get a good night's sleep and it seems that they've done everything possible to ensure that won't happen! 
Must be a cash cow . I stayed here once several years ago and again this year, but never again. I was reminded that in this economy, with occupancy rates at all time low, I can do much, much better in NYC for a comparable price.

“Good location and that's about the only good quality it has.” This motel needs serious renovation. The room was run-down with mildewed conditions. This motel was far below normal Days Inn standards. I've stayed at Days Inns before and usually satisified with the price and quality, but not this one. If the room rate was only $25, then it might be worth it. If Days Inn cares about their brand, then they should pull this motel from their franchise. At least the A/C was working well.

“Too busy for renos Westin?” If you are visiting Houston to shop the Galleria - you can't beat this Westin for location and convenience. 
Be forewarned: This is a popular hotel and nearly always fully booked and as a result, it appears that Westin hasn't been able to implement a regular refurbishment program.
The rooms are tastefully decorated - but are beginning to show considerable signs of wear. The Heavenly bed was just 'OK' - not very plush / no featherbed and limp pillows. The dual head shower head leaked terribly. Weak water pressure and the toilet did not flush completely (took two - three flushes). 
Thankfully, Westin has not sealed the patio doors leading to the small balconies. I hope that when the time finally comes to renovate the guest rooms that they don't seal the doors shut like other hotels have done.

“Don't bother - much nicer accommodations up the street” I thought I would try the Mondrian as I am a fan of Morgans. Big disappointment. Location is not good and staff is below average, with attitude. Room was not functional and wear and tear is visible. Restaurant food was awful. The only nice thing is the decor. Service was painfully slow.
If you're looking for a Soho hotel, go to Crosby up the Street. They get it. Or go to the Mercer - an old timer that beats Mondrian hands down.
Morgans is off my list. There is so much better out there these days.

“気分が悪くなるホテル” 翌日、ペンステーションからアムトラックに乗る予定だったので、こちらのホテルに宿泊しました。
早朝、NYに着いたため、ホテルで荷物を預かってもらおうと、ベルボーイにその旨を伝えると、「フロントへ行け」と顎であしらわれ、フロントでは無愛想な白人のおじさんが「1個4ドル」と一言。荷物が二つあったので、8ドルも払うことに。。。宿泊予定なのに荷物を預けるのに金銭が発生するっていうことにまず驚き、さらに地下のバゲージルームへ自分で持って行けと言われました。確かに、客室数が多く、いちいち荷物を預かっていたら大変かもしれませんが、それにしてもスタッフの態度がひどすぎます。立地のよさに胡坐をかいて、サービス業という意識がないように感じました。ただ、あらためてチェックインの手続きをした際は、非常に丁寧なスタッフが対応してくれ、なぜか朝食やコーヒー、インターネットなどが利用できるビジネスルームのキーまでくれました。人に寄るんでしょうが、私が見たところ、その彼以外はみんな感じが悪かったです。
日によって安い時もあるんでしょうが、私が泊まった日は1部屋230ドル。その価値はまったくありませんでした。部屋のバスルームの床もところどころ剥がれているし、次回またNYに行くことがあっても泊まろうなんて絶対に思いません。

“Loved it! best hotel downtown” This is a brand new hotel all suites and its amazing. The lobby is spacious and luxurious. The room are big and very modern and equipped with espresso machines . The views and location are great. My husband and I went for a romantic weekend spent Friday night at the Mandarin and they were booked for Saturday so we moved to The Conrad and I am so glad we did it was incredible. The staff was so friendly and welcoming. Well done we would definitely book again. The have a roof top bar which was not open yet but I can't wait to check it out when it does open.

“Consistent, best option for downtown location” For a downtown location the Marriott has a good combination of convenience and value. Although not cheap it has all the usual standards of a Marriott. The only negative is the absolutely ridiculous oversell of the concierge lounge -- hard to find a seat in the morning and seemed to always be running out of some food items.

“Newly renovated rooms but thin walls!” I have mixed feelings about this Sheraton. On a good note the rooms look brand new, pretty spacious for NYC standards and the common areas of the hotel are well kept. On a not so great note, the hotel is noisy and it has nothing to do with being near Times Square. I was there on a 3 day business trip and the first room they gave me was 2717. I'm a light sleeper so when I got to the room I immediately noticed a " drumming" noise ( perhaps from pipes in the walls) that would happen every 30 seconds. I later found out that my coworker in room 2520 heard the same noise from the walls in her room too, and it never went away. So they put me in room 739 after I complained. No pipe noise but I could hear the neighbors next to me as if they were in my room. The first two nights I got woken up by their screaming baby! Needless to say I'll be looking for another option next time I'm in NYC on business!

“Horrible Experience!” This is the most unenjoyable hotel experience I have ever had. After arriving at this hotel in late May and discovering it was "on the wrong side of the tracks", we were a bit skeptical. I felt very out of place here, as the hotel is not nice and is in a very trashy part of town. However, we decided to give it a chance, since we had gotten a decent rate and it was pretty close to the Sears Tower. My husband left for his business meeting there and I decided to wait for our room to be ready, since we had arrived early. Before he left for the meeting, we were told we could indeed check in early and would only have to wait 30 minutes or so in the lobby. So, I told him to go on to his meeting and I would wait. How nice, I though, an early check in....7 hours later, I was STILL IN THE LOBBY! Their computer system crashed, then there was a "problem" with our reservation. The list of excuses went on and on. So, I had the pleasure of watching noisy school groups come and go and found out that our "suite" hotel plays host to traffic school! Dozens upon dozens of angry drivers filled the lobby waiting for their classes to start and stood around swapping horror stories in amongst guests trying to check in. I was not impressed! Finally, my husband and his associates finished their meeting, but we never made it to our room! We checked out without ever checking in and made our way to a nicer part of town! Don't let the pictures fool you. It isn't that close to the Sears Tower. My husband said they walked quite a while, through the bad neighborhood we were in to get there. The W Chicago City Center is a lot closer to the tower and a lot nicer. One last tip, stay on the opposite side of the canal/river that is behind the hotel. This seems to be the dividing line between the trashy/classy sides of Chicago. Enjoy your stay in the Windy City-Just enjoy it somewhere else!

“Too Aggrevating for a Business Traveler” I realize that the property cannot do anything about the number of people it services at any given moment. However, my personal experience is that a number of the key staff is either incompetent in their positions or simply customer service ignorant. The Sunday morning VIP checkout was flat out rude. So was the executive lounge staff on Saturday. They treat their executive lounge like if it were McDonalds, having you stand in a line in the hallway near guestrooms to get it...and for no apparent reason. Madeline in the executive lounge screamed at me "Can't You Read the Sign" when I approached the lounge when there was absolutely no one at the door to assist. If this how you treat people that actually make up your core HHonors customer base and specifically pay for a higher room category? I woudl suggest more customer service training at this property. It's dreadfully lacking.

“Beautiful Property, Great Service, and Great Location” I had stayed at this hotel a while back before the renovations and I can honestly say that it is a practically different hotel: the rooms and furnishings are top notch and the service is great! 
We had a beautiful room with a view of 58th st (?) with a king bed and a gorgeous marble bathroom with separate tub and shower. 
The iPad controls for the room were a novelty, but the app to use the butler services did not work for our entire stay. 
Room service breakfast was expensive but delicious. We did not eat at any. The other food outlets (try Sarabeth's on the same block facing central park). 
Would defiantly return again.

“Absolutely your choice!!” This hotel has it all. Quick and easy check in/out, spacious rooms, and plush surroundings. Our days were filled with intense conference meetings during our four day stay, but the staff took great care to make our stay comfortable and stress free. The quiet atmosphere was relaxing. The beds amazing. We had everything we needed to focus on our busy days. The free shuttle was prompt and their drivers very informative. Loved being close to many options for dining and entertainment. I will stay here again. The best of the best Hilton has to offer can be found here.

“Doubletree Reservation Website - Trap Alert!” I review this hotel last year. It's a good hotel but they must be very desperate to keep their clients. Read on: I booked a room at the Doubletree Metropolitan at a rate of US$151.20 a month ago. At that point, that was the best rate available on their website. I prepaid a non-refundable rate. (Don't ask!)
 Last weekend Travelzoo announced a US$119 rate for the same room and period. I called Doubletree reservations, they referred me to the hotel's manager (talked to Tracie Cass, the rude manager) and they refused to honor the new rate saying that I chose a nonrefundable rate. I strongly disagree with this policy! Hilton advertise Best Rate Guarantee! Don't fall for that , if you book and prepay for a Hilton hotel, you are stuck and there is nothing you can do!
Doubletree never again!

“Great for business” This is a big 'business' hotel and it's always very busy. The staff do a great job. The rooms are big, well stocked but the decor is a bit tired (lots of mahogany furniture). The bathroom is decent and the air con works. I was on the 42nd floor, amazing view. Comfy bed and good size telly. 
If you are on business, this'll do. The room service is a bit steep on service charges and extras but the food is good. I'll stay here again happily when travelling for work. If I come back for a personal visit I will find somewhere a bit more contemporary and boutique-y

“100% Satisfaction?” Upon arrival it appeared to be a in city Hampton Inn. Was expecting the typical Hampton Inn Service people have grown accustomed too. Well, not quite. As soon as I came thru the door I was a little put off by the people hanging out in the lobby chatting with the front desk clerk. I should tell you, it was 1:00 am and the 3 men in the lobby chatting with the Night Clerk were local and obviously friends or the employee.
We were checked in. I was a little more concerned when I realized the elevator was not a security card entry. You can just walk in, push a button and choose any floor you want to go to... and no one will stop you.
Then I came into the room or should I say cubby. Small, very small. But at this point I just got ready for bed and fell asleep. It was the next morning when I realized the condition of the room. Tub was dirty with hair, vanity was cracked, ac/heater vents were broken, and what should of been a warning when I came in was the Hampton Inn sign mangled and in need of repair.
There are several of this style hotel in NYC now that seem to be build by the same company. But I have to say, the Fairfield Inn by Marriott over by Times Square or the Four Points in Chelsea (which looks exactly the same as this hotel) are in much better condition and quality than this Hampton.

“Labor Day weekend stay at the City Suites Hotel” We booked the CSH based upon the mostly positive Tripadvisor reviews, it's proximity to the "L", as well as being close to the Halstead area.
We checked in and were given a room on the "quiet" side, but once we got to the room, we realized we were on top of the yogurt shop which plays loud music, and is open past midnight, and heard the music below us. We requested another room, and we relocated to another room, on the "quiet" side, but it was at the south end of the hotel which was still close to the train. The noise wasn't too bad, especially with the air conditioner on. 
We had a queen suite, which was nice and big. The bed was soft and plush, and had a good nights sleep.
We didn't partake in the free continental breakfast,, so can't comment on that.
Parking was $22/day, and it is in a secure garage about a block away, with in/out priviledges. 
Staff was nice, and accommodating.
Went to a few places around the hotel for breakfast or lunch. Sinbad's Middle Eastern food a few doors down was good for lunch. Ann Sathers for breakfast was OK... not quiet as good as all of the hype.
Overall, the CSH was a great place due to it's proximity to the train which was a great benefit, and would stay there again.

“Great find!! Glad we finally stayed here!” We currently live in California but my husband is a native New Yorker. We usually take a trip to NYC about once a year and have stayed in different hotels everytime. I had heard about the Hotel Newton a few years ago but never ended staying there. This trip we were taking some especially budget-minded family members for a little reunion of sorts. I remembered the Hotel Newton, checked it out some more, and decided this sounded like a good location and a good deal as well. 
My husband and I reserved a Superior Queen as did his older sister and her partner. The younger sister and her husband and two teenaged daughters went with the Deluxe Double. When we arrived those of us who had reserved the Superior Queen rooms were upgraded (at no extra cost) to Deluxe Queen rooms. We were on the 8th floor but I can't remember the room number. 
The Deluxe Queen rooms were very roomy by NYC standard with plenty of room. We had a nice flat screen television and a good sized closet. Rooms were very clean. The bathroom was a bit small and the only thing I found weird is that there was a window in the shower itself that wasn't privacy glass. As our room faced the street with other buildings across the street I felt a little uncomfortable as the window would show everything from about the waist up. Maybe it was one way glass?
Our family members traveling with teenagers ended up on another floor and their room was considerable smaller than ours, especially with two teenaged girls and all their stuff. They had a problem with their air conditioner not working but when they complained it was promptly replaced with a brand new one. 
Yes, the elevator is slow and there is only one but as we were never in a hurry that wasn't a big deal.
Front desk staff was great!
Location is good in a nice family neighborhood. When walking on a back residential street we even came upon a little girl and her dad with a lemonade stand. We also spotted actor Matt Dillon one night! Depending on which subway lines you are taking the station is either very close or a bit of a walk. There was a deli (can't remember the name) a couple of doors south of the hotel that served great breakfast items at really good prices. We got our breakfast there everyday. 
I would highly recommend the Hotel Newton to anyone looking to stay at a comfortable, clean, reasonably priced hotel in NYC.

“You get what you pay for (sometimes)... it's not "nice" but it was adequate.” First, the good news: it's reasonably safe, in a good location within blocks of Riverside Park, convenient to nice (family-priced) restaurants and subway stops, and it can be had for less than $150/night. 
However, it doesn't feel like a "good hotel"; the elevators are tiny, the hallways are narrow and unventilated, and the rooms are small and outdated. We had a queen size bed that was moderately comfortable, but there was garbage left by a prior guest under a nightstand, and the bedspread was dirty. Still, we did have a nice, tiled bath that seemed to have been recently remodeled. 
There are no amenities (short of an in-room closet safe and two granola bars we assume were left by Housekeeping?) and the carpets obviously need a good cleaning. There is free WI-FI, although it's very slow. The water pipes clanked mercilessly whenever we (or anyone around us) used the shower, and the A/C unit in the window had an annoying screech. The hotel lobby was cramped and crowded, but the hotel does have a tourism desk and there was, apparently, a fitness room, although we never found it. 
Was this a property that "added" anything to our trip, like the Lucerne and the Palace did, on prior NYC trips? No. Would I stay there again, if I had to, to save money? Yes. (But beware, if you book through Priceline; their site advises bidders to contact the hotel after booking with any special room requests but doesn't disclose in advance that this hotel charges a $60-per-night "upcharge" for special requests like a king-size bed or two doubles, nor do they assist in resolving misunderstandings!)

“Trendy, chic, and oh so convenient” We stayed here with one other couple at the end of April and the location is great for Broadway shows and 5th Ave shopping. The lobby is uber chic and a hip place to be after work for a drink. Be careful not to walk right past the check in cubby holes, same goes for the elevators - adds to the element of trendiness, but it does makes you feel foolish when you do it smack in the middle of Happy Hour in the lobby.
They carry shampoo, conditioner, lotion, body wash and soap by Malin + Goetz. It is dark in the room, which is really the only down side given that our window didn't provide much natural light either. The shower was spacious (no tub in our room) with great water pressure. I like the candles in the room, it's a nice touch, but don't forget to blow them out before you leave. 
There's a fridge, full of stuff, in the room. So it's not easy to accommodate items you purchase. No ability to make coffee/tea in the room w/o calling room service. The iPad in the room, upon first check in, didn't provide much for me. It took too long to register I was there so it wasn't until the morning of check out did I realize there was a lot more functionality available on it. So don't give up on it if you think it's pointless upon your first 20 minutes in the room. The bed was soft enough for me as a side sleeper and perfect for my husband who sleeps on his stomach. I like the lighting around the bed too. Good for reading late into the night.
The doorman are super nice and super cute. And they do look like bouncers.

“>Consigliatissimo. Ci tornero' sicuramente” Posizione ottimale. a pochissimi passi dalla metro Grand Central Station. 
Staff cordiale e attento nel risolvere i problemi. Stanza ampia e ben arredata con bagno pero' piccolo. L'hotel mette a disposizione gratuitamente, la mattina il caffe' per la clientela, idem due bocce d'acqua fresca a disposizione tutto il giorno, al gusto di arancia e limone nella hall. 
Che dire, il prossimo anno devo tornare a new york, e senza dubbio ci andro' ancora.
Consigliatissimo

“Great Location -- Better Price!” This is a wonderful hotel to stay at in Chicago. It's so close to Michigan Ave. (literally half a block) and is very nice for the cost of the rooms. We've stayed there several times in the past and will definitely stay again in the future. 
The lobby is very nice and clean, and the rooms are great also. It's not the nicest hotel in the city, but it's definitely not a dive. The only thing that wasn't great is that the bathrooms are a little small.... but how much time do you spend in the bathroom anyway? :-)
I would recommend this hotel to any family and friends of mine who are visiting chicago.

“Postage stamp sized rooms, priced too high. Someone in corporate's having a laugh.” The room is a "petite queen." Nice marketing line. I'm not a fan of this particular "boutique" hotel chain. Using the word "boutique" is also a bit of a marketing joke, I mean scam.
The rooms aren't terrible, and it's not a hell-hole like some NY hotels. The lighting and the flowers (sometimes) are pretty and a nice touch, and it's fairly quiet. Security isn't great. It does feel more like a small (I mean SMALL) rented apartment than an actual hotel room. You'll have to work really hard to maneuver your body between the bed and the walls. Not quite as bad as their other location at Times Square, the walls aren't as thin as their location midtown East with the sour milk in the cappuccino machine. There are very, very few amenities here. Some people like the coziness, and I'm happy for those who find it adorable and serene (and in many ways it is). It just has a teeny-weeny bit of a rip-off feel (there's a marketing line for you, on the house).

“Quiet, Comfortable & Great Location” Typical Courtyard downtown type property. Great location in Chicago's River North neighborhood, near Michigan Avenue, shopping, and numerous restaurants, pubs, etc. Starbucks in the lobby. Didn't really notice any city street noises. It was very quiet. Rooms were of the newer Courtyard variety...comfortable bed, well equipped and "connected". Didn't have to hassle with parking and the rate for this particular night was very reasonable ($114 + tax) although it can be considerably higher depending on demand.

“Micro rooms” The russian reception lady was very friendly and deserves a special praise. 
The 2-bed room was absolutely micro size, the space outside of the beds gave little room for anything. But you get what you pay for, a decent night's sleep for little money 200+ including breakfast. The full "plastic" breakfast was included, but we really do not like it.

“Nice hotel but...” I tried this boutique hotel based on tripadvisor reviews. The location is great, only one short block to the subway stop (Lexington line 23rd stop). At $200 p.n. (inc tax), the room size was acceptable (Madison type). Decor is modern & simple. 
On the negative, no refrigerator in the room, and window air-conditioning which is a bit loud. I was on a lower floor so you could hear the traffic as windows are not double glaze. Only one of the two elevators was working so if you are on an upper floor, you have to wait a long time. The hotel provides complimentary pastries and coffee in the front lobby which is good but many of the guests hangout by the tables giving the feel like you are a fast food restaurant. 
I think there are plenty of new boutique hotels that have opened in NYC for around $200-250 p.n. (inc tax) so I will try another hotel for next time.

“You get what you pay for!” The picture on the site are definately misconceiving. However, based on the reviews I did not go there expecting much. The room was small and did not look like the pictures on the net. First thing I noticed was the dirty carpet just as you walk in. The bathroom looks like its been remodelled but there was mold in the shower. Not impressed. And to top it all off..on our very last day there we found a cochroach in the bathroom. Next time I will definately pay a little more and get a clean hotel.

“Incredible value for a pretty good room and great location” I recently stayed one night in this hotel for a business trip. The location was perfect (just one block away from where our meeting was held, and very close to Columbus Circle). My room had up-to-date furnishings so it had probably been renovated recently. It was quite spacious although the bathroom was small. The bed was comfortable. I had requested a quiet room when I made my reservation, and was happy to be put in a room at the end of the hall, far from the elevators. Even so, I could hear clearly anything going on in the hallway. Luckily none of my neighbors turned out to be noisy night-owls so I had no problem sleeping. The cafe where breakfast is served doesn't have a lot of ambience, but the service was fast and the food was fine. The room rate was amazingly low for being in such a great location in NYC, making this hotel a fantastic value.

“Quality Inn on Westheimer - Quality should not be in the name. The roaches are taking over.” "Roach Motel". Stayed here because I've stayed here before and it was ok, it's amazing how a place can change in a years time. The place was run down like the management or owners don't care. And I saw many roaches(5 my first day), not a good feeling. Pool was murky, tiles in walkways missing, walkways had build up of dirt and could have used a good sweeping or maybe even power washed. Holes in the bedroom walls, empty frames hanging in room, even a bracket with nothing on it. Ice Machine was so dirty, believe that is a Health Dept violation. My last day talked with someone at front desk and they said their changing the name of hotel, I can see why. Sometimes felt like I was the only one staying there, only 2 cars in lot. I would never stay here again.

“Great NYC value” This hotel would likely not merit five stars in other places, but in New York it was an extremely pleasant surprise. Yes, the rooms were on the small side, but the room was at least 30% larger than the last room I got for this price ($150/night before taxes). And it was incredibly convenient.
We arrived on foot from Penn Station--an easy ten-minute walk--and had a smooth and friendly check-in. The lobby is attractive, not extensive, but nice and stylish. When we got the room, we were pleased--it is also attractive, with a decently spacious bathroom and a compact and well-laid-out bedroom. Free wi-fi and water, nice toiletries, brand-new sleek European bathroom fixtures, flat-screen tvs, an Ipod dock. Of course, no view but the next building's wall, but we didn't care. The room was also clean and well-maintained.
The location was also excellent--close to several subway lines, and an easy walk to lots of great places--tons of bars, restaurants, and shopping, as well as the Chelsea art galleries. We also stopped in the Rubin Museum of Himalayan Art right nearby, which is excellent. It is about a 20-minute walk from Union Square. We couldn't have asked for a better location in New York.
I say sacrifice the space--it's just not the same to stay in Brooklyn or Jersey City. This hotel was so ideal--clean, functional, so well-located--that we didn't mind not having a bigger room.

“Hardest beds ever!” This was indeed a nice new facility, with the cleanliness that I generally expect from LaQuinta and all that, but NOT NEAR worth the $100 per night we had to pay when we stayed. The decor is plain, and the beds are very hard, like sleeping on a box. The hotel had just opened so they were clearly brand new mattresses but felt extremely cheap quality and uncomfortable. It is also a bit further off the beaten path (and a good stretch from Katy Freeway) than the company's promo blurbs would have one believe. It was okay, but would never stay there again -- didn't get much sleep with the mattress issue.

“Better than expected...” As all other reviews, I'm extremely particular and reserve the 5 rating only for amazing places. The Hilton New York was not too far from that. If there was a 4 1/2 star, I'd give it that.
The service:
The staff here was great! From the people outside to the front desk and maid staff, everyone was extremely friendly. I booked the room through priceline and got a great deal. Then I found out the room was
for a standard queen, so I asked if I could change it to a king being
that it was our honeymoon. The lady at the front desk put us in a deluxe king...very nice! When leaving for the airport the guys outside hailed us a cabby quickly and put our luggage in...
The room:
After reading some of the previous reviews I was a bit concerned. However, the rooms are beautiful. They had a nice flat-panel TV, comfortable bed, good couch, and well-appointed amenities throughout.
The lights by the bed were quite interesting...a bit hard to describe
but the first I've seen of it's kind. The bathroom wasn't the most spacious, but good enough. I especially liked the shower. There aren't many places that put the floor at little slant so the water drains easily.
Overall, I thought the rooms were great. I would definitely stay here
again, esp if I get a good deal on priceline.

“nope...” the best thing about this hotel is the chinese restaurant that resides in the old la quinta food facility before the new owners let the property go to hell...when a hotelier group lets a property go to "private" investors"...and families take up residence IN the hotel...we all know what i mean...it almost universally degrades the service and property...this is NOT owned by LQ...and staying in their rooms is becoming a crapshoot...literally gambling...and as a biz traveler, after this stay and another exceedingly seedy one just prior in little rock...i am done...off to holiday inn express for me...a known quantity...unless and until THEY start selling off to proprietors who smell of incense...

“Absolutely Disgusting!!” I stayed @ The Cooper on 27 June 2009---The hotel was so awful, with dirty, deeply uninspiring rooms & an utterly pretentious staff, that I checked out the same night!!! Never once have I done this is a hotel.....To begin, there is a strong lack of staff @ the hotel----the whole 5 hr's that I was in this hole in the wall, I maybe saw 2 people actually working---the bellman, Nelson, showed me to the room, after I waited forever in the lobby for someone to ask me my last name..there is no actual check-in desk & they have random bell people in the lobby who bring you the room key---no credit card swipe, no I.D. request, NOTHING---very odd, extremely unsafe & really unheard of.....I had requested to have a tub in the room & called several times to make sure this would happen-----of course the room didn't have a tub!!----figures......my first room (floor 10, room 7)..was really hot, had pubic hair in the bathroom, a dead bug inside of the washcloth, a tattered sink, dusty floors, poor design, the mini bar refrigerator was hot, stains near the bed---the bed was AWFUL-----the mattress was terrible----the showers do not close all the way, water leaks everywhere----the lighting in the bathroom is insanely bad, one of the lights wasn't working, the robes are an utter joke----they were all Extra small sizes---so it seemed---they were like mini robes---terrible!---also one robe was missing-----the room was really tiny(i know this is NYC--I live here & I'm quite accustomed to small spaces, but the room really only had a bed---it overtakes the vast majority of the room-----the hotels website advertises fresh flowers in the room---they were never placed in the room----I requested turndown service---never happened----They are supposed to offer drinks & snack on arrival while you wait----NEVER happened-----The staff was really rude----Samantha was so uppity & really seemed like she could have cared less about my problems----her only offer was to send Housekeeping to the room to re-service it-----she was inexperienced & obviously has no interest in her guests------She told me the hotel was fully committed & that she couldn't move me-----Miraculously, once I complained to Allison, the manager, I was moved to a Cooper 3, as I was told I was upgraded to a Cooper 5 room----yeah, ok---maybe It was an upgrade but the view was terrible & I was only on the 10th floor----they could have done so much better........Alena, (I beleive this was her name) had attitude, just like Samantha---Allison moved me, saw the first room, was trying to make small talk (oh where do you live, any special plans for tonight,-------------she was like "oh my goodness" that is a bug in your wash cloth---"?!?!?!.....she just seemed fake, NEVER checked on me in my second room)---the second room was floor 10, room 1-----the room was pathetic, but It was cleaner------The view was even worse (*not that I cared)-----This room had the noisiest A/C----it made a ticking sound every 5 minutes that was sooo loud-----the bed was way too soft for me, as were the pillows........$265 was so not worth all of this trouble........So to conclude, the rooms have NO plugs for phones or charges, etc......the rooms were gross & I arrived at 7PM & checked out at 12:45AM --the a/c was too loud & it was soo late(wasn't about to call engineering, etc)-----it was that bad---so I just checked out!!! I went back home to my apartment where I was better off that at thia utter nightmare of a hotel----Scott, the day manager, ending up only charging me 1/2 of the original rate----he was nice, but I don't really feel like I should have had to pay, but I work in the hotel industry in NYC & know this is the norm----I WILL NEVER STAY AT THE CSH OR RECOMMEND IT TO ANYONE-----HANDS DOWN, THE WORST HOTEL IN THE CITY OF NEW YORK----I was so disgusted with this pathetic excuse of a hotel-----go to The Standard or Smyth instead--both amazing hotels in NYC!!! Honestly, I haven't made any of this up-----do yourself a favor & go elsewhere---NY is filled with amazing hotels, so don't waste time @ the joint......
Patrick/NYC
28 June 2009

“Worst Room I Have Ever Stayed In!” Where did the Hudson get those 3 1/2 Stars? We booked our room through PriceLine, which guarantees the room will accomidate 2 adults.
