When we reminded the desk clerk of this guarantee she immediately trided to extract extra money from us which seems to be a common practice of this hotel. The room is 9' 6" X 9' 6" with the AC and small window located behind the headboard? The closet is an indentation of the hall which will hold one 21" suitcase on the luggage rack and the other you can put under the rack, on the floor and stand on your head to dress. The bathroom door is 19" wide. There is 12" in front of the toilet, which means you must step over the toilet to get in the shower which is normal size. The sink sits on a stainless frame with a very sharp corner that hange over the edge of the toilet. I will next be contacting PriceLine about the rating of this hotel as well as their guarantee of the room acccomidation. In my 50 years of visiting New York City this is the worst room I have ever seen. P.S. we are both small in size!

“A little disappointed” I was not impressed with the Hilton when I discovered a bagel inside the nightstand drawer. Otherwise, the executive level double bedroom was ok but the executive lounge was not. The lounge was packed - it was difficult to locate seating. The food was awful - the pastries were dry and lacked any flavor. I have stayed here before - but not on an executive level floor - and I don't think it was worth the extra money. As others have commented, the bathroom was tiny.

“Frustrating Experience” The hotel was ok for the money. Definitely less than I'm used to, but I'm also not comparing it to the Four Seasons. Upon arriving to NY around 4pm, I had my assistant call the hotel to confirm that my room would still be available if I didn't check in until after 6pm that night. They confirmed that it was available as expected.
When I arrived at the hotel - about 7pm - they announced they had "upgraded" me to a double room. I had booked a king. I explained to the front desk person that in no place on earth was two double beds an upgrade from a king, but he said they were already sold out of the king rooms and that they could move me the next day. I agreed to this, but let him know I was dissatisfied.
The room was ok, but it had more than a few things that I either didn't like or was ambivalent about.
The Pros:
Good sized room - especially for NY
Comfortable bed (although the double was small)
Clean linens - sheets with a minimum of stains, towels fluffy
Nice size desk area - although I didn't use it, I always book a king bed so I can spread out on it - obviously not possible on a double bed
Relatively clean room, although I found some hair and dust on the floor when I dropped a pantyliner sticky-side down - yuck.
Shower pressure was good
The Cons:
Gray grout in bathroom makes the whole thing look dirty
Only one trashcan in the whole room - in the bathroom
Remote control wasn't strong enough to reach the TV in the double room (although this was better in the king room)
Weird bathroom layout (in the double room, I had to close the door to use the sink)
Ugly light fixture in the center of the room. Really ugly - they could have spent 5 dollars more and made it better
Inadequate lighting - I had to rearrange the lamps to get enough light to even read
Toiletries in the bathroom were obviously not fresh - yuck.
Lousy TV selection - no TBS, Food Network, Discovery channel, TLC...?
No washcloths - is this a European thing? This has happened to me several times lately...
Hallways look like what you'd find in a hostel
My opinion of the hotel might be different if they had offered anything for my inconvenience of switching rooms... but all they did was switch me. I think they should have refunded my first room night, or at the very least offered me a discount on a future stay. But nothing. I never heard from them again, not even to check in and see how the new room was working out.
Sigh. I probably won't stay there again - another NY hotel on my "Don't stay there" list.

“Another Great Stay” We always review comments on tripadvisor.com before booking a hotel. So once again this was a great stay. Nice hotel, great location and used priority club points for a free stay. Plays, pizza at John’s (on 44th @ 8th) and Cosi (1633 Broadway @ 50th).

“Good location; okay hotel; almost no closet/drawer space” Just your average hotel...nothing special. Pros: Good location between Times Square and Central Park. Clean. Good water pressure in the shower. Friendly enough staff. Starbucks coffee in the in-room coffee makers. Nice fitness room with cardio and free weights. Cons: Small rooms and very little closet/drawer space (My husband and I were there for 5 days climbing over our luggage and storing half of our folded clothes on the closet shelf and top of dresser; claustrophobic and messy...drove me nuts!). TINY bathroom with only one sink. Bring your own shampoo, soap and lotion. Theirs is eh.

“Average at best” Crowne Plaza Times square... stayed there last week 8th April, 2012. 
We had requested a Times Square view room, and wow what a view. As I recall the room was 3011 and right opposite the lifts which we thought would be noisy. But not the case.
The food around Times Square was ordinary and expensive. The hotel was nice enough. the bar was on level 2 with virtually no view, the Manhattan cocktail was soo strong.
The hotel had no safe, well a safe you had to swipe with your credit card... no will not do that, what if you lost your card or it was stolen... Had to have my passport with me all the time, was a pain.
No fridge, no safe, was a water heater thingie, but no cups. So did not bother.
The shower was the standard bathtub with shower overhead. The bed King size was comfy and clean and I checked for bed bugs, all seemed ok and after sleeping was ok, no itching.
I had trouble sleeping 30 floors up, my body kept feeling it was falling, but did it for three nights, and glad to come home.
The breakfast was underwhelming and the hotel in general was underwhelming - but expensive. 
One of our backpacks had been opened during the day, so not sure about the security, but all our electronic equipment was locked in a suitcase while we were out of the room because of the no safe issue.
Next time in New York I would love to find an apartment and stay for longer and really look around, perhaps a month and visit other places. NYC is interesting and multi layered place, but a like it a lot

“Teeeny tiny rooms!” Until my recent stay, I've only had drinks at the Hudson. Little did I know how incredibly tiny the rooms were! I was warned by many friends but to have to walk through the bathroom door sideways. It would have been nice if there was a flat screen TV somewhere instead of that tiny 13inch tucked away in a closet. It was noisy but I am a deep sleeper so it didn't bother me.

“Quiet hotel in good location” This is a very nice hotel in an excellent location. For reference, it is near the Waldorf Hotel. However, since it fronts on 52nd, rather than a main street, cabs can be a little hard to come by. Our room was an adequate size, with lovely furnishings and a nice bathroom. I might reserve one up from the run of the house next time because I think this was probably the smallest of their rooms. The room was also extremely quiet with a minimum of street noise. The lobby and public areas are smaller, understated and elegant. This is not the big splashy lobby type hotel. In fact, two cab drivers did not know the hotel by name, so have your street adress handy. I would also strongly recommend putting out the door hangar for coffee the night before if you want coffee. There is no coffee in the room and when I called room service for just coffee I was told it would be 45 minutes to an hour. I would like to see this hotel add coffee pots to the room to eliminate this problem.

“Dirty and sad...” Having enjoyed stays at 2 other Holiday Inns very recently, I decided to stay here in an effort to save my company $$ on business travel and be close to my meeting location. Next time I will spend the extra $$ to stay somewhere else. The front desk attendant was friendly enough, but when i got to my room, it smelled so bad of "old stale smoke trying to be masked" I asked for another room, for the first time in my many years of business travel. The girl, which is the only way I can refer to her, as she was not wearing a name tag, readily gave me another room, which was not much better. The toilet in this room had black moldish looking stuff under the rim, which I probably wouldn't have noticed, but the seat was up. And this is one of the most puzzling things I've ever seen, the cord from the tv, snaked under the carpet to the outlet on the opposite wall. I had to walk across this cord every time I went to the bed. Isn't this an electrical hazzard?? Plugging in my phone by the nightstand revealed dirt and very heavy dust. The carpet made me wish I had brought slippers. I travel all the time, and am not a picky person. But, this hotel just made me sad, and I could not wait to check out. On a positive note, while leaving my room in the morning to check out, the housekeeper in the hall was extremely friendly and wished me a great day. Maybe I just had a bad room, and service is definitely positive, but not enough to trump clean, in my opinion.

“Excellent Facility - Recommended” We stayed at the Renaissance while attending a UH game this past weekend. The hotel was excellent, from the condition of the room to the friendly staff, making this one of the finest facilities that we have ever stayed in. The only draw back is the cost of valet parking but they have a self parking arrangement for only $9 per night. The location of the hotel is convenient to 59 which gives easy access to downtown , medical center , south area or west area.

“Great Value” I stayed at the Novotel in June 04. The rooms were spacious for what I was expecting. They had everything you need. The air conditioning was a welcome relief from the hot/humid weather. It was temperature controlled so you didn't have to freeze or boil in the night. The housekeeping staff was extremely helpful. Booked $169 for 7 nights via Novotel website and it was a bargain. You could walk almost everywhere and the subway stop was less than two blocks away. 
No problems at check-in or check-out. With the great views of Times Square (for the cheap rate!) and the 9th floor I was worried about noise. Didn't need to be. I never heard anything from the street with the windows closed and even though our room had a connecting door I didn't hear a thing from neighboring rooms. My husband is quite sensitive to noise in hotel rooms and he didn't have a problem. Not many Americans staying there, though, mostly tour groups and airline crews. 
Didn't try the breakfast as over $9 for continental breakfast seemed a bit steep. Go to one of the many diners around the area and get a HUGE hot breakfast and coffee for under $8 each.
Overall, it's great value for money and I'd stay there again. If you're looking for a romantic getaway, it's probably not for you, but as a base for a holiday in New York it was perfect.

“Ottima posizione, assolutamente consigliato!” Abbiamo soggiornato in questo hotel per 5 notti durante il ponte dell’Immacolata. Essendo la nostra terza visita a New York e non volendola vedere da “turisti” abbiamo scelto un hotel che fosse in una posizione strategica e vicino a Times Square.
Dopo una lunga ricerca la scelta è caduta sul 414 Hotel per via dei prezzi competitivi (rispetto ad altri hotel di qualità decisamente inferiore), la posizione e gli ottimi commenti.
Premetto che se tornassi a New York, non esiterei a riprenotare in questo piccolo hotel perché l’esperienza è stata davvero positiva.
L’hotel si trova sulla Restaurant Row, a un paio di isolati dalla fermata della metro più vicina, nonché dalla stazione degli autobus e vicinissimo a Times Square (neanche 5 minuti a piedi). In meno di un quarto d’ora si raggiunge a piedi anche la Penn Station, dove arriva la maggior parte dei treni diretti a New York.
La zona è molto tranquilla e la sera si anima per via dei numerosi ristoranti che si trovano a pochi passi dalla struttura. Ci hanno dato una stanza nella parte interna dell’hotel (a cui si accede attraversando il cortile interno) e nonostante qualche rumore che arrivava dalle scale o dai piani superiori, era davvero silenziosa. L’hotel non dispone di un ascensore e questo può effettivamente essere problematico per gli occupanti delle stanze ai piani alti. Fortunatamente la nostra era al piano terra.
L’ingresso è molto carino anche se piuttosto piccolo. Ci sono la reception, un desk con il computer (è possibile usare connessione e stampante gratuitamente), il tavolo per la colazione (se non erro con 6 posti) con un piccolo buffet, un camino e un paio di poltrone… il tutto in pochi metri quadrati.
Non ho comunque mai avuto problemi a trovare posto la mattina per fare colazione… Di fianco si trova una piccola cucina dove è possibile servirsi con bevande calde e fredde a ogni ora del giorno. La mattina invece al buffet si possono trovare bagel, briosche, marmellata, succhi, cereali etc. Non variano di giorno in giorno ma è gratis, quindi va bene così!!!
Lo staff è molto cordiale inoltre è possibile depositare gratuitamente i bagagli al momento della partenza anche se non c’è un vero e proprio deposito ma vengono lasciati davanti al banco della reception.
Consiglio questo hotel a tutti e spero di poterci tornare presto!

“Nice hotel, rooms OK but dirty” I stayed here for a business trip and found long hairs on the bathroom floor. As I have very short hair I deduced that they were not mine.
This rather put me off this property and I did mention it to the front desk who merely said that I should have mentioned it when I found it (the hair).

“This is a good place to stay in Chelsea” Located on a relatively quiet side street, this relatively small hotel has reasonably nice rooms (medium-size by NY standards) and is clean. The elevators are slow because there are only 2 and 22 stories (each floor does not have many rooms). There is an outdoor cafe/restaurant which is nicely situated as it is in a recess from the street. The front desk service can be slow as the area is small.

“Chaos due to renovation” In the midst of renovation chaos in the lobby, people, bagage and waiting lines. We got a newly renovated micro size room, the style was something you must get used to. The renovation seems to have very tight budget. The new window air conditioner was so noisy, that we had to turn it off. Strange for a new machine (the service people were in the room to change a filter and they said the ac was ok)
“Always my top choice when traveling from ORD” Great great location, room is not always so nice though.Airport view rooms are nice, fitness room pretty awesome

“The Mansfield - Great Value and Terrific Boutique Hotel Experience” I just completed a 3 night stay at The Mansfield. It was a very unique mid-town experience and combined quality/location/value in what was a very strong proposition. More specific thoughts along with grade for each area follow.
Staff
From the moment I got out of the cab in the rain, to the moment that I got in another cab (my sole cabs btw on the trip; I strongly prefer walking/subway/busses) I could not say enough things positive things about the staff. First, they were professional. They were always very friendly and made The Mansfield feel like a home away from home. If they did not remember me by name, the certainly remembered me as a guest. The great thing was this attitude held true from the front desk staff, waitresses in the restaurant even the housekeeper on my floor. Great general manger. Keep it up. Grade A+.
Location
I used to stay around the park during trips to New York, but for years have moved toward mid town location. Happy made switch, as don’t think for business traveler, anything beats mid-town if need to get around to multiple locations during the trip due to subway access near both Times Square and Grand Central. The Mansfield and its owners certainly are not alone in enjoying the location, as there are perhaps 7-8 hotels on 44th between 5th and 6th. It worked out great. Location A+.
Room
I was able to get upgraded at check in to a small suite. I enjoyed it. The good outweighs the bad, but I’ll offer both comments. Good points are the room was nicely paneled, larger than most, had two flat screens TV and generally was quite. The bad was that it was much to dark for my liking as was an interior room on light courtyard and did not have accessible temperature controls without calling the engineer. Grade B-
Ammenties
I’m still amazed in how hotels create their value proposition. In hotels like The Peninsula and Ritz Carleton and others at the upper end many amenities are extra. It seems like the better the hotel the more they feel confident charging for everything. At the lower end every amenity comes with the room. What was terrific about The Mansfield was that it offered a boutique luxury experience and included all amenities in the price. There is a terrific fitness center, better than lots of much bigger hotels, free Wi-Fi, a 24 hour complimentary espresso bar (terrific), a great lobby/library, a nice lounge off the lobby, I believe complimentary breakfast (was for me). Loved them all and used them all. Grade A+
Value
Value is the word of the moment and The Mansfield offered tremendous value. It’s not necessary to elaborate, but it would be easy to spend 2 to three times as much for a room in New York and not have as good an experience. Grade A+++
Summary
I completely and thoroughly enjoyed The Mansfield. Would stay there again in a minute and probably will again soon.

“Good value and great location” I stayed at this hotel for three nights during a recent holiday in New York. The location was midtown and close to Times Square. My room was large by New York standards and contained a small separate living area. I was not in one of the renovated rooms, but this was my choice when I made the booking. Although the furnishings were dated the room was clean and very comfortable. Check in was easy and the hotel staff extremely helpful. The hotel was quiet and there was no street noise. The room airconditioner was noisy, but didn't keep me awake. The hotel was excellent value for money.

“If you want to save money stay here!” I've stayed in so many Chicago hotels I lost count. This location is excellent. The front desk staff was very helpful and hip. Room very modern, large and included a fridge and pull out comfy chair. Parking was only $36 per 24 hours here. Recalling that you are in a big city here and not some country estate, would night noise from the Loop be a surprise? Would a panhandler on the sidewalk be a shock? Of course not. If these things are disturbing to you, don't stay here. If you crave peace and quiet, four star ambiance and price, you should go up to the Peninsula. The Italian restaurant in the building is very charming and did I mention the building is an architectural masterpiece by Herbert Hugh Riddle, 1928? Its a gem.

“Disappointing and not luxury” Poor service - staff with a bad attitude. Computer check in is not acceptable. Waiting on line to be greeted by a computer. Restaurant is too expensive for what you get and staff is far from friendly. Had to beg for bread which was stale and thrown at me.

“Fabulous Central Park location, updated rooms, good service” This hotel has been recently renovated and straddles the fence with a classically styled lobby, and updated modern rooms. The level of service was very high with the staff going out of their way to be responsive and helpful. Quite a find for NYC. I also liked the well equipped 24 hour gym.
As you would expect, given the location in NYC and level of service, it is somewhat pricy.

“A nice little midtown treasure” I go to NYC about once a month for brief business trips. I feel comfortable navigating the city so I don't really care where in Manhattan I stay in terms of location but want to get the best price on a 4 or 5 star hotel. I go for clean and safe which is not easy to find in NYC in anything below a 4 star rated hotel.
I chose this hotel because I had a promotional code from Kimpton and the price was under $200 a night. 
This is a small little place. If you are going for atmosphere in the lobby or a great street, this is not really the right thing. The room was clean and luxurious. The bed was comfortable. The room had a huge flat screen television. 
My room was on the 7th floor and faced Park Avenue and was actually quite noisy. Other than that, it was a great, clean, nice place to stay. The staff was very friendly. 
The location is great, a couple of blocks down the street from Grand Central. I would definitely stay there again.

“Great Location, good service” This is the second time we've stayed at this Sofitel. We love the location, but hate how Sofitels don't provide top sheets on their beds. One can easily walk all over the City from this location. While the hotel has what appears to be an acceptable, but somewhat expensive restaurant, a few doors down there is a diner that is great for breakfast. You can't beat this location. Its central, but not in the center of the crowds and craziness.

“Ordinary, expensive conference hotel” Check-in was good, rooms are ok - quite spacious for NYC. The gym was very small and crowded and there was a queue at 0645 in the morning which was very disappointing. One day my room wasn't made up at all. We were here for a conference, and the food was actually OK. Expensive room rates though, and charging extra for wireless internet on top of the room rate is just rude. Wouldn't rush back.

“I understand what all the fuss is about....” I needed a one-night stay, for a pre-cruise Saturday in January and had previously booked the Hampton Inn. Based on the recommendations here, I decided to try the Affinia Dumont. 
I paid $180 (plus tax and tags) for a one-night stay for two people in the cleanest, most spacious and comfortable room I've had in Manhattan. They gave us an early checkin (1pm), a late checkout (2pm), kept the car for a few extra hours and were the nicest, friendliest staff I could have hoped for.
The room was spacious - two queen beds, a comfy chair, an Aeron desk chair, a great bath with a wonderful shower, and a kitchen unit (which we didn't use). WAY more room than most studio apts. 
It was immaculately clean, had an ironing board, and a safe.... everything I could ask for.
The staff were friendly - when they overheard a conversation about dinner, the bellman offered a few suggestions. (I wouldn't rush back to the Italian place he mentioned, but I can't blame him for helping.)
All in all, it was a terrific stay... it doesn't get any better.

“Great location” This place is very convenient , located in the heart of all the attractions. It is within a walking distance to time square so it is very easy to get sound especially to the night shows. It is well decorated. The room service is average. This is a kind of place if you stay and want to travel around during the day. I didn't use any facilities inside the hotel though I was there for 5 days. There're lots to see in new York!

“Naaaaaa” Great location , that's about it.
Long line for check in and out , dark rooms , comfortable beds , great TV system,
breakfast area to crowded and busy . Bathrooms total disaster , like a very small closet , no place to put your stuff . Not worth the $$.

“Great Stay” I stayed in this hotel for two nights - it is very conveniently located and if you are using the subway - L - its on the red Line, the station is 'Grand' right beside the Hotel.
Also beside the Hotel great restaurants and Shops like Bloomingdales and Nordstrom.
I arrived late and was welcomed and quickly checked in , later I came down and needed a taxi and the Front Desk agent went out to the street in the freezing Chicago weather to flag a cab for me.
The rooms are spacious and have all the facilites that you are used to in a Hilton like Coffee Making facilities,etc.The hotel also offers free Wifi :)
I had breakfast in the Weber restaurant on the two mornings and the service, menu offerings were fantastic.They also have a great bar with great bar food and beer selections , although if you are a guest at the Hotel you will need to show ID to charge items to your room .
I also was impressed by the genuine frendliness of the colleagues and for their concern that you are having an enjoyable stay.
Can definitely recommend this Hotel in downtown Chicago , and will be back.

“The best service EVER! And fabulous views with a free breakfast.” I'm sorry, but the service was the best I have every experienced, EVER! And trust me I have traveled to Intercontinentals, Grand Hyatts, Four Seasons, etc. The staff were unfailingly polite, helpful, and friendly. The room (we had a suite and paid $500+) was fantastic! It had views of the Hudson and the Statue of Liberty. We had 16 friends over for a party and it didn't feel crowded. Free breakfast was great and room service that I had one night (steak frites) was better than many restaurants. Oh, and free internet - one of my pet peaves with other hotels. The only minimal complaint: the cleaning staff could've done a slightly better job vacuuming. That is it!

“Great Staff but some issues” Stayed in this hotel with my adult daughter for 6 nights. The room was small, even by NY standards. While it was tastefully furnished with comfortable twin beds, the lighting is ambient and impossible to really see anything. Since the room is so small, why they have chosen to take up valuable space with a huge mini bar boggles my mind. There were two drawers for clothes. But it does have a large closet that has its own light. The bathroom, of course, is small and very hot!!! There is no fan or AC vent in it and it appears that some sort of heating system is embedded in the wall and radiates extra heat.
We also had a lot of difficulty with the room heatling and cooling. Now they always responded quickly to our requests, but they replaced the entire unit three times. Each time it would work for a while and then quit blowing cold air. Now even though I'm not an engineer, even I would think that 3 units could not all be faulty. Finally the last maintenance person just reset the signal between the thermostat and the unit and it worked again. The hotel did make proper amends for the incovenience, but still we had several very warm nights. There is a large safe in the room that is a nice touch along with big robes for your use. The bedding was upscale and very comfy and the big flat screen tv was mounted on a pole and could be seen in any direction. They also provide two wine glasses and a wine bottle opener in the room, another nice touch. However, there is not a coffee maker in the room.
The free use of the Acura is wonderful and the staff there is very accomodating. You can schedule it whenever it is free to take you reasonable distances. Can save lots in cab fare. (note: they will not pick you up from someplace but will take you from the hotel). We had breakfast at the hotel several times and the food was very good, but of course, at NY prices. Across Lexington about 2 blocks toward 45th, there is a pizza, sandwich and breakfast place that has two eggs, homefries and toast for $2.99. Best deal around if you don't mind going out. The restaurant is not open for dinner but will be happy to deliver room service with about, in the aggregate, a 30% surcharge.
There is a bar in the hotel and a living room where people sit for cocktails. However, they play this "house music" very loudly and it really is for a young crowd. It is also played on the elevator and is very annoying. You can use the business center computer for about $1.00 a minute. You can, however, print out your boarding pass for free in the lobby.
My daughter and I both used the Bliss Spa and we were very disappointed. This is not a first class spa. My manicure took 12 minutes!!! My daughter said it was the most expensive and worst massage she ever had. When I went to check out, the line to pay was 8 people deep. We were not impressed.
We also were not impressed that you had to pay $16.95 for internet service in your room. You could use wireless service in the living room area so I saw many folks taking up seats in this cocktail area and just using their laptops. I know I did. 
The hotel is not very near good shopping. You can walk to it but it is a little hike. There were really few restaurants nearby either. It is close to Grand Central which is an experience in itself.
The concierge was very helpful and we used this service many times. The front desk staff was also very accomodating and helped in any way they could. I was truly impressed with all of the staff as they all treated us valued guests. The Manager(s) responded quickly to any of our problems. All in all, I thought it was a great hotel.

“Great experience!” The hotel was close to the airport and close to the location of my meetings. I was very happy to be able to order late room service when my evening meeting went much longer than anticipated. Because I did not rent a car during my trip as my meetings were in walking distance, I did not have the option to go elsewhere for dining. But the hotel restaurant was great and the value was very good. The airport shuttle was very convenient and quick. The front desk staff were great and even though there was some sort of convention going on, it was still very quiet and I was able to work. I come to Houston often but never stayed at this particular hotel. I will definitely stay again and again.

“Could Use Some Improvements” Stayed here for one night and here are my observations on the plusses and minuses:
Plusses:
1) Great location
2) Terrific water pressure in shower
3) Comfortable bed
The minuses:
1) Lots of street noise (I was on 12th floor). Having stayed in many other midtown hotels, this was by far the noisiest. Windows must be original construction
2) Flat screen TV's, but no HD! It's 2012 folks. Watching a standard def picture on a flat screen tv is ridiculous for an upscale midtown property
3) Others have observed that you cannot adjust the room temperature by yourself. You must call the front desk to have someone come up. Not very practical. I returned from an evening engagement at 11 o'clock to find the room chilly. Was I really supposed to call the front desk at that time and wait for someone to show up to make the room warmer?
I do hope that the hotel will consider improving on these points.

“Great stay! Would stay again” There are a lot of bad reviews of this hotel. I was a little concerned about staying there. But it was great! Beautiful lobby, efficient service, friendly staff and - best of all: nice room! I mean the room was BIG when compared to what you normally get in NY. Everything was clean. Sure, a tiny bit of wallpaper was peeling off in the bathroom. But so what - who even cares? The room was comfy and quiet. Can't beat the location. I'd definately stay there in the future.

“Cool place” My 4 year old son and I were impressed by Le Parker Meridien. It's a hip hotel that's also appropriate for little ones. The lobby has an incredible, funky, unique hamburger joint that feels like a great hidden secret (it's behind the curtains). The Sunday brunch is one of NYC's best. It's within walking distance to the park, museums, 5th Ave., etc. but it's also on a relatively quiet Ave. (6th). They play cartoons in the elevators for gosh sakes! It was hard to get my little one out of the elevator. And no turn down service or robes, but the price was a bit cheaper than comparable properties so it was worth it. The room was a bit small as was the bathroom. But this is NYC. I was impressed by the staff and the gym/pool. Not many pools in the city, especially top floor ones with a view. We were very happy with our choice and will stay there again.

“Am I the only person on earth that does NOT rate this hotel???” Have found TripAdvisor reliable for a couple of years now and rarely disappointed by highly rated establishments but was on this occasion. Maybe we paid over the odds but I felt that our 4 night stay was NOT value for money! We paid approx average of $370/£253 per night....could do a lot better in most world cities for that price am sure.....can't help feeling could also have done a lot better in NY! Positives: location in Midtown (handy for Times Sq; Main train stations etc.); bed comfortable; quiet; staff very helpful and responsive. Negatives: Room size not large (and understand that we had a larger room as there were 3 of us; but understand NY rooms are generally small); bathroom in need of redecoration; breakfast uninspiring but plentiful althought I would hardly call it complimentary when considering the overall cost!; found pair of dirty pants under bed (not ours!) on leaving!!!; 2 light bulbs not working; security lock broken and unuseable (housekeeping staff were very responsive but did not trouble them re the last 2 items). Could have certainly got 4* hotel at least £100 per night cheaper on hotwire.com (which only discovered after we had left) although you don't learn which hotel until you make the booking. So all in all, not bad as such but just felt that we got poor value for money. Will certainly try somewhere else next time.

“good place, good location” Booked this room on hot-wire.com. It does have a great location and the place is in overall good condition considering its age. The only issue i had with the place was the size of the bed. but thats wasn't that big of deal. Check in was smooth and fast. People working at the hotel were freindly

“Never Ever Again!!!!” Lame, lame, lame. Booked 8 nights here and from the first night, nothing but problems. Air conditioning system didn't work and it took three phone calls and an hour later for a maintenance worker to look at it. It didn't get fixed so I asked to move rooms at 4 a.m. The t.v. didn't work properly when I bought a movie, so maintenance had to check that out too. Fixtures in the bathroom were falling off. If you want to use a fridge, you can rent one for $50. We asked specifically if this was the rate for the entire stay or per night and the woman on the phone said it was for our entire stay. When we received the bill we were charged $50 for each day. I called the service desk and the guy Mike said he'll take it off but when we went to leave and asked for a copy of the receipt, it was still on there. The snotty front desk agent said well, "you guys got the friends and family rate" but we took it up with the front desk manager who said he can't take all the charges off. What a way to treat friends and family right? Just because we got a discount doesn't mean we should be treated like dirt, people. I will never stay at a W Hotel ever again. There were a couple of nice workers but most of them are jerks. Don't judge a book by its cover. The decor is nice but the hotel itself is falling apart as is the communication between staff.

“Not ready for prime time - not now and maybe not ever.” The positive things first: friendly staff, great location. Those are really the only two redeeming things about this hotel. With so much competition in the hotel market in NYC, they are really going to have to do something drastic to compete. When we walked into our suite there was a very distinct odor of something like a damp basement. It never went away. When my husband saw the rooms he wondered what had possibly been renovated. The decor was nice enough but it didn't look that new to be honest. The bathroom was definitely new and was nicely marbled. However, for a suite it was extremely small. There was a single sink and the toilet was right next to the bathtub. The shower was decent and the fixtures were new. There is no powder room so any guests you have will be using your bathroom. The bed was very comfortable. The service was excruciatingly slow. It took over 45 minutes for a bucket of ice. It took 2 days for my husband to get internet service. The restaurant (Le Cirque) is not owned by the hotel and it took over an hour to have breakfast served. There were about 8 elevators but only 2 every working at one time. The rest were not in service. One evening we returned from dinner to find the security man and the plumber in our unit because someone on the floor above had left water running and the entire kitchenette was flooded. The did offer to move us but since we were leaving the next day, we declined. They did clean up the mess. The following morning there was non stop hammering going on below us and it took about 4 calls to the front desk to get it stopped. They are still doing construction out front so you have to walk around the barricades. We just can't ever stay here again. We're going back to either the 4 Seasons or the Mandarin where we usually stay. We thought we'd give this a try due to the renovation and the location but it's not worth the price in any way.

“I'll be back.” Clean, quiet, great bed, good views, fabulous Cosmopolitans, convenient Starbucks, best showerhead and working elevators. Our stay was perfect. Let me take a moment and explain the elevator system. Go to the correct set of elevators, the floor numbers are on the entryway wall. Enter your floor number into the system, an electronic version of pushing the up button. The system will show you which elevator car to go to, for example enter floor 8 and the system tells you to go to car B. If you enter a floor number and double XXs appear, the system is overloaded and the elevators have individual attendents that will stop on each floor as called. We found the system overloaded only in the evening rush hour. Everything else about the stay was perfect.

“Aging, but excellent value and large room” Somehow my taste, or should I say Priceline's taste, in Manhattan hotels runs to type. Beautiful restored lobby, old-fashioned hotel, and a room behind it that has seen better days.
For some reason I was upgraded to a suite, despite paying much less than rack rate during the UN General Assembly when hotel space was almost impossible to find. Cookie-cutter places like Doubletree were demanding over $400. I got the Wellington from a blind Priceline Bid (3-star level) at $241.
The suite was huge, with a full-sized living room and a bedroom. Each had TV and phone. The LR had a sofabed, but the only bathroom was off the bedroom which would make it a little less convenient for a family or two couples. I had a mini-fridge between the rooms but no stove. No plates or utensils in the cabinet, either.
The room was clean. Upholstery was faded, paint chipped, but it was definitely not "run down" or derelict. Just past its prime. The bathroom had a wonderful old shower and sink. The toilet was new, flimsy, and awkwardly placed.
The location is outstanding.
I notice some other reviewers got rooms inferior to mine. I trust TA reviewers, but in this case I can say it is possible to get lucky and have a very pleasant stay.

“Great Location” If you want to stay at a chain hotel, be close to Times Square as well as great dining and shopping then this is a pretty good location. I've been to New York several times but this trip I found to be the best location wise. I wanted to stay longer just so I could try more of the numerous restaurants that were within a block or two from the hotel. The rooms were redone, with black marble surfaces. I found the pillows to be too hard, yet my business associate, in a different room, found them to be too soft. The bathrooms are nice but very small. There are glass enclosed step in showers though which is a huge plus for me (I hate bathtub showers). The two lobby bars are great and the breakfast buffet is plentiful (including bread pudding!!). The maid staff were the friendliest i have ever encountered in all of my travels. The always stopped to say hello, have a nice day, etc. Amazing. I would return to this hotel.

“Excellent Hotel” What can I say that hasn't already been said. This is an excellent hotel. The location is superb, the staff were extremely friendly (and I mean all of them), the rooms were spacious and spotless and the view from the 32nd floor beautiful. We spent seven nights here in July, (myself, my wife and two children) and loved every minute of it. Its's ideally situated with only a 5 minute walk to the metro, 10 minutes to the Empire State and 15 mins to Grand Central. There are loads of places to eat within easy walking distance and you feel perfectly safe walking around day or night. The doormen were particularly pleasant and helpful and made my six year old feel very special by greeting him and engaging him in conversation every time they saw him. I would definitely stay here again and would recomend it to anyone visiting New York

“立地最高です” ２０１１年１０月に家族で宿泊しました。
ＭＯＭＡは目と鼻の先。タイムズスクエアやブロードウェー、セントラルパークも徒歩圏内。ホテル周辺には日本食を始め、各国の料理を楽しめるレストランがあり、立地は抜群で便利です。
３８階のヒルトンクラブ用の部屋に宿泊しましたが、部屋からの眺めはよくありません。東京都内のオフィス街？みたいな景色しか望めません。ただ、このヒルトンクラブ用の部屋は、３７階にラウンジが設けられており、朝の時間帯：朝食、昼から午後：クッキー類、夕方：ワイン、ビール、おつまみ等、が楽しめます。部屋は狭いです。クイーンサイズのベッドが一つで、そこに大人２人で寝ました。大人３人と子供一人で宿泊しましたが、エキストラベッド１台とベビーベッドを追加で入れてくれました。バスタブはありません。でも、TV JAPAN（北米専門の日本語放送）は入るし、快適なホテルライフを楽しめました。また宿泊したいです。
P.S.　ホテル内のレストランは味はまあまあですが、結構お高い・・・。B級グルメに興味がある方は、夜７時位からホテルの目の前に出る（アラブ系の料理？）チキンオーバーライスの屋台（Vender）は、地元でも有名です。いつも行列ができています。６ドルなので、ここでテイクアウトしてお部屋で食事してもいいかもです。

“Good hotel” I stayed here for a business trip. The location of the hotel is good to walk to Times Square however I would be mindful at night as it is across from the Port Authority and on the same block as the NY Parole Board, which was unsettling. 
The staff was helpful and good at their jobs, check in was efficient.
The lobby is quite small and despite the location of this hotel and that it is in a city, a room key is not required to use the elevator as I would have expected and is standard in similar hotels I have stayed at in Manhattan.
The room was clean but quite small as was the bathroom. It was adequate for just one person for one night. I would not suggest this room for more than one person or for a much longer stay.
Overall I would stay here again if I had to, but as a female travelling alone, I would prefer to stay at a hotel that is not a block off a main road and by the parole board. I do not feel that the cost of over $300 per night was worth it. I have stayed at other properties in Manhattan for the same or less which had much larger rooms and more ammenities. This is a fine hotel, however I feel that for the price, you can find a better location and one with larger rooms but its not a disappointment.

“Disappointing stay” I was in New York on business for one night and wanted a hotel in the theatre district. I normally stay at another hotel, but it was full for the night I needed and so decided on the Paramount based on the recent reviews. I was given a room described as a superior queen size bedroom (a true contradiction in terms as the size must of been for the queen of the ants it was so small) and charged a discounted rate (!?!) of $350.00 (including taxes) which in my mind was one third more than the value of the room at any given time. Even though I was on a high floor, I was kept awake most of the night by the street noise because of the very poor sound insulation from the window in the room. I find little to recommend this hotel and can only assume it appeals to the most naive of tourists who like it because of it's very central location and who would assume that they are getting a great deal for their expenditures. I for one, shall not be recommending it to anyone.

“Nice Rooms but Shady Managment” May 2010. We had the opportunity to spend several nights at this property when we were in the area. The hotel off of a side road off of the toll way. Be careful about driving on the toll way or you could get fined $25.00 each way. I recommend staying off of it. This property is near Rice University, the Galleria is not too far by car and it is near the River Oaks area of Hourston. There is plenty of free parking in the parking lot. 
Check-in was very fast but I knew something was wrong when the front desk person take my Marriott Bonus Bucks coupon. 
Our room was comfortable and your standard Big Box Residence Inns. The bed was comfortable. Lighting in the room was good. , The work desk was moderate and the office-style chair okay.. My The room had a sleeper sofa and a comfortable arm chair.
 The bathroom was clean.. Lighting was good, the towels were plentiful, and the shower worked well – immediate hot water with plenty of pressure. Bath amenities include shampoo, conditioner, lotion/
The breakfast buffet featured scrambled eggs, hot oatmeal, and all the other things you would expect on a breakfast buffet.
The hotel public areas were clean and well-kept, but could use an updating to the style of the rooms. 
When checking out and settling my bill, the front desk management processed Marriott Travel cards first before the Residence Inn Bonus Bucks. This left with a difficult choice because if I used the Bonus Bucks I would not able to use the face value. This is awful strange since on this trip the Residence Inn at Alamo Square in San Antonio had no problem accepting the full amount of the Bonus Buck. Also after this trip I used a Marriot property bonus buck at the Renaissance Madison in Seattle and they had no problem accepting it. I had used a Bonus Buck at the J.W. Marriott Union Square in San Francisco and they had no problem accepting it. This property and it's managment was sneaky in what it did just to make a few bucks!
I have a future trip scheduled for Houston and it is fair to stay I won't be staying at this property again!

“Both good and bad points.” The hotel has many good and bad points so it is up to you to decide which ones are more important to you.
From our stay on 24-25 April. 2010
Good - location, libary with free espresso machine, free NY times daily.
Free lobby computers, renovated bed, showers, hardwood floors.
Bad - Don't care attitude among all staff and shifts.
Rooms ending with 07-12 as these face the airducts on roof of the building next door.
It is like sleeping in a subway station.
Room 611 - great beds, shower - very noisy poorly constructed air condition in window, very noisy due to air ducts outside windows - earplugs were not enough.
Room 809 - lower noisy volume, but saging bed, very small bathroom.
The door is actualluy the same size as the room closet.
BTW, this is the 3rd and last time at this hotel. staff has always been bland, but we dealt with that when the rooms were better pre-renovation.

“Great Stay, nice hotel !” I stayed at the Westin Michigan 3 nights last October and I loved it. Friendly staff, beaufitul lobby, nice room, everything was perfect for me. Right in front of John Hancock Tower, near shopping area, subway station, .... If I return to Chicago, I will book again at the Westin, for sure.

“Poor Experience” My experience fell far short of what would be expected of the 4-star Marriott hotel that this claims to be. The room was clean and the bathroom was nice. But the furniture and room were seriously tattered: the wooden window sill was greatly stripped (see photo) and the desk chair was ripped and dirty (see photos). Wouldn't housekeeping and maintenance notice these things and cure the problems? Also, the heater in my original room was broken and necessitated three visits from maintenance. Despite moving rooms, even in the new room the heater turned on and off abruptly and quite loudly, making sleep extremely difficult. Frankly, my stay here undermined my confidence in Marriott, and I was happy to check out.

“Great Times Sq. Location, Value Priced, Comfortable and Clean” I booked here for a last minute business trip through bookit.com. Base rate was $135/night plus taxes and fees for a total of $165/night. For a last minute trip and a Times Square location, that's a good deal if the hotel is decent, and it was.
The room was small and efficient as expected for this price. It was a bit dark which made it seem smaller. The bed and pillows were comfortable. The steam heat was pumping out and needed to be turned down, but it's better than freezing! The closet was small, but there was plenty of bureau space. 32" lcd tv worked well, the iHome iPod clock player was a bonus, individual led reading lamps on the head board was a nice touch and the work desk had power in easy reach.
There was no view from the rooms unless you were right in the front or right in the rear. The benefit was that it was quiet until the bells on the church next door started to ring at 7:45am.
WiFi access was free and there was complimentary breakfast each morning. I didn't partake in the breakfast due to schedule, but I checked it out and they had a good spread of fruits, yogurt, muffins, waffles, etc (continental plus) that would satisfy most people's quick breakfast requirements.
If you aren't going to spend a lot of time in your room lounging, then this would be a great hotel to consider.

“Excellente surprise dans l’un des quartiers les plus agréables de New York” J’ai séjourné dans cet hôtel du 14 au 14 aout 2011, dans le cadre d’une visite amicale à New-York. Situé dans le quartier East Midtown (truffé d’hôtels), il est très facile d’accès depuis JFK par la ligne E du métro, dont la station Lexington&41st est située à quelques pas.
Etant à l’époque « simple » membre Gold du programme de fidélité SPG, j’ai été agréablement surpris par un double surclassement à l’arrivée, d’autant plus surprenant que la réservation a été effectuée dans un tarif ultra-promotionnel de dernière minute ($200 la nuit sur le site SPG). Le check-in fut très simple.
Le lobby de l’hôtel, de taille restreinte, est très bien décoré, de façon très « branchée ».
La chambre, elle, malgré le double surclassement, minuscule. En revanche, elle reste très fonctionnelle, et décorée avec beaucoup de gout et un design à la pointe, avec de petites touches très appréciables telles les lumières dissimulées un peu partout de façon très habile, mais dans le respect de la tradition du bâtiment. Le carrelage façon Métro parisien dans la salle de bains est très original. Le système de portes coulissantes également. Le lit est très confortable.
Le petit-déjeuner (non compris dans mon tarif) est d’excellente qualité mais cher, et peut faire office de Brunch sans aucun souci.

“Hit and miss stays here” This is a tough one. I have stayed here four times and three of the four have been great. The location is great and very easy to catch the subway on 53rd street. The lobby bar is good and gets very busy with a DJ that plays in the evenings. There is also another bar located at the hotel that is also fun and worth a try.
Can't say much of the restaurant because I haven't tried it. Rooms are good, comfortable and reasonably priced. So why the average rating?
Well on my last stay, we were getting ready for bed after a night out, and to our surprise we found a cockroach...What a disappointment. I did complain and the manager did move us to a suite, but regardless it is very hard to look past this type of incident.
I like this place but they need to be proactive and avoid these types of incidents at all costs.

“Great location and nice rooms but be aware of nearby construction” Location is close to many things and to subway stops. Just a few blocks to Central Park. Construction is going on nearby. Beginning at midnight on a Friday night in February there was heavy work going on. The front desk said they weren't aware it was going to happen that night and offered to move me if it was continuing the next night. They said they would call me to let me know the status of the construction, but they never did and would not answer calls to the front desk. Luckily there was no more construction during the weekend.

“Great location, great price” This HoJo Inn is the true definition of value. It is an older hotel - the motel of family vacations when I was a child - complete with free parking (amazing in Chicago) and free WiFi. The staff is small but incredibly helpful, the rooms are basic & a bit worn but very clean. A king suite is one large room with a couch, desk, mini fridge, microwave & sink. The prices are far lower than other hotels in the area. There are two El lines within a short walk (brown is one block, red is 4 blocks). The little on-site coffee shop is a gem - wonderful breakfast & VERY friendly, fast service - even on a Sunday AM when local restaurants had long lines. We were very pleasantly surprised and will return.

“Rude” I made a request via the web page for a flyer, brochure on the hotel, as they have that option available.
 I was just about to make a 7 day booking, but not now.
 A day later I received a rude email stating "we cannot do anything without a credit card.
 I wish I had taken note of the other negative comments about the staff.
 A shame really, as it sounded like a good hotel and I am sure this is not the first time it happened.
 With customer service like this, I will be looking elsewhere.

“Nice stay at Times Square” My kids loved the, we had an spetacular view on the 31st floor. The Hotel has a small microwave and small refrigerator inside the rooms, a separate living room with a very comfortable sofa bed. The queen bed we had was very comfortable and the bathroom was adequate. The staff was very kind.

“A Bad Acid Trip” The decorators must have been on a bad acid trip when they did the interior design of this hotel. It is trendy to the point of bizarre. I suppose the hotel appeals to a certain demographic---and I am not it. 
As reported in other reviews, the rooms are tiny. You would not want to be in the room with anyone else, unless you are extremely intimate with them. 
My non-smoking room smelled of smoke. I did not have much interaction with the staff, but they seemed pleasant to the extent I did interact with them. 
I did not eat at the hotel, or go to the bar. I was there on business and was out of the hotel early in the morning and back late in the evening. 
Overall, a mediocre, though not horrible experience.

“Good Value in a Great Neighborhood” I first visited the Excelsior in October 2004 on vacation with my husband and my fourth grader, and have since been back twice on my own -- once to visit family and once on business. The most recent visit was in mid-January 2005. The Excelsior continues to impress with its terrific location away from Mid-town, the consistency of its amenities, and its astonishingly reasonable rates. On my last visit, the valets were particularly pleasant about stored luggage, even storing the luggage of a relative who had not stayed at the hotel. There are several places to grab great coffee and a quick breakfast in the neighborhood (breakfast at the hotel is expensive), excellent access to bus and taxi service and good access to subways, and Central Park is a mere block away. The rooms are not glamorous but are nice -- an acceptable size for NY with appropriate business amenities. The rates have been consistently terrific for NYC. If I must stay in midtown I stay at the Michelangelo; if I can avoid midtown, the Excelsior is my choice every time.

“Avoid avoid avoid, unless you are on a budget” Honestly, this hotel should get a 1 star at best. I've given it a 2 as if you are on a budget, business or personal, and you will be out of the room a lot then maybe this hotel is ok. 
However, be warned. I stayed here as the usual hotels I book were full and I wanted mid town. However I moved 1 day into a 4 day stay. Dreadful. The only good thing about this hotel is the location and the price. I am an honest reviewer, see my other reviews. Here goes:
The lobby is ok, as the bar. The staff were generally rude - the receptionist was awful, the bell man too. The operator was also dreadful. Didn't care much and didn't do as they said. The lifts, lighting and hallways are 40 years old, with no decoration. They are way way out of date. The carpets stained. 
The rooms - I'm honestly not sure where to begin. It smelled old and musty; carpets stained: don't touch the window unless you want to lose a finger, I'm serious; the windows are tiny, no light; the next curtains, well, if they were washed before 1950 I would be shocked; the safe didn't work; the bath was blocked; the bathroom, well that is worse than a 2 star motel. I've stayed in plenty of 2 star motels and they beat this hotel by some way. The maintenance is just shocking, don't get ice - you will see a room that was last cleaned with Kennedy was president. 
To be fair there is wifi and a tv, with few channels. There is a gym, but not one most people are used to. I will not, ever, go back to this hotel. I would rather sleep in my car. Honestly. 
I'm no snob, I review on value for money. This hotel is not value for money.

“Still good!” Have stayed here several times, and it is still good! Big rooms, great sleeping comfort, very nice to have the turndown service and the complimentary hot beverage served at your door in the morning. Cleaning is good, nice and big towels. It is a big hotel but still quiet. The bar is nice, the bartender is very good. Love the free shuttle service to restaurants in the evening. They bring you and pick you up - lovely!! Will return!

“Artsy Fartsy” Really should have read the reviews before booking this hotel. pics online look great. I reserved a 2 doubles with kitchen. What did I get? When we arrived you walk into a pretty cool looking lobby with lots of art and I thought it was cool and kindy spooky that Sid Vicious had killed his girlfriend in room 100. who knew? We were in room 126.
Anyways, Jason at the desk tells me there is a little problem. They overbooked the hotel and now they only have 1 queen room available. I don't mind sleeping with my girlfriends but this was just not good. We got to the room and the bed was at least 6 inches off the floor, no amenities, no pictures on the walls and the bellmen says " you have 28 channels on your tv" wippee. Look out the window to barbwire. I didn't feel safe at all. Called housekeeping for extra pillows got them 1 hr later.The best part is, there's a club on the first floor and at 6am all you hear is techno music. I'd like to sleep at that time. Oh, they did offer 15% because they overbooked the rooms. Still, not worth $300+ dollars. Would I stay again. No

“Great location, front desk is challenging, rooms showing their age” This hotel (and it's neighbor, the Residence Inn) opened as a brand new, newly-built hotel about 4 years ago in 2008. First, the rooms are good, but they're showing their age with constant, tourist use. The light colors of the in-room interiors and carpeting and hallways are starting to look dingy. The powers that be did an unfortunate thing a few months ago and got rid of the larger king suites (end rooms) and switched them to doubles only. Who's bright idea was that? So, on this visit, I was in the smaller king suite. it was OK. The bed linens were clean; the desk seems cramped; the sitting area was acceptable if not tight; the refrigerator worked; the bathroom was small, cramped, but clean; and I didn't bother with the coffee maker. On my first night, I had thread-bare towels. When the room was serviced the following day, I got a few better, thicker, towels. Housekeeping was fine on this visit. I never eat the breakfast with so many other options in the area. The front desk attendants must have gone to the College of I Can't Help You. Every question I have for them, they can't seem to answer. So, I stopped asking a long time ago. Head next door to the Residence Inn if you need information. (The check-in counters for both hotels are on the same floor.) Check-in at this hotel makes my blood pressure rise. The way the curved desk is designed doesn't help define a clear line for "who's next?". As I'm waiting to be served, someone comes out of no where and approaches the desk. Does the desk attendant ever say: "I'm sorry, but I'm helping another guest and there is a line." No. He or she stops what she's doing to help this person who clearly has no consideration for anyone else. The desk attendants perpetuate this problem by not correcting the offending guest. This problem is constant and endemic at this Springhill Suites and I try (but never succeed) in keeping my mouth shut about it. It has happened EVERY time I've checked in as the front desk is usually undermanned. When there are 2 or 3 people waiting, there's one attendant. When the line swells to 5 or 6 people (or more) all of sudden, two or three attendants come chatting and laughing out of the back. I find this consistent and repeated behavior completely offensive. Whoever supervises the front desk (along with the General Manager) should be replaced or returned to Hotel Management School (assuming they've ever attended it or know what it is). Internet was complimentary (and I don't know if that's because I'm a Marriott elite member), but you'll pay extra if you want faster Internet. The basic speed was fine for me. The location of the hotel is excellent: within walking distance of several restaurants and shopping along Michigan Ave. Parking as with any downtown hotel is pricey, but I can live with that. I can't abide with poor management, however.

“small rooms with a political agenda” I have never stayed at a hotel in the USA where the politics of the franchisee controls what the hotel offers before i stayed here. 
The rooms are small and very dark as the windows are two feet from the building next door so it looks like midnight at noon in most of the rooms. The cable offerings are scant, and FoxNews network is not offered. When i asked why, i was told that it is because it is not fair to Muslims, and the internet access code was Sept 11 per the bearded ( and scary) front desk clerk. 
the hotel is clean but i can see why the rates are consistently lower than any others in a very high-rent part of Park Ave. And the Clarion on 23rd, which may not be run by the same franchisee, seems to never have Fox New network "working" when it supposedly is offered. 
and you have to be a very agile person to reach the air conditioner located at the top of the window standing on the bed. I'm over 6' tall and could barely reach the controls, and in a very dangerous reach where i'd go through the window if I slipped. 
I won't be coming back here again.

“Overcharged by $2,000” Two weeks ago I reviewed this hotel, and gave it a very good rating. Then I got my credit-card statement in the mail. It had two charges from the hotel totaling more that $2,000 for days I did not stay or reserve. Even stranger, I only used my card to reserve the room, and we paid for the room using my wife's card because she was on business. Her credit-card bill had the correct charge. As a result of this huge billing error I deleted my review and I am now replacing it with this one.
 I hope this was the work of a rogue employee, and that the hotel has corrected the problem. Still, no one wants a huge undeserved bill.

“No, no, no - overrated, overpriced, over.” The best, and the only, good thing with this hotel is the location. Close to the vibrating nightlife, good selection of restaurants and near shopping. 6 months earlier we had visited the hotel’s rooftop bar on a sunny afternoon drinking a ridiculously expensive bottle of champagne in plastic glasses. Somehow we then got a feeling for the place and decided to stay here for our next trip. The reviews we read where mainly positive. But we were misled. Starting from the top of the hotel, this is an area solely for the entertainment of non resident hotel guests. The “hip” crowds have since long moved onto another new nightspot, and we can understand why. The view might be great, but the décor is simple, cheap and run down. The music is disappointing and the overpriced cocktails are not well done. There is a rooftop pool, which was open but covered by a boring plastic shelter. Even when it’s removed it’s not going to look by far as attractive or inviting as the rooftop pool you can see across the street at the Soho House. Where do you sunbath? The terrace offers no other benefits for the hotel guest but the fact you will not have to queue to get into the nightclub. However as hotel guest there are no other privileges, and to our surprise we could not even get a table to sit at unless we opted to pay the minimum bottle charge. Nevertheless, there are plenty of better clubs to visit in the neighbourhood where the money spent will be much more worth it. 
Moving down to the accommodation. We stayed in a grand deluxe room which is on a higher floor and has a fantastic view of the Hudson river. The room is small and the decor is simple. We had booked several months in advance asking for a silent room but were put right next to the elevator and we were disturbed by screaming kids in the morning and late night party people in the corridor. We immediately complained to the duty manager but it was left unsolved. Yes they offered us a smaller room with a different view, obviously with no compensation, but on the contrary at same price. A pretty smart try. 
The breakfast is a JOKE! Fortunately it was included in the price because I would never have paid for it. A buffet consisting of some dry croissants, yogurt, fresh fruit (which was the only bonus), and regular coffee or tea. As you sit down the waiter gives you a menu to order from, but all this is not included, which, of course, the waiter do not let you know. So I reckon a few peoples have been royally ripped off here.
The over all service is really poor. There is no charm. And the hotel is starting to become rundown. Don’t stay here there are better hotels like 60 Thompson or the Mercer. We stayed 2 of our planned 6 nights here. We regret staying so long.

“Excellente situation et confort standard” Idéalement situé, chambres bien insonorisées mais il faut aimer les hotels immenses et seulement deux ascenseurs.
Piscine sur le toit.
Pas l'hotel le plus charmant de NYC mais un point de chute intéressant pour le prix (150 euros).

“Upscale Lobby & Location - Downscale rooms.” I'm not at all sure you get what you pay for here. The location is literally on the airport rail system. The lobby and restaurants are really nice. But what a disappointment when you get to your 2star room

“Impressed” I read some reviews about certain issues with the hotel, but I found none of them during my stay in August at the Soho Grand. 
The room was clean and a good size for a NYC hotel room (I had a deluxe king); the hotel is nice with great bars and restaurants. We had a few drinks at the "Yard", which is an outside bar on the first floor and more of a lounge really. 
Small details about the rooms - they were clean, the WIFI was free and had great signal; not sure how much TV you'd want to watch while being in NYC but they offer many more channels than your regular hotel. The AC in the room worked really well; we had to turn it off all the time b/c it was so cold.
I saw reviews about the staff being snobby (which I wouldn't have minded taken the area I'm in); but everyone was friendly. The valet guys were helpful and nice, the front desk was friendly enough and even gave me a late check out on a Saturday. 
Overall, very happy with our stay. It's a great hotel in Soho, and in a great location. I paid about $350 per night so you expect a great stay for that money, but I'm happy to say I was indeed pleased with the Grand.

“古いタウンハウスでのユニークな体験” バウリーストリート近くにあるラファエットハウスは住宅が軒を連ねるストリートにある古いタウンハウスです。薄暗い館内は、ビクトリア朝の家具やインテリアでまるで誰かのお宅に泊まっているような印象です。私は上の階のゲストルームに宿泊し、マンハッタンのスタンダードからするとかなり広々としていました。ベッドはクイーンサイズでガス式暖炉、ソファー、バスタブつきのバスルーム、それにキチネットまでついていました。家具や設備はおそらく19世紀～20世紀初頭のもので、かなり古いです。さらに、ゲストルームからアクセスできる広々としたテラスには木のベンチとテーブルも備えつけられてありました。ラファエットハウスハは、こういったビクトリア朝の古い部屋が好きな方や余計なサービスなど不要で誰かの部屋に泊めてもらった気分で一人で過ごしたい方には喜ばれるかと思いますが、サービスや施設面は
充実していません。ちなみに私が宿泊した時には、スタッフは1名のみでした。また、一般のホテルのようにコンシェルジュやポーター、レセプションは無く、ホテル内にバー、レストラン、ランドリーサービスもありません。食事は隣のBowery Bar & Grillからオーダー可能でデリバリーサービスがあります。到着した際、ベルが故障していたようで鳴らしてもしばらく誰も対応に来ませんでした。たまたま玄関前を通りかかった唯一従業員が私の姿に気づき、開けて案内してくれましたが、雪の中、外で待ち続けるはめになるところでした。
他のレビューアーが知人にここをすすめた際、宿泊した知人が幽霊を見たと書いてあったのが気がかりだったせいかもしれませんが、寝床に着く際、天井からかすかな音がするのが聞こえ、落ち着けず、結局その夜は友人宅に泊めてもらいました。ウエブサイトにある電話番号に電話し、担当者に不審な音がして昨夜眠れなかったので払い戻しをしてもらえないかと相談しましたが、相手の方は丁寧な対応でこちらの気持ちを汲み取ってくれながらも、そういった理由で払い戻しができないと返答しました。この電話番号に出て対応するスタッフはホテルに常駐しておらず、別の所で運営をしていました。
最後に、たった一人のホテルスタッフと打ち解けて色々と話していると、唐突に「ここに幽霊が出るのを知っているか？」と尋ねてきました。このスタッフも消したはずの暖炉の火が部屋に戻るとついていたなど他にも不思議な現象を目の当りにしているそうです。
ホテルに期待するサービスが一切なしといった観点から、価格は高いといえるでしょう。

“Great place to stay!” Great location just off Times Square, close to everything. Grey Line bus tours are close by - so are all the theaters. Staff was fantastic, friendly and professional. The affiliated restaurant was also really terrific. Will stay here again!!

“A converted Hampton Inn with some problems” This Hampton Inn was converted from another hotel. Actually, there is a similar, attached building which is a Days Inn and the property has the feel of a Days Inn more than a higher caliber Hampton Inn. From the outside you can see the old sliding glass doors and inside the rooms you'll realize not everything was renovated to make the hotel look like a Hampton Inn. Still, in the rooms you will definitely find all of the Hampton style furniture and decorations you would expect.
When I checked in I wondered if I had made a mistake booking this hotel. A couple was changing rooms because their room smelled bad. Everything seemed as good as they could have been in my converted Hampton Inn hotel room until I saw a cockroach running across the floor. I looked under the bed skirt and found a nasty old sock that wasn't removed by housekeeping. Unfortunately I was already settled into my room so I didn't bother asking to be moved plus I didn't want to get into a worse situation. I did not sleep well with the thought of a cockroach running around the beds.
If you don't mind cockroaches, the beds were comfortable and the furniture is nice in the room. The bathroom was attractive with marble although I'm guessing the bathtub was old because it didn't drain well.
If you stay at Hampton Inns and expect a certain level of quality this hotel is definitely not for you. The other Hampton in the area is the real thing although it will likely be more expensive.

“Loud noise, tiny space” Some of the tiniest rooms you've ever seen in a New York hotel. You may find this funny, or charming, or insulting in the rip-off sort of way (considering the price), or claustrophia-inducing. Some of the rooms do have nice showers (not bathtubs) with marble and open glass. No lobby to speak of. When you come in, you feel like you're coming in to a high priced hostel. Everything is cramped all through the place. They do charge quite a bit for amenities. The guys at reception, particularly the manager, clearly did not graduate from the School of Basic Customer Service. That's a shame when you pay this much. This boutique chain has it's ups and downs. Most of the them offer free capuccino, etc in the lobby. That's okay-- but at the Mansfield, the milk is sour and about a week old. Also, apparently in many rooms at Hotel 41, they allow guests to bring cats. That's okay-- but then if you're going to rent it out to a non-cat-owner who has cat allergies-- it's a good idea to ask them about it first-- especially when the room reeks of cat urine (which was our case). We were in a room on second floor facing the street, and right before curtain call for the theatre every night, a group of percussion street musicians set up shop for two hours-- right below our room. Cat urine and two hours of banging percussion-- come on.

“AMAZING HOTEL IN NYC!!” Our stay with our family here was amazing. From Mary at the Diamond check in area all the way down it is run top notch. We loved the location and our room. The service is great. Any request was addressed instantly and completed. We will definitely be staying here again and can't wait to go back!

“Large Rooms, but it ends there” Let me first say we never stay in Midtown, but we had 2 Broadway shows in 1 weekend, so decided to stay in touristville and the London had nice reviews on T.A.
The rooms at the The London are very spacious, almost empty, no artwork or prints on the wall, very cold and sparse. 
Terrible check in. Young girl checked us in, telling us our room was not available at 4 pm, because the current guests have not checked out as of yet. ?????? 
Well then, it's a 500 room hotel, find us another room. Duh! She was a dope. No customer service or smile or kindness and no upgrade. Made us stand there while she called a someone.
No HD tv's in the room, and the wireless had to be hooked up by calling an outside I.T. tech that was not located in the hotel. More headaches. 
Last thing, I am a New Yorker and have never spent $21. on a cocktail in my life. Even in Paris or London. Can you say RIP OFF? Bar is filled with guests in sweat shirts and hoodies, while other people are having high tea. Makes no sense. How about a dress code? Gordon Ramsey would be appalled.
If you are looking for a $349. bargain price and close proximity to the theatres and midtown, then this is a fine choice, if you like mediocrity. 
I suggest you stay at the Trump Soho and take a cab to your show, same price and a fabulous hotel who appreciates their guests.
PS. the lights on the desk did not work either. Such a shame. The Rhiga Royal was a swanky place.

“I wonder what they do when it's really crowded...” Was here for three nights on business (with a little bit of sightseeing thrown in).
Renovation is really noisy and has made the lobby/reception area tiny. They appear to serve a LOT of foreign tour groups as there would regularly be groups of non-English tourists blocking the doors, halls and elevators, getting on/off coaches, not knowing how to use the elevators, etc. Having said that, I checked in early evening (apparently between tour groups) and checkin was very easy. Same with checking out as I was checking out at the crack of dawn in order to catch a morning flight out of Newark. Reception was uniformly friendly, baggage services was uniformly surly and my questions for both were answered quickly and appropriately.
For a single business traveler, the hotel is ideal. I was in room 1150 (which I was informed was a handicap room, although when I described the room to my colleague, she said it sounded just like her non-handicap room). #1150 is on the south side of the hotel looking down 8th Ave, up high enough that traffic noise wasn't bad and I heard none of the renovation noise in the lobby. The room was small but efficiently laid out with enough desk and cupboard space for me to work. Large flat-panel TV was a nice addition although selection of satellite channels appears to depend on where you are in the hotel (as my colleague claimed she received different channels than I did). The footprint of the hotel is a classic E shape which means about a 1/3 of the rooms are going to have a view of another room...nothing to be done except ask for a room on the outside.
Personally, I like the renovation. Linens were decent, furnishing were basic, but fine. As a solo traveler, I hadn't noticed the problem with the bathroom door cited by previous poster and the room would be tight for a couple. Bed was firm (but not hard) and I had REALLY nice chocolate cupcake waiting in my room. Have no idea why as my colleague didn't get one.
Since others have commented on the elevators, I'll mention that I only had to wait once when the doors opened to a full elevator. The elevator system is one of those where you punch your floor into a panel and then you're instructed which elevator to take (no panel of buttons inside the car, only a panel telling you on which floors the car is stopping). I've seen these in a number of hotels, but this is the first one I've seen actually work well (as you really need to have a bank of elevators to make the system work efficiently).
I really liked what they did with the decor in the halls, painting NY street scenes in various places (including on room doors). It really broke up the monotomy of long hallways and made the space much less claustrophobic (although the hallways and walls are fairly dark).
The location is ideal for seeing shows and for getting around. Times Square is two blocks away and the 42nd/Port Authority stop of the ACE lines was one block away. Getting to/from Newark airport on the train was a piece of cake (although I did get a little lost transferring between NJ Transit and subway at Penn Station). Tons of restuarants around (including a Junior's restaurant/bakery on 45th).

“Buena relación calidad-precio para ser Nueva York” Elegimos este hotel porque era de lo más barato que encontramos en Nueva York que tuviera baño en la habitación y que no fuera habitación compartida. Unos 70 euros la noche reservando con antelación en la web del hotel. 
Habia leido críticas que decian que habia chinches y que era muy sucio, pero pude comprobar que ni habia chinches, y que afortunadamente, era muy limpio. Limpiaban la habitación y el baño cada dia. La habitación era pequeña, pero tenia una buena cama y un baño, aunque pequeño, bastante funcional. 
Pasamos un poco de frio, ya que la calefacción a veces no funcionaba. Luego nos explicaron en la recepción, que lo de la calefacción, no era cosa del hotel, sino que la ciudad de Nueva York, corta o pone la calefacción dependiendo del frio que haga. 
El desayuno no estaba incluido y no puedo opinar que tal era porque habia muchas panaderias cerca donde se desayunaba muy bien. Lo que habia en el hotel era café gratis. 
La wifi es gratis, pero tienes que bajar al saloncito de la televisión, ya que no llega hasta las habitaciones.
Está bastante lejos del centro, por lo que es necesario coger el metro, que está a unos 5 minutos andando. 
En definitiva, un buen hotel para dormir.

“You Get What You Pay For” Great location, no doubt about it...but if your budget allows you to pay a little more stay somewhere else. The hotel's rooms are way outdated, not the cleanest and the bathroom is the worst aspect of it. Right out of the 1930's, not much has been done to spruce it up. I'm all for art deco decor and all, and can appreciate the beauty in something that's antique but this hotel is just not well maintained. It's not about it being an old hotel, it's just that the property has basically been left to age, and it hasn't aged well. The restaurant on premise is just average and they don;t even accept credit cards! The hallways have the smell of people bringing in fast food into their rooms. I stayed here once because I waited until the last minute to book a hotel on a business trip I had to take, but in the future when I'm in NYC, I will stay anywhere but at the Edison.

“Mostly good but one HUGE problem” Got a great rate and was excited to try this Irish property. Arrival went smoothly, Room was small, but typical by New York standards. Bed was very comfortable and desk large enough to do work. Bathroom was larger than expected. Both room and bathroom were exceptionally clean. Pub was nice for a quick drink at night and breakfast was well-prepared. Up until the morning of my departure I was very impressed. Then it happened....NO HOT WATER for a shower on my departure day. It worked fine the day before. I spent so much time trying different solutions to get hot water to my room (leaving spigot on, leaving sink on, flushing toilet, trying different handle positions, etc.) that I had to leave in a rush to catch my flight.
