Because of the line to check out, I had no time to complain to the front desk, I just left. They will hear about it in a letter. I travel quite a bit for business and have never had this happen before.

“Such a good NYC hotel that I am concerned too many travelers will choose it” What a wonderful boutique hotel find in NYC's Murray Hill neighborhood! I stay here every other month or so for both business and visits with my college-aged children. I have been reluctant to write a review because I find it to be such a well-kept secret. Pros: great neighborhood esp for Indian food, area is less busy than Times Square/Grand Central midtown hotel locations, fantastic views of the Chrysler building and the East River from upper floor suites, large rooms with kitchens, helpful staff, two blocks from subway stop. Cons: can get pricey for upgraded rooms esp during busy tourist seasons, a bit of a walk to Grand Central.

“Generally one of the better hotels in NYC” On the whole, Hotel Mela is a good bet if you want something decent in NYC. Having travelled to NYC every year for the last five years and stayed at different hotels, I must say Hotel Mela does provide good value for money.
1. Location, Location, Location
The best feature of this hotel is its location. It's right in the centre of all the action, Times Square. You will have no issue with food, transportation or shopping.
2. Free Wifi
The whole place has complimentary Wifi service (from the hotel lobby to my deluxe King room). This is great! 
3. The toiletries are magnificent.
The H2O toiletries are great. They smell really great. Much better than what some of the hotels are providing.
4. Room
The room is big! That's partly because I've got a corner room and it's a deluxe King. Enough space for my 4 huge luggages. And they do have a very comfy bed. The only complaint is that the service can be quite inconsistent (if I compare my experience with the other reviewers). I did not get any turn down service at ALL! No chocolates or what so ever. Hmmm.... have they shortchanged me?
5. My ONLY Complaint
I've requested for an early check-in because I had endured a 25-hour flight and arrived in NYC at 6am in the morning. Instead of trying to find me a room at the earliest possible time, the front desk staff insisted that I come back again at 3pm (the official check-in time). They did not even offer to call us if the room became available. The only helpful thing they did was to allow me to leave my luggages at the hotel while my partner and I roamed NYC in zombie-like state. No good for a first impression.

“Older Property, Friendly Staff” This is an OLD Hilton property that is in need of updating. The rooms were comfortable and clean but nothing special. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The airport shuttle is prompt and runs every 15 minutes.

“We had drinks and Tapas by the bar and had to return 3 bottles of champ.” We sat for drinks and we had to turn back 3 bottles of champagne back. That was not the issue the issue was the way it was handled by the sommelier which felt insulted even though he knew that his product was not but to snuff.

“Great Place” As a present to us for our engagement party, we were given a night at the W union square. I dont know how much the room I had would have cost but I can guess more than $500.00 a night. It was a great room with comfortable bed, nice big heavy drapes to keep the room dark, big bathroom with Bliss products, and three windows for a 180 degree view of the city. It was a great place, super quiet and very comfortable.
We werent there for long and so i didnt have any interaction with the staff. Although, we had planned to have one last drink at the bar before bed but when we got in there realized that it was a real scene and super crowded. We decided to forget it after waiting at the bar for about 10 minutes with no service.
That we had that experience at the bar didnt affect my appreciation of the beautiful hotel, great location and comfortable room. It was a great hotel.

“Good, If you have business at McCormick Place” Spent two nights here for a trade show. The upside first, hotel is clean, my room offered a pretty nice view of Lake Michigan, the bed was quite comfy, the staff was nice, and it is attached to the convention center.
The downside, there is nothing to do around this hotel and it is about a 10 to 20 minute cab ride from downtown depending on time of day, the hotel is in a tough neighborhood, if fact I advise people do not go outside at night except to get a cab. There is no need to go outside between the convention center and the hotel since they are attached and well secured. I was also pretty surprised to see a $9.95 per day fee to use the internet. In 2008 you would think this would be included in the room rate especially at $275.00 per night.

“Great location, nice room” I stayed here for a long weekend in NY. I had purchased the room on JetSetter on special and I was totally satisfied with my experience. The hotel is four blcoks from Grand Central. The decor is lovely, and the nightly free wine happy hours are great. The room was perfect. I really liked the decor. the only hard part was that the bathroom is very small. But other than the size, it had all encessary ammenities. I will return to this hotel.

“You will be sorry if you stay here!” I stayed here overnight day before yesterday. Was in Chicago in business and hotel was booked by agent. This is probably the worst property that I've seen in a long time. Carpet is dirty with flee or some sort of bugs -still scratching. Bathroom with 8 layers of paint and dirt. Bed is rock hard piece of foam. This place is scary and dirty. I actually check out and moved to the airport Hilton the second night after paying for the Congress plaza for 2 nights. I put do not disturb sign basically because I didn't want anyon to touch or move anything but room service came in and made the bed again and put the nasty pillows back on the bed. I would not touch anything here without wearing gloves.

“Small and Dirty Rooms” The Milford Plaza (which apparently is changing its name to "The Milford") is undergoing extensive work in its lobby and on certain floors. However, it does not appear to be upgrading the look of the hotel, but perhaps making the hotel meet fire and building code.
The first room we had on the 22nd floor was very small with a window unit hanging out of the window, right over the built-in AC unit that apparently no longer works. When asked if we wanted one bed or two, we thought they meant one king bed or two doubles -- when we said two beds, we got two twins. This isn't Europe, so two doubles is a pretty standard expectation for US hotels.
The room and bathroom was very small, with not much space for luggage. It was literally the size of a typical cruise ship stateroom (only without the closet space). The bathroom had been cleaned very quickly, with a dark ball of toilet paper left under the back of the toilet and perhaps half a dozen long black hairs around the toilet, tub, and sink. Fixtures leaked and the room "matched" the rest of the hotel--generally run down. Wires were in conduits throughout the hotel, showing where the hotel had updated to add telephone/cable/fire, but didn't bother actually pulling it through the ceiling and drywall.
It was apparent the hotel had tried to update its look, but it did so with some stick-on wallpaper and some thin carpet in the hallways that wasn't installed correctly, so it buckled up. There was constant hammering in another room that appeared to be directly behind our room. Since we had an early AM flight, we needed rest in the afternoon, so we asked the front desk for another room.
We were put into another room on the 9th floor. This room didn't have a window unit, but the built-in AC was very loud and took several hours to cool the 12x12 foot room. The bathroom was a little cleaner, but hair was still around. The beds in both rooms were very "well used" and on shaky foundations whereby if you sit on the edge, the mattress would flip upward. This may not be the case in rooms with a larger bed, but as stated earlier, we anticipated having two double beds, not two singles when we request a room with "two beds" (as they were careful not to indicate that "two beds" means "two twin beds").
Avoid this hotel. We would rather have stayed further from Times Square in an updated hotel.

“modern!” Pros: excellent location in the heart of tribeca, the room is big and the beds are very comfortable, smyth uses khiel's amenities which i love. absolutely love the decor and design. the subway access was across the street. the front desk staff and concierge was very helpful, they're recommendations were excellent. 
Cons: the shower glass door is frosted, so its a little uncomfortable to shower when you are traveling with friends.

“Just Right for New York City!” We stayed one night at this well-rounded hotel and it was surely worth it! Our room had a wonderful view over the New York city streets and made you know that you are in the heart of New York City. The location was amazing with steps away from all of the attractions in Times Square, and the subway is on the same block! All the amentities were prime!

“Just Great!” This hotel is just great. I was amazed each time I went in. Stuff was really friendly and check-in was really fast. Ans there is one thing in NY that really counts; location! The hotel is located perfectly. I will never ever stay somewhere else in New York.

“Hotel needs a service makeover” Plat PCR Member- (not Ambassdor) booked a deluxe corner view room online with PCR Points for the last night of our trip in NY. This room has 2 walls of windows, and was supposed to be secured with our confirmation number booked weeks earlier---- not so. We arrived late in the day 5pm (on a Friday), the lines were long at check in (only 3 people working- very understaffed), and they told me my room was not ready. I was baffled at how our room could not be ready at 5pm, I would understand at 12 noon, maybe even 3pm, but 5pm come on! Then the desk clerk tells me that she might have a "sky view room" that is an upgrade in an hour or she can put us on the 5th floor and give us breakfast vouchers. I ask how long for the higher floor room, and she types on her keyboard again, and says maybe 45 mins. I ask her to check on the higher room, she picks up the phone, and a "conversation" lasts 5 seconds, and says its ready now. We go to our room on the 27 floor, and it sure didn't feel like an upgrade when we looked out our 1 wall of windows and see the corner rooms, that I booked weeks ahead for a romantic trip our last night in NYC. At this point I already felt lied to by the desk clerk b/c they sold our room, and tried to push us to a 5th floor room. 
In the room it was clean, and and nicely appointed. The AC unit was slow to cool the room, so we had to open the window. Our bags were brought up 20 minutes after check in. I called the front desk to ask about internet, and they said it was paid only unless you are an Ambassador club member. PCR platinum status at this hotel means nothing, no club lounge, no breakfast, no internet included ($12.95 per day). The woman on the phone asked about the amenity basket, I told her we didn't get one, they said they would send it up to the room. 10 minutes later, we get a call from a porter to make sure we were there for this "basket". When we returned to the room 3 hours later after dinner, our amenity "basket" was there.......5 small apples on a plate with a bottle of Fiji water (picture included). There was such a big deal made of this, once it arrived, I wish I never saw it.......pretty embarrassing in my opinion. 
This was only a one night stay before going to the airport, so the next moring, my wife and I went to make a cup of the instant coffee in the room, there was only 1 regular coffee "pod" provided (the rest was decaf or tea), and the machine didn't work. I went down to the front desk, and told them about the coffee machine not working...........I told her we are checking out and don't have time for a new machine, and I asked for a comp. She provided a $20 comp to the downstairs cafe. 
My main problem with this hotel is they are painting themselves, and pricing themselves at a 4 star hotel. The service is no where near that, the staff is young and more concerned with their cell phones and personal conversations than opening doors, and thinking of the "next step" to make your stay memorable in a positive way (like offering a comp), or having your bill ready at check out when you tell them, or your car ready at the valet, after you've told them you'll be right down to get it. Also lacking are the quality of the towels in the bathroom, it was a nice bathroom (rainshower only), but the towels were nicer at the Crowne Plaza. The bed was very comfortable. I use IHC hotels for work, and if this is their flagship in NY - it might be time to switch. This is far from the Ritz or Fairmont brands I've stayed at, that I think it's trying to compete with.

“Stay here!” I've stayed in many hotels in New York city and this was one of my favorites so far. The moment we stepped out of the car we in good hands as Deron greeted us and made us feel welcome and important. He arranged an escort to the front desk and then returned to give us an overview of the hotel. He also provided us with some some very appreciated drink coupons! Check in went smoothly and we were given a complimentary upgrade without asking as I see others have also. This is a very highly rated quality. Yesliere at the front desk was pleasant and the call to our room after we settled in to be sure we were happy with the accommodations was a very nice touch. Ryan brought us along with our bags to our room and he was very friendly and provided us with a lot of hotel information. He also gave us a tour of our room. This gave us an immediate favorable impression. Our room was larger than I expected and the bed was very comfortable. The shower was a good size and the water pressure was good. The hotel decor is interesting and the living room area was always busy with people hanging out. We ordered room service for dinner. The food was of very good quality and the delivery person Huran was nice. He gave us a card with his name to call him when we were done with the table. Another nice personal touch. We had drinks in the Oasis bar at night and they were delicious. We enjoyed breakfast in the restaurant as well. We were helped with our luggage and car return by the warm and friendly Troy H. He was very personable and made the trip down to get our car fun. Fittingly, we were helped in to our car by Deron. It was nice to be able to see him as we left and upon departure he told us to be sure to buckle up. Who does that? Someone with exceptional customer service skills - that's who. My only complaint is not about the hotel but their neighbor. We had a view of a parking garage and some of the attendants honk the horn when they make the turn on each floor. It was annoying and if you wanted an afternoon nap it would be an issue. It luckily did not occur during sleeping hours and we did have a restful night. Thank you W. We will return again.

“Impressed” Had never stayed here and was very pleased by what I saw. Room was fantastic. I was pleased to see it had a full-size ironing board, which sounds immensely boring but anyone who has ever flown cross-country and needed to press a pair of pants can tell you... that's a nice thing to see in New York.
Plenty of room to work and the room looked brand new. The gym was really nice and appeared to be well-kept up. The location is fantastic, especially if you have an office in midtown. I didn't need the subway, but the 6 train is only 3 blocks away.
Would absolutely stay here again.

“Nice hotel, fantastic staff” A great hotel - clean, comfy, reasonably priced restaurant. I was there for a meeting - the meeting staff was great. While not walking distance to places, it's 2-3 miles to great restaurants. I was there for the Auto Show - just everything you could want in a hotel...nice gym too

“Perfect for one” I echo the previous comments on the size of the rooms (small), the quality of the staff (excellent), and the strength of the air conditioning (arctic.) I would also add that the shower was a good size, and there was an endless supply of hot water. With the heat and the humidity in New York this past weekend I took three showers a day. While the TV didn't work, and I couldn't figure out the ipads for guests on the lobby wall (my fault, not the hotel's) the place was running extraordinarily well for a shakedown cruise. If they can keep the level of staffing while also opening the rooftop bar (I heard later this month) and the Great Room then this will be a wonderful, well located property with very small rooms. I had a queen bed room -- I measured it with footsteps and came up with a 10X10 bedroom and a 4X5 bathroom. Very cleverly organized but I expect it would not be appropriate for larger people, or families, or groups that were not on the most intimate of terms -- while sitting on the toilet you are most visible and audible to anyone on the bed. A major plus of my room (806) was a great view of the Empire State Building -- I fell asleep watching the camera flashes on the observation deck.

“3rd visit in 2012” This was my third visit in 2012. The location is key and one of the best in the CIty. If you are coming from JFK take the Air Train to Jamaica then the E subway to 7th and 53rd. The hotel is 2 mins walk from the exit. For just $7.50 door to virtually the hotel its better than a $60 cab ride if you include tolls and tips. 
I stayed in an Executive Floor room which allowed access to the Executive Lounge. What a transformation! It has been moved from the 44th Floor to a brand new location on the main floor, This to my mind is a great pity as the views before were amazing. However the refurbishment is really good and for breakfast the extra space using the part of the lobby bar area is great. In the evening when they are serving canapes its a bit of a zoo to be honest. Too small, too many people.
I will continue to stay for the location and the staff who are always friendly.

“It not they way it's described on the website.” Checked into hotel and was told that there was no resturant. It apparently recently closed; so recently that the website has not been updated. The hallway need a good scrubbing. My room, with a 'view', is filthy and the blind are broken like a run down housing project. Would switch hotel; but and too tired. 
They are trying to be a new trendy hotel; it's not working.

“It is just ok” I booked the hotel through hotwire as a hiding the name deal for a 3.5 stars hotel. When I finally saw the hotel name, I was quite disappointed. Best Western should be just 2 or 2.5 stars.
Anyway, the hotel is a typical best western. The staff at the front desk is very nice though. The free breakfast is the same as in other best western. The free WIFI is extremely slow and sometimes it doesn't work.
What I personally don't like is the location in lower east town. It might be convenient for people interested in the fish market. For people like me wants to use NYC public transportation, the hotel is about 15 min walk to the subway station. I would like to stay in Mid-town next time when I travel to NYC.

“Superb views- In dire need of renovation” Checking in I felt like I have arrived at the grand old dame of Chicago. Beautiful old house - located with magnificant views of Grant Park and the Buckingham Fountain and all of Lake Michigan at your feet. However this grand old dame really needs some makeup. The rooms are fading the wall paper peeling off the walls - the beds overly soft after years of use, and the electrical installations eliciting sparks every time you plugs something in.
The service is completely impersonal - no one seems to care any more , front desk, bell desk, room service - all of them seem to have lost the spark.
Sorry I cant give more than a '2' for this place, I hope someone with money will soon see the potential of this place and have it properly renovated to its former glory.

“You have to see the picture!!!” A picture is worth a thousand words!!!
#1 I arrived at midnight. Had to use the courtesy phone and it took 15 minutes for someone to come and check me in even though I already had a reservation.
#2 I was so tired i just walked in and laid down on the bed without even unpacking.
#3 I dozed off but was awakened within 15 minutes. My back, my neck, my arms and legs below the knee where covered with fleas. I could see them jumping all over.
#4 I jumped into the shower, put all my clothes in a plastic bag and changed. By then even my suitcases had fleas. Called the front desk. The night lady was very courteous and did the best she could and she changed me from room 102 to another room. 
#5 In the am I asked to see the manager very politely and showed the reception lady the flea bites. She was RUDE and unpolite. They did not offer any type of apology. I had to ask them for a refund of one night.
VERY RUDE SHIFT MANAGER AND LADY DURING NOONTIME. 
Also, hotel is nextto the freeway and there are no restaurants within walking distance. 
Parking is an issue at night. You may end up parking far from your actual room.

“What A Find!” After reading so many positive reviews of the Cosmopolitan on this site, it seemed too good to be true. The cynic in me wondered if they hadn't been planted by the hotel itself. But I needed to be close to the World Financial Center for an early morning meeting, so I booked one night there and was pleasantly surprised to find that all I'd read was true. It was about a ten-minute subway ride from Penn Station, and the subway stop was almost literally at the door to the property. Check-in was prompt and efficient. I asked for a room on a higher floor and, while I overlooked the proverbial alley, it was as quiet as a church. I never heard a sound, either from the outside or the corridors. The room, while typical for NYC in its' small size, was spotless. I was thrilled that the bathroom actually had a countertop where I could spread things out, instead of the typical pedastal sink I usually find in the city. There are about 5 or 6 subway lines on either corner, and it's a quick walk to the financial district. The only problem I encountered was that the lightbulb in my desk lamp was burnt out. I asked twice for a new one and it was never replaced, so the lighting was not the best for the reading I needed to do. Otherwise, I wouldn't hesitate to stay here again or recommend it to others.

“Nice hotel, big problems” I stayed 2 weeks at the Indigo Galleria during a business trip to Houston, and my overall experience was negative in many ways.
To begin with, as many reviews say, the decoration is colorful and very nice. Everything looks fresh and funny. The frontdesk staff is friendly but not very efficient.
There is a small bar/ restaurant by the lobby. Food is quite good and you have many choices considering the size of the place. Room service is also reasonable. However, it's closed during the afternoon, and after 11pm. So if you work long hours like me and still want to have dinner when you get back, you'll be in trouble.
The rooms are quite small for US standards but size was not really a problem. The same applies to the bathroom; the only drawback is that there's no bathtube.
Beds are really comfortable but air conditioning is very noisy. And the worst issue with the rooms is that there are no blackout courtains and there's light coming from the parking all night long. I found that really annoying and, although I was changed to a different room, the problem didn't disappear completely. Try to get a room facing the Hotel's parking lot and in a high floor.
Homekeeping is not very good either, and the laundry service is quite annoying: you have to take your dirty clothes to the front desk if you want to have them washed.
Internet access is free but the connection is not very good. In my room it was usually OK, but when working in some of the ground floor meeting rooms it was quite hard to send or receive e-mails because the connection got interrupted every few minutes.
Location, however, is great. Although in a weird place behind two other buildings, the Indigo is just a few steps from the Galleria, with lots of shopping and dinning offers.
All in all, it's a nice hotel and if you get a low fare it could be great for hollidays. If you go to Houston for business, go somewhere else. I recommend the Hilton Post Oak, a few blocks away. It's a bit more expensive but it's worth the difference.

“Disappointed to be honest” OK, let's do the good bits first
1. Location. You know this. You've checked the map, you've read the other reviews. It's 10 yards from the nearest subway entrance.
2. Provided you have it included or complimentary, the breakfast is good and service friendly.
3. Decent gym.
The not so good.
1. Nearly 400$ a night for a room which was dark, small and a bathroom like a cell in a dungeon with poor extractor fan, and a bath (which we had to ask for specially otherwise we'd have had a shower without specifying) you get your ankles wet and not much else in.
2. Very sparse amenities in the room - limited toiletries, had to ask for coffee top-ups every day as otherwise not renewed.
And this was with a Diamond HHonors upgrade on board.
We picked this hotel for it's proximity to Penn State and the the Amtrak to Boston. Here in Boston writing this review, I'm in a massive room for the same cost with a grandstand view of Fenway Park, right next to a Subway station. 
Increasingly it's hard to forgive the price of central New York Hotels when you get almost nothing for your money. This hotel has well meaning and friendly staff but that can't make up for the pokey rooms and lack of amenities.
I'd be even less chuffed were it not for the fact that for the room rate I chose, with added HHonors membership I scored a colossal 80000 points for the stay, effectively getting 2 nights for free. I won't be using the points to return here I'm afraid.

“Don't” Ooh, don't stay here. A client of ours from California had me book him a hotel in Chicago, and I thought I was getting a good rate on a botique hotel here in town. Wrong! According to our client, the place is a dump. He said he could tell as soon as he saw the lobby. I was so embarrassed. He hasn't had me book a hotel since.

“Tiny depressing room - horrible value” This is one of the most unpleasant hotels that I have ever stayed at. 
On the hotel's website, the rooms looked charming. My room was far from charming. First, the room was very small and if a hotel room is small, it needs to be intelligently designed. This one was not. The room was so small and the space so badly used that there was no place to put my suitcase other than next to the door, which meant I had to squeeze in and out of the room. The bathroom was miniscule, with no counter space and a combination tub and shower. The bed was very uncomfortable - much too soft -- and the pillows were horrible. The room was dark, overlooking an airshaft, and it didn't help that the window was covered with this strange window covering that didn't open, meaning that you couldn't raise it to let in what little light was coming from the air shaft. Also, the strange wall covering was partially transparent, so it didn't keep out all the light at night. So you had the worst of both worlds - a room that wasn't completely dark at night and a room that wasn't bright during the day.
The hallways of the hotel were narrow and depressing and there were only two tiny elevators, which took forever to come.
After two miserable nights at this place, I got smart and moved to the Sofitel down the street. For exactly $10 more a night than I was paying at the Algonquin, I got a room that was approximately four times the size, with a huge beautiful bathroom with separate shower and tub, with curtains that actually opened and closed and a beautiful view. The Sofitel, unlike the Algonquin, also had huge efficient elevators that came promptly. It pays to be be a smart consumer. The Algonquin is a rip-off for what is provided.

“Best deal in NYC!” This hotel doesn't appear to be amazing from the lobby, which is rather small. However, don't let first impressions deter you from staying here! We loved staying at the Residence Inn! We had reserved two rooms for six of us and they asked us if we'd prefer to be in a two-bedrooms suite, which was wonderful. There were two very large bedrooms (each with own bath) with a completely separate living room between them with a sofabed. Also a nice kitchen and full-size refrigerator! The touches to make it more like home are so nice (huge lounge area, wonderful breakfast,afternoon snacks). Plus free wireless high speed internet was nice to have. 
I'm not sure why this is considered "Times Square" because it is actually a ten-minute walk from the center of Times Square. We loved the area -- lots of bead/jewelry shops (6th Ave.) and other great shopping as well (Macys, Herald Square, etc.). It is very close to Madison Square Garden and Penn Station, too. We actually preferred this location, being closer to midtown, over Times Square. Our room was extremely clean and very spacious. The Residence Inn will be our place to stay for future trips to the city!

“A really terrible place to stay” We had used 1800hotels who recommended this hotel for 2 single travels. Instead on the 2 beds reserved we were given a tiny room with one bed and no room for a cot (which they didn't have anyway). Fortunately I could stay with a friend in New Jersey! There is no doorman, no concierge, no amenities except mouthwash were provided. The staff were sullen and told us they couldn't accomodate us because we had booked through a third party - even though they had a room with 2 beds in their SoHO property. The next day when a room with 2 beds was 'found,' we were charged $20.00 to make the switch even though our reservation through 1800hotels was for 2 beds. 1800hotels gave no assistance and I would never use them as a booking agent again.
Oh yes, the neighborhood is dangerous after dark.

“One of Better Hampton Inns” As a Hilton Diamond member, I get to stay at a lot of Hampton Inns. This is definitely one of the newer and better ones around. The place is very clean and very up-to-date in decor. I got upgraded to a suite which is very roomy with a small kitchen. The overall layout of the hotel is unusual and the free parking spaces somewhat limited. I think they tried to maximize hotel space on small lot. Location is in the midst of many medical centers and there is a Luby's Cafeteria next door, with many better dining choices down Old Spanish Trail. The light rail is a block away. Place is very busy - I guess everyone knows what a good choice it is.

“My Mini Getaway” I stayed here just after Hurricane Ike had hit and I was looking for a mini getaway for myself. I wouldn't normally have chosen this hotel (the decor isn't particularly appealing to me and I don't really like the downtown location) but my choices were limited as few places had reopened after the hurricane hit. 
The front desk clerk was polite and professional (though I don't remember him cracking a smile) and I was upgraded to a king corner suite instantly. The room was large with a great view of Minute Maid Park, and there were no noise issues at all (either from next door, outside, hallway) The bed wasn't very comfortable as it was a bit hard and the linens weren't very soft. The bathroom was my absolute favorite part of the room - Not only was it large but there was tons of counter space, plenty of towels (an issue I've encountered at some other hotels) seperate tub and the shower had just the right water pressure. 
I didn't have a chance to try out room service as I ventured to Vic & Anthony's for dinner (a must for anyone that loves a good steakhouse) but I remember being tempted by quite a few items. If I'm not mistaken, the only parking option is to valet which was either $20 or $21 for an overnight stay. Overall I was happy with the stay (not just because of my circumstances) but I just don't think I'd stay here again as I prefer the Galleria part of town rather than downtown.

“Exquisite Stay” I just returned from an absolutely wonderful stay at the Gansevoort - Meatpacking District property. The service, ambiance, and location were just superb. And, my room (Deluxe Room) was just wonderful, with a very comfortable bed, and great bathroom. I enjoyed a quiet night's rest in this very active neigborhood. While I was in NYC this time for business, I will return here for both business and pleasure. I highly recommend you enjoy a stay at his fantastic property, in a fun neighborhood.

“Never again.” For $300 a night you'd think you'd get something more then this. It's a shame to call these cramped rooms a 'suite'. They should've utilized more of the lobby space on their rooms. Housekeeping service was shoddy - during my week stay only once was my room cleaned without me calling the front desk.
However, the happy hour and breakfast were great. But these two things aren't at the top of my priority list when returning to a hotel. I'm surprised this hotel is ranked so highly.

“Fabulous!” Awesome hotel. Great service, very clean. Well worth paying an extra to get a lake view. The restaurant had amazing food (breakfast and dinner). The lounge on the 33rd floor was small and only had one client...I was disappointed to see that, thinking I would be partying there. The bed is very comfortable too! The only real negative that I am seeing is that even though it is right beside the lake and the navy pier, it is a bit far from public transport. You have to walk quite a bit to reach a subway or train station. The living room (lobby) is great! I am looking coming to the W Lakeshore again!

“I liked this hotel” This hotel was in a great location. Very close to the Galleria and Memorial areas. The rooms were very well kept and maintained the bathroom was spotless, and all of the necessary amenities were provided. The bed was EXTREMELY comfortable. The internet access was a little odd, as the login screen kept interupting my session, however. I never had that happen ANYWHERE before. 
The service was very good, especially, in the restaurant and the bell service. 
The grounds were very nice, and the pool looked nice (though I did not use it) and clean.
I would recommend this hotel.

“excellent location” Everything about this hotel was perfect really. Firstly the price was very competitive. The location perfect. The end of the road one way was all the buzz otf Times Square and in the other the exclusive shopping on Fifth Avenue.
The room clean and good with lots of storage, the bathroom clean and new with a shower over the bath.
I was very comfortable here.
The staff were pleasant and helpful

“overrated” We stayed 5 nights in april at the end of a three week NY trip, quite frankly i found the hotel to be overrated and over priced for what it was. We stayed at Affinia Manhattan and found the staff there to be much more personable and competent than at Affinia Gardens and found the rooms, although more dated more adequate for the price we paid.
We had a junior suite i think it's called at AG, the first room we had was huge, on the 10th floor at the front of the building but we were only there two of our four nights as someone directly below us was smoking and it was filtering up through the vents and choking us. As it's supposed to be a non smoking hotel this was unacceptable and the way it was handled by management quite unsatisfactory, in fact i emailed a complaint through the website on return from NY and never received a response, this was typical of the management style we observed during our stay. Whatever you do don't buy breakfast at the hotel, we received it free as compensation for our terrible first two night and we threw it out it was so terrible, when i told the front desk on check out (they tried to ccharge us for a supposedly free breakfast too) they just shrugged and said sorry to hear you didn't enjoy it. The pastries were stale, coffee was bad, a granola/museli bar and a bottle of apple juice in a paper bag...what a rip off, glad we didnt pay for it.
anyway, this was the most expensive hotel we stayed in during our stay and that included stays at two Thompson hotels which were far superior and in a completely different league, but $40 a night cheaper, go figure, i wouldn't bother with Affinia Gardens again and have told my friends not to bother for their trips.

“Surprising Service” Unlike some reviewers, we had a very positive experience with customer service. We had reserved a N/S king room, because a rm with 2 beds wasn't available when we made the reservation. We arrived at 11:30am, and asked if rm with 2 beds was available. They gave us the option of waiting til regular check in time if we wanted 2 beds, or checking in immediately if we stayed with the king. We opted to stay with the king, and check in immed, which was great. Check in was a breeze, and luggage was brought to the room promptly. 
 The HUGE surprise was the gentleman who delivered the rollaway bed, he chatted about area restaurants and patiently waited while we decided where to position the bed. When my husband handed him a tip, he politely REFUSED it, saying he just hoped we enjoyed our trip and came back!! We were totally surprised -- this is NYC, with the reputation of gruff, rude service -- and this fellow is politely turning down a tip! That certainly left an indelible impression, and was a great start to the trip.
 The room was clean and well appointed, king bed was comfortable, and our teenage daughter never complained about the rollaway bed. The marble bath was small, but clean and had two showerheads. Of course, location can't be beat, and bus system is terrific. Would highly suggest (as the concierge did ) a "fun pass" -- for $7 you can take buses and subway as many times as you need for the day. We're from Maine, but had no trouble negotiating the city buses, from upper east side to SoHo. 
 Only suggestion is to have at least two concierges on duty when hotel is busy. They only had one concierge during our stay and she was very busy, and always had one or more people waiting to speak with her. 
 Would definitely stay again, overall we had a very positive experience with both the room and the service.

“Un albergo ideale per chi vuole trascorrere una settimana all’insegna del miglior spettacolo di Broadway.” Un albergo ideale per chi vuole trascorrere una settimana all’insegna del miglior spettacolo di Broadway.
In questo albergo sono stati girati numerosi film e l’ngresso sembra davvero l’ingresso di un hotel di un’altra epoca.
Fantastiche le camere, rinnovate e lo staff sempre gentile e disponibile per qualsiasi richiesta che riguardi un biglietto per broadway.
Carine le cabine telefoniche in stile retro dietro la lobby principale.
Assolutamente consigliabile !

“Not up to Starwood Standards” I stayed here in June 2012. I stay in a lot of Starwood hotels. I normally find all of them to be very nice. This one disappointed me. The hotel is not well maintained. The room was in serious need of a complete makeover. The wall paper was dirty and torn. The carpet was worn. The bathroom had an old vanity and dirty tile. The hotel appears to have lost all of its pride. I am sure it is operating on a shoestring budget. On the positive side . . It is in a nice location. The beds and linens were fine. I almost gave it three stars, but I expect much more out of Starwood.

“Showing its age, but still reliable” I've stayed here several times on business trips. It's a good and reliable experience whose location is either its biggest asset or greatest drawback, depending on your trip plans.
The rooms are quiet and spacious. The staff I've interacted with have all been polite if not overly helpful. My times here have been essentially for sleeping and showering, with not much spare time to experience the restaurants or other aspects of the hotel.
As the hotel is part of the Galleria Mall complex, just finding the entrance with a car can be challenging (enter from Post Oak Avenue) and parking is expensive. If your trip's going to be Galleria-centric, either for shopping or for work, the hassle of parking is well worth it.
All in all, a solid if unspectacular Westin.

“Mixed bag at the Standard” I stayed here for 5 nights for a work/pleasure trip late November. First, don't get me wrong - this hotel is very popular and sort of exudes this hip urban vibe that is difficult to not want to be a part of. The location is excellent and for that alone, maybe worth the plus 500.00 a night room rate. For what this hotel puts itself out there as, however, it falls a little short. The rooms : while incredibly clean and well serviced every day, and fabulous views was like a dorm room. No room for luggage or clothing or toiletries. For a short trip workable, but this space crunch wore on me as time went on. The shower has a major design flaw - just a short piece of glass separating it from the rest of the bathroom - leaving water all over the bathroom floor every day which necessitated towels on the floor just to get ready. We were on the 14th floor with views of the Hudson - that was fab - and no noise from the club or the street below on that floor. We ate in the Standard Grill for breakfast - frankly awful service. They brought the wrong orders and when we pointed it out - the very surly waiter without a single word picked them back up and walked away. Really?? Your not that special. Save yourself the aggravation here. There are plenty of places nearby to eat with good service. The front desk staff was friendly but unhelpful. The concierge couldn't be bothered to even pick up the phone to see if we could get a restaurant reservation- telling us to just walk down the street and go on in and ask if we could get a table. Another really?? moment. Another concierge sent us to the gallery district in Chelsea. We specifically asked if the galleries had reopened since Sandy and he told us yes - ummm no. We went there - not a single first floor gallery open and only a handful of second floor galleries. I guess I should've googled. Who knew the concierge would lack the knowledge of such basics within the neighborhood of the hotel. Perhaps I'm picky - but when you go to a hotel such as this - these are basics that should be delivered stellarly. That just didn't happen here. The bell staff were the best and most helpful. Friendly guys who remembered you every day and were always offering to help. Nightclub? Impossible. Closed for private parties for several nights - which also means by the way that the hotel elevators are reduced from 4 to 2- as if the hotel guests are the afterthought. The decor is hip and modern, and there are always people around everywhere - lots of great shopping and restaurants. I might stay here again, but was disappointed with the service issues, micro room and shower flaw. It was cool- sure, but not very inviting and comfortable.

“What a dump” Very disappointed in the staff at this hotel. They could care less. Its a problem at many NYC hotels but this place takes it to a new level. The problem is it is a tight hotel market and it is sad to say I am paying $700 a night and I don t get much back.
Some things like showing up at night and there is no bellman to help with bags. A front desk staff that is very poorly trained. A pathetic night manager Mona who has no concept that she is in the hospitably business. Her English skills are poor and her people skills are worse. Come back from being gone all day and our room was not cleaned. Road noise from the street is the loudest I have experienced from My 20 plus years coming to the city.
The Management of this hotel should embarrassed but the fact is they are the problem. Every employee I come in contact with could give a crap.
$15 for internet. I will include a photo of what looks like mold. What is wrong with this world when a major hotel chain in a major U.S. city can pull this kind of bait and switch ?
Starwood should be ashamed this place falls in every way. You can do better move on to another property.

“No Umbrellas!” The other reviews here are all correct - this is a nicely located hotel with overall friendly staff. Decor is ok. Check-in and check-out were both quick and easy. The included breakfast buffet is really weak, but Ann Sathers across the street is amazing so no need to eat at the hotel anyway. 
We stayed three days/nights, including the single rainiest day in Chicago's history. I took it for granted that a hotel that bills itself as an "elegant boutique hotel" would have umbrellas for guests to use. Our bill averaged over $300/night with taxes - for that money I would definitely expect some umbrellas sitting near the main door or in the room. Nope. Not a single one. I had to run down the street in the pouring rain with my 11-month-old to get a cheap one at a local store. And here is my other hotel-related rain complaint. The ramp that comes into the hotel is fully visible to the front desk staff. It's curvy and takes a little while to navigate with a stroller or the luggage cart. There were numerous times when I was trying to navigate a stroller with one hand while carrying an umbrella with the other hand. Approaching the lobby door, the 2-3 otherwise unoccupied people at the front desk would be watching this. Never once did one of them come around to help with the door. It wasn't just me - they never helped with the door with anyone else either - old people, people loaded down with luggage, etc.... NOT what I would expect for $300+ a night. 
So would I stay here again? Maybe, based on location. Definitely not, based on overall service for the price you pay.

“We anticipated a better experience.” Great location but that is about all I can really say that was good about the hotel. We chose to park at the lot a half a block down from the hotel that was $17 dollars less per day than the hotel charge. Our room was not ready upon check in so we had to check our bags with the bellman and wait until we received a phone call five hours later when our room was ready. So we had to take our bags back to the first floor ourselves since the entrance of the hotel is on the first floor and the check-in desk is on the 2nd floor. The system to use the elevators and door locks needs a lot of work! Every time we got in an elevator with other guests there was a difficulty with the magnetic key cards, and it got amusing as guests were riding the elevators trying to get them to stop at their chosen floors. The same was with how frustrating it was to get into the gym. Someone put a prop to keep the gym door open because the key never would not open the gym door. The pool is very shallow and small and not very good for exercise or even relaxation. The housekeeping is lacking: they came late and only left us three towels for three people. They didn’t even put away the iron or ironing board. Their ‘Corner King” room was a triangle shape and very awkward. The bed and pull out couch are right next to each other due to the awkward shape of the room. The towels are thin and small. The bed sheets smelled like BO. The TV had horrible reception and was a 1970’s boob tube. Check out was old style, we had to go to the desk again... not very advanced.

“The Roosevelt Hotel” I stayed at the Roosevelt hotel recently while I was in New York. The hotel itself was once a grand NY establishment but it has seen better days and is not particularly well maintained. My bathroom tub was in terrible shape. The staff is somewhat helpful but I couldn't even expect the doorman to flag down a taxi for me. Still, the location is wonderful and the rooms fairly comfortable.

“I don't say this lightly. stay away” I stayed at this hotel for 1 night due to a late night, and early morning for work at my office in midtown. The lobby looks nice, and is quite trendy.
The rooms are old, like many in NYC, but the hotel makes no attempt to up-sell anything. Old sheets, towels, mattress, curtains, carpet, windows, door...
I got back late and took a shower. Very basic amenities (soap, shampoo) and when I pulled the towel off the rack, I discovered what can only be described at black shoe polish, covering part of the towel. I dried off using my dirty clothes, and tried to call down to get fresh towels, but no one answered after about 10 minutes of trying (without exaggeration). I gave up and pulled the sheets down (yes, really...) and found large rips in the sheets (about 1ft x 1ft triangle). I made the effort and followed through on the ringing and ringing, finally reaching the front desk and having them send up new bed linens. The housekeeping supervisor came up with new sheets (by chance, I didn't request that she do it personally), and while she was putting them on, I noticed more small rips in the new sheets. I pointed them out and the supervisor said "they're all like that." I commented that I would be ashamed to put linens in a room like that, and she agreed! It was all the owners were willing to provide... The supervisor brought new towels, but left the old ones... 
When checking out in the morning, I brought the dirty towels to the front desk with me. They were completely grossed out, and I pointed out the fact that I had discovered them while getting out of the shower with no alternative to dry off. It also made me question the overall cleanliness of the room- had any of the linens been replaced when the room was turned over?? They didn't charge me for the room, which I'm sure my company appreciated, but it didn't do anything for me... On my way out the front desk manager gave me her card and offered me a free night to make it up, but honestly, I wouldn't even stay there for free. Really.

“Simply a wonderful hotel” My husband and I stayed here in January 2007 while he was on a business trip. On the day he was working, I felt like I had New York City on my doorstep! The hotel is literally in the middle of Times Square. Since this was my first trip to NYC, it was a great introduction, and I didn't have to worry about finding my way around the city. I walked north up Broadway to Central Park, spotting some famous sites along the way. The TKTS (half-price) Broadway ticket booth is located right at the hotel, so I simply went downstairs and purchased our theater tickets for that evening. That's convenience! The hotel is also very large, but the elevators are fast and the layout is simple to navigate. The hotel lounge/bar was fantastic, offering us great views of the Times Square action while we sipped our drinks. The rooms are nicely decorated, and we had a great view of the city. I also LOVED the beds -- one of the best night's sleep I've ever had on vacation. I highly recommend this hotel.

“brilliant hotel” I had booked a one-bedroom suite, at a very reasonable price. We arrived late due to a storm at the airport, all the suites had been taken so they gave us a complementary upgrade to a 2-bedroom suite. This was the largest room I have ever seen in New York ! We had 2 large bedrooms 1 king the other 2 doubles, 2 bathrooms 3 TVs , extra large lounge, small kitchen and dining area, and 5 huge walk in wardrobes.
The hotel was very clean and friendly.
The breakfast for $3 per person was great
The only down side was it has no lounge or bar hence the 2 stars.
Great position, 2 blocks from Central Park, across the road from Carnegie hall.We walked to Time Square, Empire state building ,but got a bus to china town.
The Hard Rock café has now moved to Time square and is no longer on 57th street.
When I return to New York I will stay at the Salisbury

“Large rooms and great service” I recently stayed at the Affinia Dumont for 3 nights. I selected this hotel based on the recommendation of a colleague who hosted a meeting there and based on the incredibly affordable rate we were able to get. The hotel itself is located in Murray Hill area of midtown east. Though it is not necessarily the most touristy area with loads of fantastic restaurants, the hotel's location is within walking distance of more populous areas. We walked up to Times Square which was about 7 blocks or so. 
The staff at the hotel was attentive and polite. Room service is served from the on-site restaurant, the Barking Dog, and while it certainly isn't 4 star quality, it was ok and really inexpensive in comparison with most NYC hotels. 
The rooms are exceptionally large with a small kitchen. I was in a King Studio Suite and could have easily fit a pack and play in this room without compromising walking space. MOst importantly, my room was impeccably clean. 
I will certainly consider this hotel again in the future.

“Pleasantly Surprised!” I had booked the hotel on a "blind" travel site, so I have to admit that I wasn't that excited when I finally saw the name. I thought I knew most of the hotels in mid-town, but this was one that was definitely beneath my radar. 
The hotel ambience is very boutique-like, and check in was smooth and efficient. One of my pet peeves in NY hotels is elevator waits, but I could always get from the lobby to my 5th floor room in about 30 seconds. 
The room was completely updated and BIG by NY standards. Definitely one of the larger rooms I have ever had in the city. Everything was clean and the bed was modern and comfy. I recommend this hotel, which is not typical for most of the NYC hotels I have stayed at!

“**A Diamond In The Rough**” Just spent the weekend at the Mandarin Oriental, and I must say that by most standards, this was the most superb hotel I have ever had the pleasure of visiting. We found the hotel public spaces and room to be an architectural and interior design masterpiece. The designers did a wonderful job of melding the understated, elegant, peaceful asian atmosphere of MO with the vibrant exuberance of NYC. It seemed as if wherever we stood within the hotel, we were treated to the most spectacular views of NYC I have ever seen. Its location within the brand new Time Warner Center is sublime. It gives a whole new dimension to Columbus Circle and the scale and exterior design of the place is awe-inspiring.
Upon arrival, we were upgraded to a Premier Hudson View Room, and what a room it was. Beautiful and plush with every amenity imaginable. It was a corner room with sweeping views of the river and city from every vantage point. We could lie in bed and have a perfect view of the Empire State Building directly in front of us. Amenities included plasma TV, DVD CD comprehensive entertainment system with surround sound, deep soaking tub with another great view, separate marble shower with huge rain shower head, minibar with great selection of beverages and Dean & DeLuca goodies, luxe bedding and feather pillows, robes, slippers, Fiji water and fresh flowers at turn down etc.
We also had the pleasure of visiting the hotel spa. This was by far the best spa experience I have ever had. Again, the place is beautiful and refined. We were advised to arrive 45 mins early to use the "heat and water experience" which consists of beautiful, separate whirlpool, sauna/steam bath areas for men and women. Then a rest in the co-ed tea lounge and you're off to your treatment. By the time I got to my massage, I was already so relaxed that the transition was seamless. Afterwards, I was escorted to the relaxation room which is yet another exquisite area with daybeds, fountain, refreshments and more spectacular views of NYC.
While the young staff was very friendly and tried very hard, I give the hotel less than five stars right now because of some of the gliches they are still having with service. Examples are, waited 40 mins. for our bags to be brought to our room upon check in (called several times and my husband finally had to go down and find them), room service a little slow (however the food was delicious), difficulty getting room service trays removed, turn down service on second night was very late (we were already in bed), minor difficulties with entertainment center that had to be reset on the second day. This and the fact that everything is EXHORBITANTLY expensive keep me from giving this hotel the highest mark. However, given the fact that it is still relatively new and working out the bugs, I am willing to give MO a little more time to become 5 star.
Overall, a wonderful (albeit not cheap) hotel experience for any hotel snob.

“Comfortable, traditional and reasonable value” Stayed 3 nights whilst on business. Comfortable and quiet room 518 with a small bathroom but better than average shower. No tea or coffee making facilities or complimentary touches so not up to the standard of Setai on 5th where I stayed last time. Location is fine and only about 15 mins walk to Time Sq and close to all the shops on 5th Ave. Bar is small but friendly with decent choice of beers and bar food until midnight.

“A Good Hotel -- But It Is What It Is” This is a perfectly fine hotel in a very convenient location. Some of the negative reviews here seem to be from people who somehow expect a huge business/convention-oriented Hyatt to be a five-star establishment with a high level of personal service. If that's important to you, then you need to find a nice upscale boutique elsewhere, but if you just want a clean, comfortable reasonably sized hotel room with the usual amenities (though admittedly no in-room coffee) this will do just fine. While this is not my favorite NY neighborhood, the transit connections just next door at Grand Central Station make it easy to get just about anywhere in Manhattan and beyond via subway -- you'll save on cab fares. Check-in and check-out were quick and painless. Can't comment on the fitness facilities or restaurants as I didn't try them. However, if you don't like the hotel restaurant offerings, there is no shortage of breakfast and lunch places in the neighborhood, though I might venture a little further afield for dinner. The area is bustling because of its proximity to Grand Central, but I heard no street noise at all from my 20th floor room. Again, you have to understand what sort of hotel this is, but if you're OK with that I would recommend it.

“Great place to stay in Chelsea” We have stayed at the Maritime several times over the past few years and we still love the place. Great location, especially if you need to be near the Chelsea art galleries which are walking distance, as is the HighLine. The rooms and bathrooms are small but well-designed. Good amenities. Great honor bar with everything one might want. The on-site bar and the restaurants are terrific and should not be missed. Matsuri is first-rate, and gorgeous. Service at the hotel is quite good; staffers are really responsive and efficient. The rooms could use a bit of rehabbing, but overall, it's still an excellent hotel and we will definitely return. Upper floor rooms are a bit quieter and have excellent views.

“another great priceline win!” as a regular visitor to NYC, I have become quite comfortable with my Priceline "wins"..Typically, I usually am quite happy with my wins below $200 a night, but this was great. A beautiful location at 61st and Park Ave, the hotel was spacious, well equipped, and professionally staffed..Beds were great, I would be thrilled to land her again!

“Great place to stay at NYC” We loved everything at the hotel! It is a classic hotel, Art Deco, the lobby is amazing! 
The Oscar`s buffet was a great place for our morning breakfast! Lovely decorated and great ambiance! The room was great, facing Park Avenue. The staff was efficient and provided answers to all our questions. At our arrival we made the check in and left our luggage, once it was early in the morning. A couple of hours latter (well before check in time) they called us that our room was ready. Room service was done twice daily! The hotel is close to everything, a few blocks to Central Park, Fifth Avenue, Broadway. My only complaint was that we stayed only for 4 nights

“Great HOtel!” Great Hotel! The room was big, bathroom was clean and also very large. And, something that usually doesn't happen - lots and lots of towels in the bathroom! The bed was very comfortable and had enough pillows. 
The rooms were quiet - I never heard our neighbors. THe elevators weren't slow at all and we never felt like we had to wait a long time. CHeck-in and Check-out were also very fast. We go to NYC a lot and this hotel was by far one of our favorites. The location was even nice. We don't usually like being in Times Square but it didn't seem crowded at our hotel at all. The hotel is about 1-2 blocks off Times Square so it wasn't very noisy. Easy walk to the subway and the subway is a major interchange so you can catch lots of different subway lines from that one stop. ALso, the Public Library is one block away. 
Two downsides: 1) breakfast wasn't that good at all. Our room came with a free breakfast - we only made that mistake the first morning. After that, we ate elsewhere. 2) the windows in the hotel room only opened about 3 inches so we couldn't get a good breeze going. 
I would definitely stay here again. I recommend this hotel to anyone going to NYC.

“Love Westins... consistently great and this hotel is more of the same.” Booked this hotel for a first visit to Houston. Connected to the Galleria shopping mall, the hotel offers an easy way to shop, while still being connected easily to major highways, making it easy to get around Houston.
Front desk staff and concierge were very helpful, always willing to provide answers, or research it if they didn't know already. A wealth of information, with instant recall.
A nice outdoor pool, though no hot tub.
Numerous dining options, both in the mall and in the immediate local neighbourhood.
Comfortable beds, coupled with Westin amenities make this a great hotel, in a great location, with great staff.

“Very Nice 4-Star -- But not quite a St. Regis” I can't complain, and I'm not. I booked a room at the Essex house for $339 per night during the week of July 11 (good deal for that time frame) and was "bumped" to the "St. Regis Club." The St. Regis Club is basically the upper floors of the Essex House. It tries to be a separate hotel in the same way as the Tower at the Waldorf tries to be different from the Waldorf Astoria. Both are grand old hotels, but the emphasis on both is on "old."
Location: Central Park South -- really can't beat it. It's close by the Theatre District, Lincoln Center, the mid-town financial district and the near-upper East and West Sides. An oasis, especially if your room has a park view.
Room: Good size for the City. Large entry foyer made the room seem like a junior suite. Excellent Starwood "Heavenly bed." Decor and bathroom were, overall, a bit on the dated side, but quite nice. Plush bathrobes and outstanding bathroom amenities. Somewhat obstructed, but refreshing, view of Central Park.
Service: Check-in and check-out were very smooth. Lots of security by the elevator who were very good at remembering that you belonged, even if you came by sweaty and in gym clothes after they had seen you in your suit. Service at the bar was a bit spotty -- but remember you are dealing with a unionized New York work force.
Two gripes -- (1) The gym. They would like to have you believe that they have a full "spa" with complete work-out room (for which they charge you $10 per visit). This is one of the lamer exercise facilities I have come across in the last few years at a 4-star hotel, and certainly the worst one that charges you extra for the privilege of using it. It has some cardio, a few weight machines, some decent dumbells (but only one measily bench). Most newer 3-star business hotels have equipment that blows the Essex House away and they do not charge. (2) Internet access -- Really, charging for the internet at a fancy hotel is bush league these days. It's not just every budget hotel that offers free wifi hi-speed access, but in my experience, every hip up-scale hotel does so as well.
The main reason that I do not give this hotel 5 "gold dots" is that it pretends to be a St. Regis. Senator, I have stayed at the St. Regis, and you, sir, are no St. Regis. The St. Regis is a 5-star hotel in every nit-picking, over-the-top luxurious way. This hotel, while a solid 4-star, is not even close. With all due respect to Starwood (my favorite hotel chain), they are diluting their highest-end brand by labelling this as a "St. Regis." 
One final note -- The Essex House is the perennial favorite of Saturday Night Live guests and alumni, so don't be surprised to encounter SNL glitterati on the elevators or in the lounge.

“Decent place, average service, could be cleaner.” The hotel lobby is nice, clean and modern. I attempted to check in around 1:30 pm and was told very matter of factly that check in was at 3:00 pm and I would have to wait until then. No apology or anything. That's fine. So I waited in the bar until then. The fire alarm was being tested that day and went off 3 times. 
The room was pretty modern and nice and was decorated with soothing colors. The bed was very comfortable. The toilet seat was not cleaned and there was hair on the seat and in the toilet. Gross. 
The TV in my room was at least 15-20 years old but worked well. It was just strange to see all the modern furnishings and a TV that old. 
When trying to eat breakfast at the restaurant the next morning, I had been working in my room all morning so I arrived at 9:55 am and breakfast was over at 10:30 am and the girl seating me almost acted as though she was offended because I was the only one in the restaurant. She called me a late bloomer. Interesting. 
I will probably give them another try sometime. Everyone has off days.

“Deceptive Website” Since we have been pleased with the one bedroom suites at the Beekman, we booked a studio queen suite on the website for a one night stay. We were dismayed to find a very small double bed in a room that was too small to hold a queen sized bed. The room would have been adequate for one person, but was too small for two -- small enough so that it was difficult to get into the bed. When we complained, we were told that queen suites may have either a double or queen bed, but this is not clear from the website. The room was significantly overpriced for what it was.

“Basically decent hotel--outrageous luggage holding policy” This is a pretty good, if bland, hotel--clean, as quiet as you can expect in downtown Manhattan, a block from a subway station (and near more), easy walking distance to lots of interesting areas--which is why I stayed here. I can't see these rooms as large--there was not enough room to sit in the desk chair between the desk and the bed! (they should get a smaller chair). But they're not intolerably, claustrophobically small, like sometimes happens here. The bathroom has a bathtub almost long enough for shorter people to lie down in. There's enough lighting in the room, and enough darkness--there's only one, decent-sized window. I believe this is a new building, with its regularities, or it has been very thoroughly renovated.
It's in Chinatown, so there's activity most hours of the day and night, a good thing.
I guess by New York standards just over $200 per night for a double queen room is great, though almost anywhere else it would be excessive.
There are lots of European tourists here.
I'm very annoyed, however, by the idea that I have to pay $5 per bag for them to hold our bags after I check out. Every hotel I've ever stayed in--cheap or expensive--has done that service for free. Also they've got an exercise room that opens onto the roof, but you can't use the roof--a waste.

“Intimate Hotel-Recommended Hilton propery in the area” I've stayed at all the Hilton properties in the mid town area from the Waldorf to the hampton inn and I'd say this is my favorite. You almost feel like a local here, indiscreet from the outside, a small lobby with a door man that you feel you know well after a few days. The rooms are nice, fridge, robes and pretty big for new york. I believe this property was set-up as a timeshare and I kept waiting for someone to try a sales pitch, but it never happened, in fact I found the service impeccable. Only down side is was the TV had this surround sound speaker set-up that is overly complex for the quality of the sound...

“Prima locatie” Dit hotel heeft een ontzettend goede locatie.
Je loopt zo het Grand park in waar je uitkomt bij het Shed aquarium
Shield museum en planetarium.Ook kun je de watertaxi nemen naar de Navy pier
vanaf hier.
Voor je het park inloopt bus 3 pakken die je voor 2,50 dollar in de magnificent mile afzet.
Hotel zelf is niet bijzonder.Zwembadwater is ijs koud.En dat de ramen totaal niet open konden om te luchten vondt ik zelfs smerig.Badkamer oud en met licht wat op een timer was.Alles deed een beetje groezelig aan.Maar voor 593 dollar voor 5 nachten en die locatie zou ik het iedereen die toch alleen maar slaapt aanraden

“Not Lovingly Preserved” It should be obvious to just about everyone viewing this site that this is not a world class stunner of a hotel. It's very old, like most buildings in New York, but one would expect such structures to be renovated, or at least properly maintained, and this place is not. I stayed here with my family in 2006, but it doesn't seem like much has changed since then. This was during one of NYC's most wickedly hot summers and even though we're from Miami, the extreme, concrete-contained humidity in the hundreds was like nothing we'd experienced. As we made our way through the mildew-scented, dingy-looking corridors (after entering through the immaculate lobby, of course), a sense of dread crept over us. This hotel was not going to be our shelter from the swelter. Our room was so hot and stuffy, we could barely make ourselves comfortable. Nor could even begin to assess the anciently drab decor. We just started calling our local relatives in panic and complaining to the staff. We were then upgraded to a "suite" with a shoddily-furnished sitting area, only one queen-sized bed paired with the most spine-disfiguring cot, and a temperature that was just a few touches tolerable. The view was nice, though. So, we stayed. But, we won't return. This place needs a renovation desperately.
“Not Ready for Primetime” The Hyatt folks recently purchased this property from Wyndam and it needs work! You will be disappointed.

“Disappointed” We had tickets to a show and decided to stay in town rather than drive home. We had stayed at the Hotel Icon before and loved it but wanted to try a different downtown hotel. There was no bell service at check-in we unloaded our own bags since there is no driveway and we were blocking traffic while waiting. After check in a bellhop appeared with a cart for our one bag. The elevators are small so we had to wait 3 times because there wasn't enough room for the bellhop, the cart, and us - if anyone else needed a ride up. The room was small. There were no drawers to unload our clothes into. The closet was SO small that we couldn't unload our hanging clothes and hang the hanging bag - and this was for a one night stay. The furniture was threadbare, not charming antiques but garage sale castoffs. The bathroom was stark and uncomfortable I didn't like being in it. It didn't look or feel clean. I sent in the comment card but with mildly stated details of my disappointment and no one has ever called to discuss. Also, small uncomfortable bed. No coffee maker in room or morning coffee in lobby. There wasn't a trashcan in the room or bathroom - I had to call to get one brought up and she told me she had to get it from another room (uh- I didn't want to know that!) We paid more to stay here than at the Hotel Icon - please no one else make the same mistake.

“Would never stay there again!” This hotel was nothing like the brochure and was very dated. The reception area always seemed to be full of people complete with luggage waiting for transportation or to check in no matter what time of day you passed through it. Our room was very small with a double bed directly behind the door, in fact it was pushed up against the wall and it was not possible to walk down one side of it. The room was very dated and dirty, I found someones toe nails stuck in the carpet! The lifts were always full and on occasions we had to wait up to 10 minutes with some elderly guests complaining that they had waited 45 minutes the previous morning.

“Comprehensive Review for Those Considering a Long Term Stay” Stayed at this hotel for 5 weeks for business reasons. Since I was a guest so long, I feel that I need to leave a comprehensive review. If you are just looking to stay here a night or so, it is a fine, if a bit dated, hotel and you could definitely do worse. Since we met so many at the hotel who stayed here for extended periods, I will systematicaly review the hotel with my opinions on the different aspects aimed at those considering long term stays.
RECEPTION/CHECK IN
The lady that checked us in was rude and unhelpful which got us off to a rough start. Thankfully, the vast majority of our interactions with the reception staff were very positive. Most are friendly, helpful and kind. Unfortunately, there are just a couple that probably need to improve their attitude.
ROOM
I was notified when we checked in that this was one of the original Residence Inns and there were no units that had partitioned rooms. Every room is a studio style. The set-up is much like an apartment complex with buildings with several units and everyone has their own walk up door. This is quite different than other Residence Inns that I have stayed at.
We were hoping for a suite with a separate bedroom since we have an infant but that was not an option here. We stayed in a penthouse suite which we would highly recommend for guests with children. There is a common living area with fireplace, TV, couch and chairs; full kitchen with all appliances including dishwasher and disposal with a small breakfast nook and pantry; small dining area with 4 chairs and table; a pull down double bed across from dining table; desk diagnonal to the dining area and next to where the bed pulls down then a full bath. We actually put our daughter’s pack and play in the hallway to the bathroom as it was the only quiet and dark place. They supplied a very loud fan that helped with the noise. Upstairs is another double bed, small desk, another flat screen TV and full bath.
The rooms are very drab and probably have not been updated in 10-15 years. Furniture is functional but well worn. The beds are ok but the pillows are super soft so be warned. I had to sleep with 3 of them. The upstairs gets VERY warm during the day even with a large fan they supplied and a ceiling fan both on high. We usually just didn’t go upstairs during the day.
CABLE/INTERNET
Cable is pretty decsent with 45 channels, all the ESPNs, movie channels, and HGTV. Missing the Food Network though. Internet was pretty slow and quite inconsistent. Pretty frustrating aspect of this property.
BREAKFAST/RECEPTIONS
Breakfast is served daily and there is a reception Monday-Thursday evenings with snacks and drinks. We were impressed with breakfast. I am not sure I would want to eat at my favorite restaurant every day for 5 weeks. So the fact that we would still go after 5 weeks is a testament to the variety and quality. The staff are incredible. Most of the reception food is like tortilla chips and nacho cheese but they have some days with cheeseburgers. Nothing great but if you are crunched for time or money, it is an option.
HOUSEKEEPING
Gems of this hotel! Nice, attentive, hard working. No complaints here.
POOL
Really nice pool area with two basketball courts, volleyball courts and a grilling area with two gas grills which we took advantage of a couple of times. They have balls and grill tools at the front desk. The pool is even 8ft at its deepest. The grounds crew sprays around the pool every day so it stays very clean.
LAUNDRY
Five washers and dryers that are large and efficient. Loads are $1 for both W or D and I never had to run a load twice in either machine.
SHUTTLE
Shuttle is operated by Blue Diamond and is shared with several other hotels in the area. I found it to be very frustrating: long waits, often spent over 30 minutes on the shuttle, drivers barely announcing that they are taking off, etc. The hotel is a 1.3 mile walk to the closest buildings of the medical center (Texas Children’s Hosptial) and takes 25-35 minutes depending on your pace so it is often faster to walk if you are able. I occasionaly rode the shuttle if it rained or I just happened to catch them but found overall that it wasn’t worth the trouble. One of the most frustrating experiences was waiting over an hour for a shuttle but they always go the same route so they kept filling up before they got to us. After calling the hotel a couple times, they agreed to pay for a taxi to get us back. I viewed the shuttle like I viewed the hotel’s the slow and inconsistent internet: if it works it is a bonus. I would not recommend this hotel if the shuttle is a main attirbuite that you are looking for. There are also several rental car companies (Avis, Hertz, Enterprise, Dollar and Thrifty) a mile or less away.
LOCATION
As above, not too far from the medical center campus but you can get closer with other hotels. You can function here without a car as Kroger (grocery) is next door and a CVS and Target are less than a half mile walk away. There are plenty of restaurants within a 15 minute walk (Spicy Pickle, Long John Silvers, Chipotle, Panda Express, Which Wich, Schlotskys, Wing Stop, Papa Johns, Taco Cabana, and many more). 
Hope this helps. Happy to answer individual questions if you want to private message me.

“Great accommodation in a great location . Lovely door staff .” Booked two luxury king suites via Expedia for a 4 night stay in January. Accommodation was excellent , spacious , clean and newly refurbished . One of the suites was missing mirrors in the bedroom and the "ice" concierge system , so probably only just finished. Couple of minor issues resolved immediately we flagged to maintainence. Beds and furnishings very comfortable and kitchenette well equipped. Huge living room . Tiny bathroom . 
The Upstairs Rooftop bar is beautiful but too pricey to hang out for long in . 3 gin and tonic cost over €50. Worth it for the experience but not every night ! 
Wifi at €12.95 per device per day mounts up with a couple of iPhone connections , but cheaper than paying international data roaming. 
Door/bell staff as friendly and helpful as I've met anywhere in New York . Front desk with one exception were a little brusque and the concierge staff managed to avoid any eye contact with us for 4 days . 
Unless you "need" hotel amenities like bar and communal areas , I'd highly recommend as very comfortable suite accommodation in a good location.

“Don't waste your money” I'm a frequesnt NYC traveller, usually for day trips, but this for for a weekend away. I had gotten a good price on this hotel, so I was expecting the room to be very small (but who goes to NYC to stay in the room anyway?) Checkin actually went pretty smooth. We got to the room, which was literally just a bed and a TV with a view of an alleyway (not recommended for the claustrophobic) After dropping off our bags, we went out.
