This hotel was located about 2-3 blocks from Rockefeller Center and across the street from the Gershwin Theatre and the Circle in the Square Theatre so in fact it was a really great location.
After coming back a few hours later we realized our room was pretty hot even though we had turned on the AC full blast when we left. Called the front desk & they sent up a repairman who said the AC was broken and couldn't be fixed til morning, but opened the window for us. Gee thanks!
So I went down to the front desk & had them change our room because I did not want to sleep in that sweat box all night. After swithicng to a new room (slightly bigger, w/ a better bathroom and nicer view- but still as small as a walk in closet) we were in bed when at about 11pm the door busts open. The hotel had booked someone else into the same room and given them a key. So after being extremely shaken up I tried to call the front desk only to discover that the phone was broken. So at around 11:30 had to get dressed and walk down to the front desk to explain the problem. They were "very sorry" but that was about it. So the next morning we are awaken by a repairman at 8:30 (even though there was a do not disturb sign on the door) wanting to fix our phone. We sent him away and since we were now up, went down to the front desk who said they would move our room again if we wanted. The front desk wasn't rude or anything, but wasn't really sorry that all these things kept happening.
We left the next day, making our displeasure known when we checked out. When we stay in a cheap hotel in NYC again, it will not be this one. This was by far one of the worst places I have ever stayed in.

“My Favourite NY Hotel” Until staying in the Sofitel, I had always been disappointed with New York hotels in the 4-star, $400-ish/night range. Other NY hotels I've tried in this class tend to either have tiny rooms, in need of renovation, have thin walls, are noisy (can hear street noises), etc. I had stayed in a Sofitel in London and loved it, so decided to give the NY one a try. It didn't let me down. Service first rate. Exquisitely comfortable bed. Rooms sparklingly clean and not tired-looking. Most importantly, pristinely quiet. Definitely will make this my hotel of choice in New York.

“Non è più all'altezza del suo nome” Considerato il numero di stelle ed il nome Intercontinental, non è assolutamente adeguato alle aspettative.
Sembra voler mantenere uno stile mooooolto classico, ma in realtà risulta spesso solo vecchio.
La camera è pulita e curata, ma si vedono i segni degli anni ed il bagno era molto piccolo.
Avevo una camera con un letto king e penso che si potrebbe ridurre la dimensione della camera per dare più respiro al bagno.
La posizione è molto buona comunque perchè ci si trova vicino alla gran centra e la mobilità è quindi semplificata ed agevolata.
Alla reception sono molto gentili e cortesi, ma i bell man sono decisamente spiacevoli. e se gli date 20 $ per caricare una valigia su un taxi vi guarderanno comunque con uno sguardo insoddisfatto che vi fa sentire dei barboni.
La connessione internet è a pagamento anche nella hall e questo è veramente scandaloso, soprattutto se si considera il prezzo dell camera.

“You will regret booking this hostel.” My family came to New York so excited, only to be completely let down before our trip even started.
Don't be fooled by the marketing or website photos.
Rooms- I have stayed in multiple hostels and dormitories with larger and cleaner facilities. Honestly, unless you are 2 feet tall, your body will barely fit in the entire room. My husband had to use the lobby bathroom that thousands of people use, as he could not sit down on the toilet without his legs painfully pushing into the door. The see-through shower is right next to the bed and there's nowhere else to go (a few inches of space on either side of the bed and that's it) which is awkward. We asked to move to a larger room- a rude staff member said they were sold out, and that if a larger room was available, it would be many hundreds of dollars more a night, and basically discouraged me. Believe me when I say stay away! 
They also told me Hudson had a complete renovation. When I asked where, they told me the beds were lifted so you can place a suitcase underneath (how thoughtful), and that the lighting was changed. This is a "major" renovation???
The lobby and bars are hip and fun, yes, but any joe schmo off the street can experience these outlets.
My advice- look at the photos guests post and compare square footage to other hotels. The hotel isn't a focal point of a trip to NYC, but also shouldn't ruin your vacation.

“Fabulous rooftop pool” We spent a great night at the Soho House, and we fell in love with the rooftop pool and cafe. The pool itself is very small -- but there weren't too many people actually swimming in it (and we saw only one person get their hair wet). No, this is a place to enjoy the view of river and skyline, to sip cocktails (ordered from the efficient wait staff) and to have a delicious lunch. The chic crowd made me feel a little frumpy, but the attitude quotient was low for a place this trendy, so we had a thoroughly enjoyable afternoon. 
Our room was pleasant, too, though less special. Our room (one of the smaller ones) was still quite spacious, though that's partly because there's no separate bathroom. Sink, tub and shower stall are all in the main room. (There's a small closet for the toilet.) I think I might find that wearing during a long stay, when I might want to catch a few extra minutes sleep while my husband is showering.
Other positives: The bed was extremely comfortable. Service in general was friendly and helpful. Free wireless service. Room is supplied with a few DVD's, in case you have a late-night urge to watch a movie. Location in the Meatpacking district, especially if you're into clubbing.
Negatives: The controls for AV system were impossible to decipher. I don't think there was an iPod dock -- strange for a place this up to date. Uncomfortable chairs that are too small for the accompanying table. The "black hole" between the huge credenza and the wall: easy to knock jewelry into it, much harder to retrieve it.
Would I come back here in the winter, when the pool is closed? Maybe not, but I know I'll be back next summer.

“Excellent location - most reasonably priced, small room” Personally I don't feel that this hotel is a boutique, I feel that it is a very well located hotel that is far cheaper than it's competitors in the area.
The service is friendly & attentive - we had a misunderstanding with our room service on 1 of our 5 day stay (our fault as we indicated we were still in the room instead of requesting housekeeping) this was resolved well.
The location is RIGHT IN Times Square & this prompted us to extend our stay. Subway is just down the street, Madison Sq, Hells Kitchen etc
Happy Hour is popular with the locals after work, it is packed. Contemporary, bar staff are excellent, cocktails are delicious & well priced.
Breakfast is a simple affair but is provided in fee - coffee, juice, bagels & pastries. 
Downsides: We paid the extra to upgrade to a queen room purely to have room to put our bags down - so trust previous reviews of room size - they are TINY, loud heating system, hot water takes a long time to come through, water is a rusty brown colour at times, can hear traffic on the street & a strong cold draught comes from under the heater, the bed is comfortable but there is a thin cotton blanket & a coverlet which I felt was insufficient in below freezing weather.

“Room 936 is a dirty dump, a Sheraton in name only” I arrived at 2am and was exhausted so didn't do my usual room inspection before saying yes and moving my luggage in...what a mistake. Dirt on the bathroom floor, high miscellaneous hair count (not mine) mold and mildew all over show tiles, burns on carpet, left over hairpins on floor, desk chair was a refuge from banquet dept., easy chair had huge tears on both arms, and the carpet hasn't been properly vacuumed in some time. I called the manager's office and housekeeping sent a supervisor who called it "appalling" and moved me to another room. I asked, "who supervises the work the maids do?" Three people cover 600 rooms so they are too busy to deal with many problems. Nobody seems to care in this hotel and it is full of bus tour and airline staff guests who have no other choice so they can get away wit h calling this shabby shack at Sheraton. It's like a roadside motel stacked up 22 floors high.
Wisely the hotel did not charge me for my night in room 936. Stay elsewhere the Sheraton NY Towers across the street is a completely different experience. Will I consider Four Points by Sheraton for another stay, anywhere? Heck no.

“Service is good..Hotel and Parking is poor” This hotel is in a great location for conventions and the staff is strong and experienced. Kudos to Louise at the front desk and Gabe at the bar for their excellent service. However, the rooms are dated and Ive had problems with rashes on my skin after staying here. The 50.00 parking cost is simply unacceptable given the location and the type of hotel here. I've often paid over 30 percent of my total lodging cost for parking. Also the construction of the new wing started before 7am which the hotel states starts at 9am.

“The right value for the price” like everyone else, i was a little apprehensive about the shared bathrooms, but they WERE VERY CLEAN!!
probably the cleanest hotel bathrooms i've ever encountered. it seems like they somehow clean them after every use.
the rooms were small, and yes, the elevator was too. but it's an old building and back then people didn't take up nearly as much space!
seriously, it was clean, comfortable, and the staff was helpful and friendly. the right (and expected) value for the price. i have no complaints.

“Good but noisy” Booked this motel on the internet having never been to Houston before. It was clean, well serviced and the staff were helpful. Motel 6 is a good budget motel choice and this is one of the nicer Motel 6 buildings we have stayed in. However, because it is by an intersection it is also one of the noisiest motels we stayed in. It didn't disturb us too much, but when we returned to Houston we enjoyed being away from the freeway.
It isn't very easy to walk anywhere from this motel but the location next to the freeway is very convenient. This motel felt safe, was well lit and was friendly.

“Amazing location and value” I only stayed for a night but was very happy with my selection. You never know what you're going to get in NYC sometimes but this turned out great. The room was a good since for NYC, I was on a high floor so I had a great view as well. The room included a fridge and a decent sized bathroom as well.
Its an HGI not a Hilton so not as fancy but the internet is free so who cares in the end. Its adjacent to a subway stop so an easy connection to anywhere you want to go in the city. The elevators require a key to get up to the rooms and there is lobby security at night though the location is so good they don't need it.

“Good but mixed” We had a good, but mixed stay at this hotel. We definitely liked the location – the Upper West Side beats Mid-Town hands down in my book. The subway is ultra-convenient, being literally two minute walk away. The neighborhood is also decent for grabbing a bite to eat (try Amsterdam over Broadway for better eats) and of course close to Central Park. And, the room is pretty spacious – especially for Manhattan. There were some negative things however. The bed was one of the worst I have experienced (bad mattress and bad sheets – clean but flimsy and coarse). If I was not so tired from walking around, it would have been a big problem. The shower we also had was bad – half the spouts did not work and the stream was weak. In general, a few areas of the room seemed to need more attention and up-dating. Finally, I did not like the Fairway across the street. It was sub-standard to a typical American grocery store (poor selection and high prices) and was crowded and stressful. I actually found a better selection of what I needed at the pharmacy down the street – this advice may save you some time and hassle.

“Excellent cheap option” Luckily I ended up at this property at 1 am after leaving another hotel that had bed bugs (ew!) Jose, the night manager, set me up in a fabulous and clean room for very cheap, and even helped me treat my clothing from the previous hotel experience so I had something to wear the next day. Their breakfast is fabulous and the rooms are extremely quiet for being so close to the airport. I was very grateful to the whole staff that salvaged my stay in houston. Two very big thumbs up!!

“Soho Grand was great!” We stayed at the Soho Grand over American Thanksgiving. It was our second time there and we had a really good experience. The woman who checked us in was very friendly -- big warm smile and a "welcome back". We had booked a regular Queen size bedroom and were upgraded to one of the newly renovated rooms. Very comfortable bed -- three or four different types of pillows -- from soft and feather to harder and foam -- nice duvets, a welcome plate of cookies and water and a personalized note from the manager -- generous bath products, windows you could open a titch to get some fresh air. They also threw in a free continental breakfast. All good. Small complaints -- not very good hot water pressure in the shower, especially in the evening for some reason. Hair dryer old and smelly (hard to explain, but the warm air coming out of it had an odd smell). We'd periodically see our check-in woman -- always very warm with a big smile. She was great! All of the staff were open and friendly, including the guys on the door, though the concierge(s) could be a bit stiff. Great drinks at the bar with a really good D.J. spinning fun tunes. Fabulous location. I've read some negative things about the Soho Grand, but both times (and especially this one), we've had a positive experience. We'll go back in a flash! Love the location.

“Worse Nasty Place Ever!!!!” Worse Hotel in NYC the area is not bad at all, but the service was really bad. Please avoid this hell hotel it is filthy dirty, it smell so bad, the room didn't even look like the pictures they have to view the rooms, there was no towels, hair dryer or ironing board in the room and the TV only had one channel. It was a nightmare we only stayed one night thank god.

“The wrong Hilton property to choose in New York” I only stayed here one night (for business), but had it been a longer stay, I would have changed hotels, something I never do.
When you first enter the hotel, you'll be impressed . . . friendly people, attractive and quaint lobby area, nice bar. So all that I needed was a decent room and that's where everything fell apart. As a Hilton Gold member, I usually get at least a pretty decent room. But when I showed up (around 3:30pm), I was told they only had a queen room at the moment and that the king room I was scheduled to have would not be available for a while. No problem I said, and took the queen room. I really don't care that much about room size, particularly since this was a one-night stay and I needed to get some work done.
When I entered the room (Room 1024), I was a little surprised. Chipping paint on the ceiling, and a couple of small chips of paint had fallen to the carpeting. In my opinion, this is not something you need to bring to the hotel's attention - see the picture I'm posting and judge for yourself. This is evidence of a hotel that's stopped caring. Then, the bathroom. It was clean enough, but smelled like mildew - I assume from the shower curtain, but I didn't investigate further. I just dealt with it. I'm a little angry at myself for not leaving the hotel right then.
And yet neither of the above two issues was my greatest issue. I went out to a business meeting and dinner, returned to the hotel at about 10:30, and got ready for bed. The room was a little warm so I turned on the a/c. It worked fine. But every time it cycled off, there was a loud bang and rattling of metal, which woke me up periodically throughout the night. We're not talking about typical city noises (I live in Chicago). I'm talking about what it would sound like if someone took a metal bar and starting smashing metal pipes . . . repeatedly, and loudly. Needless to say, not a great night of sleep.
I travel occasionally to New York for work and to visit family, and I've stayed at a Hampton Inn and Hilton Garden Inn, both near Times Square, as well as two Hiltons (one near Times Square, the other on 6th), the Doubletree Times Square and the Waldorf. All of the above would be acceptable if you're Hilton Honors and looking for a Hilton property. I can't recall which one I liked the most - the Hilton Garden Inn and Hampton were actually surprisingly nice, and I also liked the Hilton Times Square (Hilton on 6th seemed a bit dated) - but I would never return to this hotel.
Perhaps I just got a bad room - but nobody should be subject to a room like this one.

“Wanted to love Dream” I stayed at Dream for 3 nights. I wanted to love it, but I came away just liking it. It won't be a go-to hotel for when I'm in NYC, but I haven't counted it out.
Pros:
- location: steps from the subway, great breakfast options, Times Square/Broadway shows, Columbus Circle, etc
- clean, decent size room (*for NYC*). I've seen some complaints on here regarding the rooms being small... I've stayed in several MUCH smaller NYC hotel rooms. Unfortunately when visiting NYC you have to skew your idea of ideal room size.
- loved the apples that were in my room the first night.
Cons:
- know that if you're staying here, there's a lounge in the building. On Friday and Saturday night my room was vibrating from the bass, and if I wasn't as tired as I was, it would've kept me up. Not a huge thing for me, but I can see where it would bother some.
- room service ends at 11pm... I kind of wish it would be an hour or 2 later. Not a dealbreaker for me though.
Would I recommend Dream to a friend? Eh, maybe. It won't be an enthusiastic recommendation, but it's still on my list.

“Good Location for those who just want a bed to sleep” Ok, Ok, I read all the reviews and finally booked this hotel through a wholesaler. We arrived and checked in and were given room 509.
The room is tiny but it id not bother us as we were not in NYC to stay in the room to admire the 4 walls. We could not hear any street noise as our room faced another part of the hotel.
What I strongly advise management is to train the staff to be more engaging with their guests, like wishing them "hello" and "welcome", and maybe small talk. I mean it was me 90% of the time who had to wish them Happy Valentines or Have a nice day, etc....
I will not pay more than $100 per night, as we paid $120(priceline bid) at the Affinia for an excellent room with a kitchen and a full bath.
Below are the pros and cons for anyone deciding to stay at this hotel:
Pro's
Excellent location in Chelsea a block from the subway.
Very clean rooms and nice showers
Con's
Gym missing a Universal weights machine (underutilized gym)
Tight rooms but ok for 2 small people or one person
No fridge in room
Loud ventilation fan in the washroom
Loud room heater
Bed was too small for us(it was not a queen or king but rather a double bed)

“Your home in NYC” Having stayed at the hotel before, we nearly made the mistake of trying another hotel, for a change. How pleased we were that we decided to stick with The Regency.
We reserved a Park Avenue room, with more than ample space for tw persons, and indeed it could easily hold your two children as well!
The position of the hotel is perfect if you want to be near most stores and museums, yet be in a calm neighbourhood. The staff, many who have been at the hotel for many years, are just so guest orientated that you might be in the Four Seasons, but 4S is just too big to be comfortable.
Restaurants abound, and the subway is near. The only drawback is the current woeful value of the British Pound, makes NYC much more expensive than a year ago.
Hope this helps.

“Door-bells !! Comfy, spacious, clean, efficient fairly close to everything” We'd rate this hotel officially: 4.1
Terrific views, right on the Chicago river, almost bordering Lake Michigan, within easy walk to Michigan Ave., and tourist-heaven attractions: 5-10 min walk to Mag Mile shopping to the North; 10-15 min walk to Navy Pier to the NorthEast; 5-10 min walk to Millennium Park, Bean sculpture and Art Institute of Chicago to the South. All others, including Field Museum & Shedd Aquarium, are further South, but easily reachable by walking or taxi/bus. Plenty of restaurant options North or South, but we did not try the hotel versions.
The Good: The room was spacious, with terrific SouthEastern high-floor views (see pics) of the Lake and Millennium park, along with the condos/rentals nearby, built-up obviously for the views. The bed was comfortable, the layout was pleasant, with glass-topped credenza-desk, in-room safe, and relaxing club chair. The bathroom had a tub and a separate shower-enclosure (bit small to move around in, but worked), basic toiletries, and most importantly, clean. Loved the European style door bells for each door/room. There is a spacious fitness center, along with a pool on the top floor. We did not utilize but visited. Appeared clean, well-run. The elevators are a little different: when you request an elevator, you enter your floor # on a panel (instead of the usual up/down buttons), and it points you to a specific elevator to use. We guess it's more efficient, but could not tell. The front-desk was uneven, depending on who you get. First person during check-in was stone-faced, and barely trying. Did not explain too much about the hotel amenities, other than elevator uniqueness. The second person, for a request, was very friendly, willing to go above-beyond to assist. We did not request room service or visit the hotel's restaurant, which was a branch of the famous PALM restaurant. We're sure they were fine (and accordingly pricey), given the name, but we wanted to explore Chicago dining.
The bad: The only negative was the lobby area ... it felt congested, granted there were probably business meetings/conventions, or other activities in the city. The lobby felt small, even when there were less people. The area has a small general seating area, and then there were tables and seats for the bar/lounge ... small also. Even at check-in, it felt tight, and people seemed confused where to stand to form a line. Just too many people not having anywhere else to stand, mill about. The concierge desk faces the front-desk, making it feel even more tight. So think of it this way ... if there were people waiting on line to check-in/out, and there were people asking concierge questions, it gets messy. In-room internet access was not included ... Annoying!!! If using the Business Center, everything is chargeable, except for printing boarding passes ... another annoying !!!! 
Summary: Clean, comfortable, convenient hotel ... sort of in a triangular shape, that exudes quality. There are terrific views,especially on the high floors. The hotel is close to everything, as you're just about smack in the middle-point to reach points in all directions. We were comfortable, and would recommend this hotel to all.
Happy Hotel-ing !

“Hotel Is Suitable for a Quick Trip” For a quick weekend business trip, this hotel works. It has a central location and it is clean enough (I've stayed at much worse in NYC). But it is definitely showing its age and the rooms have taken a beating (peeling wallpaper, stained tubs, chipped furniture). Also, if you have noisy neighbors, the thin walls and adjoining rooms will be a nightmare. I was next to a group of kids on a field trip and had to request a room chance - it was so noisy. However, once I was moved, the sleep quality was just fine. Hotel is in a good location and service was very good.

“Microtel is the pits” We were booked in this hotel not by choice for a school field trip. They ran out of clean towels the first day and did not give us enough clean sheets for the pull out sofa. The pull out sofa was supposed to be a second bed. We were in for a shock when two moms and two kids were not given a room with two double beds. There was a hole in the wall in the stairway, the toilet had brown streaks in the bottom, there was no plug for the bath tub and the shower squealed when the hot water was on. The continental breakfast was pre-packaged muffins, a gallon of milk resting in an ice water bath, containers of Fruit Loops, coffee and juice. There was no fruit or yogurt and no one came to clean up the milk spills. The entire hotel was in a state of disrepair. There were scuff marks and dirt everywhere and a general state of uncleanliness. I would not stay in this chain ever again. We were terming the place the MICROHE**.

“Take a Pass...” Not clean, understaffed and awful service. Room smelled terrible, had to change. No doorman or anybody to help you with a bag. Valet parking a joke, nobody around, must find someone to park the car for $24 a day. Return to hotel after midnight, told to park on street and get car at 6:am because no valet on duty. Good location to musuems but spend the extra money someplace else and enjoy your travel. This place is a cash cow for owners, take out revenue, put nothing in.

“My Favorite Mag. Mile Hotel” I stayed here for one night this time but stayed here every week for almost a year back in 2003 (when the hotel had just opened). I had picked this hotel after trying 20+ downtown and Magnificent Mile hotels. It was good to see that the hotel has maintained it's high quality over the years. I love the location - it is a block from Michigan Avenue restaurants/shopping and 3 blocks from the Navy Pier. 
The new beds are even better than in 2003. It offers all the features that come with an Embassy Suites hotel - an atrium, all suites with a sofabed, cook to order breakfast and a manager's reception (open bar) in the evening, pool and a functional gym. If you are a HHonors Gold or Diamond member, you can use the neighboring Holmes Place health club for free. It is probably the best health club I have been to in the US. The rooms facing Navy Pier or the river offer the best views. Overall, a great, comfortable hotel.
“Pretty Good” They're going through renovations so there are some basic issues that come with that. What I liked was the staff: the staff was great. That made up for some other challenges.

“Never Again” My wife and I stayed at this hotel given it's convenient location to Central Park and on 6th Avenue. We stayed here in the past and enjoyed our stay, so we thought we'd stay there again. The visit was marred by a rude staff, $51.00 a day parking (with also rude staff), and the hotel placed two expensive "holds" on my credit card that blocked me from being able to use my credit card. The hotel is extremely expensive for what you can get for the same money in Manhattan. I will not be returning and won't recommend anyone else do the same.

“fantastic hotel - pinklady Trinidad” Our family had the most memorable trip to nyc last spring, most of which we attribute to this fantastic hotel. Its location is ideal, smack in the middle of Times Square. The rooms were virtually sound proof and the beds were equivalent to sleeping on clouds. There are also many restaurants to choose from and a very friendly staff. The major addiction were those supersonic elevators!

“Not Bad” For what I paid I could have stayed in a midtown hotel. Nonetheless, it did its job. As far as the location, it's a bit out of the way if you want to see all of the attractions, although it's only a short walk to the park. There are plenty of places to eat nearby, but it's a good ten minute walk to the lively bars on Amsterdam Avenue. The room was a bit small compared to what we've been used to, but it was okay. The reception staff were friendly and helpful, although the cleaning staff were a bit noisy in the corridors on a morning, which might not have gone down too well with anyone nursing a hangover. Even so, I quite enjoyed my stay at this hotel and in The Upper West Side area. This might not be the ideal location for everyone. However, for anyone that wishes to escape the tourist atmosphere of the midtown area and experience a bit more of what Manhattan has to offer it does have its advantages.On the whole, not bad.
“First Class” This is a first rate hotel. The staff is unusually friendly and helpful and the rooms are clean and comfortable. The location can't be beat. One of the best places to stay in NYC!

“Great Location, Modern Room, and Excellent Concierge” Check-In was not impressive. What should have taken 5 minutes, turned into 20 because of the lack of training. I'd requested a quiet room, instead got the room outside of the loudest ice maker on earth. Called downstairs and was moved in under 10 minutes. The hotel room was nice, love the ability to hook up my laptop to the flat screen. The concierge had top shelf beverages and the food was excellent. I hope to stay here again on my next trip to NYC

“Kimpton's Ink-48 Makes a GREAT Impression” Stayed 1 night at Ink-48 (Kimpton property) located on the Upper West Side. Was convenient 1 block from the West Side Highway. 
Valet Parking & attentive bell hop & check-in service. 
Lobby has a great retro vibe (calling all fans of Don Draper & Mad Men) this hotel is named INK as it was a print shop, mid-century modern decor & lighting. We didn't eat at the restaurant, but did stop for a night cap at the bar - great vibe & banquet seating, tufeted booths, chic lighting. 
We stayed in a 1 bedroom suite with a fantastic view of the USS Intrepid - very large and well appointed - the bathroom was enormous! All marble, a giant tub. We had a comfortable seating area separate from the bedroom also had a large TV (low/platform bed) great lux bedding. 
Very clean, Very cool - There isn't anything I didn't like about this property - I highly recommend & look forward to returning.

“Expectations exceeded” We got this through a random site and were a little disappointed initially but our expectations have definitely been exceeded. We did have to wait a few minutes to check in, and the hallways are a little drab, but otherwise, it's a really nice place to stay. We asked for an upgrade because I needed space to work and it was $30, but we took it and it was well worth it. Our room was huge (almost too big- the tv was a bit far from the bed), with a nice chaise, a very comfy bed, and a large bathroom. I wouldn't call it especially nicely decorated, as it mostly was just pictures of Chicago, but it was nevertheless pretty tasteful, clean, and functional. Service was prompt and friendly in all matters. A few details were missed, such as a slow-flushing toilet, a slow-draining shower, and the lack of an ipod dock/modern radio. Otherwise, everything was spot-on. Finally, you can’t say enough about the location. Right there for food, transportation, shopping, everything really. As a Chicagoan on a staycation, I can say that this is definitely in one of the very best locations in the city, right there with the Ritz Carlton and Park Hyatt around the John Hancock Building and on Michigan Ave. The view of the towering Hancock was fine for us, but I’m sure the lake and Mich Ave views are even better.

“Top Hotel in NY” Sicherlich eines der teuersten aber auch der besten Hotels in NY. Top Lage am Columbus Circle, in Laufnähe zu den wichtigesten Shoppingorten als auch Museen (bsp: Guggenheim). Obwohl es sich hierbei um ein klassisches "City-Hotel" handelt ist das SPA ebenfalls sehr zu empfehlen. Der Pool ist für ein solches Hotel außergewöhnlich groß und bietet einen super Blick auf den Hudson.

“Good Location, Great Price” I'm really amazed by some of the reviews I have read on this hotel. If I had thought to check Tripadvisor before going to New York City last week, I probably would have changed my plans and stayed somewhere else but I'm glad I didn't because the place really isn't that bad.
No, there is not an elevator. Lugging baggage up to the third and fourth floor is a little inconvenient, but it's not like you have to do it everytime you come and go to your room...just at check-in and checkout. Besides, most of us could really use the exercise...enough said.
The rooms are the typical New York City small and very simple but also clean and comfortable. I had a double bed w/ private bath so I didn't actually use the shared bath and shower facilities but they were right across the hall from my room and looked very clean and new.
The mattress was definitely not the best, but I slept just fine and didn't have the back pain that I usually experience on a cheap mattress. The hotel is in the middle of a renovation. My room had the new hardwood floor as found in the hallways throughout the building and brand new shower, toilet and sink.
There is no cable television but there is a T.V. in the room so you can at least catch the news while you're there (which came in handy since I was there during the subway scare). There is also no phone in the room which didn't matter to me since I had my cell phone but I didn't see any pay phones so I could see how this might be an inconvenience to some people.
Sohotel is about a two block walk from the Grand/Chrystie St. D and B subway station which is great since from either one of those lines you can get to just about anywhere in Manhattan, Queens and Brooklyn either directly or by transferring at West 4th, Penn Station or Times Square (42nd St.).
The staff could have been more inviting but they were definitely not the drug mongrels that previous guests seem to have encountered. The manager (at least, I think he was the manager) was very nice. He offered a friendly hello every time I saw him and the guest room attendants are also quite friendly and do their jobs well. The front desk staff could use some work...but since you're not paying for a four-star hotel, it's not right to expect four-star service.
Actually, the girl who was behind the front desk when I checked out was very friendly. She stored my bag for me ($5.00 charge per bag -- which is less than some of the nicer properties in midtown charge) and wouldn't even accept a tip. She just smiled and said that she enjoys doing her job.
As for being racist, I feel the need to comment on this only because I saw people from all over the world checking into the hotel during my stay and the front desk staff showed each of them the same level of service. Was it the best service? Absolutely not...but for the price, it is better than I expected.
I think Americans especially have gotten too used to paying low rates while expecting very high levels of service. I work in the hotel business and hear this all the time....people book a rate of $100 per night and expect to be upgraded to the nicest suites and get the most friendly service.
I would ask you to think of it this way...the less you are paying for a room probably converts to the hotel staff being paid less. How would you feel about giving four and five-star service to individuals who could care less about how much you are making so long as they're paying a one-star nightly rate.
Give this hotel a try. It's great for travelers on a budget.

“Location, Location,Location” I have stayed in this hotel several times over the last 10 years and it is in a perfect location.. The staff is very responsive and friendly, but the rooms need updating badly. The room was clean, but the furnishings are really worn. The noise from adjacent rooms also can be very distracting late at night. I could hear my neighbor's conversations quite clearly. It would be nice to be able to cool the rooms a bit more when the weather fluctuates this time of year. The restaurant is only open for breakfast and I feel that the buffet is a bit expensive for what is offered. It would be nice if the bar were open in the evening. It is located in the theater district, making it very convenient the shows and other attractions. It is also very close to several subway stops. One great convenience are the computers in the lobby for guests.

“Great Location and Fair Room” I stayed at the Grand Hyatt the weekend of July 21. It is in a great location since you are next to Grand Central. However, I have been to NYC a few times but if you are going for your first visit I would recommend staying somewhere near the Times Square area.
The rooms were average nothing special. But I do not go to NYC to sit in the room. 
For the priceline rate I paid it was fine. I would try somewhere else next time but it was Ok.

“Never again” The staff was great but the hotel has two main flaws. 1) if you have at&t wireless you will have zero reception here. Zero. This is the only place in NYC I have ever experienced this to this extent. We are talking zero bars. 
2) the bathroom shower is glass and the room has a view. Aka you can sit on the bed and watch everything your partner does in bathroom. Everything. 
Call me old fashioned but I like my phone to work and a little privacy. We checked out after one night. The gym was very small also and the furniture was very college dorm ikea

“A little sad about the Regency” We stayed in the Regency for 4 nights. I've stayed there before and encountered no problems, but this time was different. We were not able to check in till around 3:45 PM, though I had requested early check in. The room needed repair, though small, an area of the ceiling needed repair and the carpet in a small area needed repair. The closet door didn't work and one light didn't work as well. The stay went fine and the cleaning staff does an excellent job. I would not rate this hotel at this time a 5 star. I think it is time to update at least some of the rooms. I'm not sure, but we did get the room at a special rate and maybe that is why the room was a little less than expected and we had problems upon check in.

“Perfect location” We stayed here last December and had an exceptional stay. Spacious room, masses of wardrobe space, huge and perfect bathroom, fantastic views and the most helpful staff & concierge Ive ever met. It is in a fantastic location - and is absolubtly everything a good hotel should be.
Only negative - was the Joel Robuchon restaurant, the food wasnt bad but portions were tiny and despite ordering twice the reccomended amount we didnt stumble across a single dish we would rave about. Not a disaster - but there are an abundance of better places about. 
Wouldnt go back to Atelier - but would return to the hotel anytime.

“im moment etwas laut, aber herrliche Aussicht” Wir waren im September 2010 zu Gast und hatten ein Suite im 33. Stock mit direktem Blick auf Ground Zero. Das war auch der Grund, für den einzig zu machenden Abstrich. Die Baustelle ist von 6.00-0.00 Uhr in Betrieb und auch bei geschlossenem Fenster deutlich zu hören. Aber die Aussicht hat dafür mehr als entschädigt, die Skyline bei Nacht war fast nicht zu toppen. 
Ein weiterer Minuspunkt war, dass das Hotel keine Gepäckaufbewahrung über Nacht anbietet. Da wir einige Tage ausserhalb von NYC waren, hätte uns dies gut zugute kommen können.

“modesto ma utilissimo” Si tratta di un motel modesto situato in North Lasalle, in zona a North del Loop e decisamente vicina al centro. Volendo si può arrivare nel Loop anche a piedi ma sono comodissimi i mezzi pubblici (l'abbonamento 3 giorni a RTA 14 $ consente di viaggiare indistintamente sia sulla EL che sui bus). L'aspetto esteriore è abbastanza modesto mentre invece le camere sono grandi, ben fornite di quello che serve ed anche pulite. Decisamente ottimo il prezzo vista la posizione.

“Always an extraordinary experience” Manhattan offers travelers a large array of high-end choices, some of which are landmarks like the Waldorf and the Waldorf Towers. While I've enjoyed the stark modernism of the Four Seasons and the Mandarin, I keep coming back to the Towers for its incredible beauty and sense of history. The rooms in the Waldorf itself are lovely and beautifully maintained by a very large housekeeping staff, but the Towers suites each tell a story about the world political and entertainment figures who have visited or lived in each. It's great fun to have a full dressing room or fireplaced living room or perhaps even a large dining room and kitchen and know history -- or an interesting romance -- happened in the same place. While the regular part of the hotel may seem like another NYC tourist trap, the staff at the Towers works hard to make you feel your privacy and comfort are their goal. Of course, it's not an inexpensive choice and the parking price keeps going up, but it's an incredibly well maintained landmark property.

“Lo peor de houston” Ok se supone que nos ibamos a quedar en un buen hotel y que esta HORRIBLE, para empezar se taradarón como 2horas en encontrar mi reseración, luego me dijierón que mi suite no estaba y me dierón un horrible cuarto y solo me regalarón una cena y un desayuno. El servicio regular y para acabar la cena horrible, talvez 1 de cada 100 personas reciben algo mal de este hotel y yo fui una de esas, trarten de quedarse en otro como el four seasons o el marriot o si buscan algo economico el westin galleria

“An excellent host” Spent a couple of nights at 70 Park during November.
70 Park customer service is really tops. Everyone was very friendly and helpful. 
The hotel is a low rise building so there isn’t much natural light coming into the rooms so you would have to turn on the lights during the day time. Even though the location is near many points of interest, Park Avenue doesn’t seem to be too busy compared to other numeric avenues so waiting for taxis might be a bit longer.
The room was clean and nicely decorated. The room was also quiet except for loud door slamming but since there wasn’t too many rooms per floor, it didn’t bother me much. 
Other people have mentioned the unavailability of hot water which can affect your whole enjoyment of your stay. There is hot water from the sink tap but for showering, hot water is just barely luke-warm. I think it is a matter of plumbing which cannot be easily fixed for an old building.
The bed was comfortable and the sheets were of excellent quality but the pillows have absolutely no firmness to them. When you lay down on them, your head just sinks to the bottom. I think the pillows have past their lifespan.
I appreciated the extra-large flat screen TV but unfortunately the hotel only subscribed to few watchable channels.
Compared to other wine receptions at other hotels, I was surprised that 70 Park made it inviting, not intimidating, with various beverage choices and allowing self-serve non-alcoholic drinks. 
I enjoyed my stay and would probably try other Kimpton hotels in New York next time or even stay at 70 Park again.

“An Overly Priced Bar With Unfriendly and Rushed Service” I am writing my review based on my experience of the Broadway Lounge at the hotel lobby:
We sat down with a nice table overlooking Times Square. We wanted to order some coffee (I wanted to go for a Martini but it was too early for one) and something quick to eat. 
The ambiance of the hotel lobby and bar is very nice, clean, airy and I was very impressed with how nice everything look... but that's just the only good thing that this place has.
After waiting for over 15 minutes and seeing one waiter running back and forth all over the place, I thought we had to order at the bar. As I started walking that way, the very rushed, unfriendly and sweaty waiter took our order and left. We ordered to cafe lattes and an order of the chicken quesadilla.
About 10 minutes later, we got our lattes and about 5 minutes later the quesadilla. I do have to say, the food was pretty good as well as the lattes. Mind you that the whole time we sat there with our coffees and empty water glasses, no one came over to check on us. Meanwhile, I did see a few servers standing by the bar area chatting away and even one of the managers walking around the restaurant but not really doing anything... hum...
All of the sudden a crazy man that we saw earlier on the street singing to himself, found his way to the bar and now he is at the piano, playing it rather badly. Another one of the very unfriendly servers was talking to a patron and I asked her to come over and she yelled from 2 tables away: "I will send you your server!" I told her to come over and explained what it was. She left. A few minutes later, a security person removed the man from the piano but not escorted out. My friend actually ran into him in the bathroom (after the piano fiasco) talking to himself and screaming profanities.
We left the bar but I am rather disappointed that the personnel here is so rushed, unfriendly, not following through with customers needs and just with a "I don't care" attitude. I have received better service at lower class places and if I am paying $30 for 2 coffees and a quesadilla, I do expect to receive service from someone who cares.

“Nice hotel and staff, worst mattress in years!” Since I didn't want to fight the rush hour traffic and given the the Marriott was booked, I chose to stay at this Courtyard the night before my return flight home. Never again.
The hotel is in great condition, it has been recently renovated, has the Bistro restaurant which served me a nice sandwich for supper at a decent price. The room was very clean and the staff were all full of smiles.
In renovating the hotel they must have run out of money for mattresses. The one I had in my room was simply awful. It was hard, sloped in the middle and towards the front of the headboard and I awoke with a backache. Despite my request for a feather free room the pillows were probably filled will down as I was sneezing too by morning. Room 118.
Since I am a lifetime Platinum with Marriott I will contact their Corporate offices as well.
This hotel will be placed on my "do not stay again" list.
“Excellent large convention hotel” Very nice room, quite large, good workspace, on the Hilton Honors floor. Very nice & friendly staff. Excellent breakfast buffet.

“Not up to par....” I stayed at this hotel a few years ago when it was brand new. The location is excellent for a Broadway Theater trip. We decided to give it another try as our first experience was wonderful. Since opening, the hotel has been acquired by the Kimpton Hotel group and undergone a total renovation. 
Arrived at midnight after a long day of travel. Was shown to a beautifully remodeled room (an executive suite). All was well until 6:30am when we awoke to the most horrific construction noise I have ever encountered. After an hour of trying to sleep thru it we gave up. I called the front desk to request a new room. 
A room just like we were in but on a different side of the hotel away from the construction would perhaps be available later in the day. However a room considered a step above the one we were in was offered. I was taken to this room. Tho it was still on the same side as the construction, it was many floors higher and the noise was considereably muffled. The problem was, tho all rooms in the hotel are now non-smoking, this one reeked of cigarette odor. The concierge couldn't smell it but promised an ionizer. After some consideration i agreed as there was no guarantee that the other room offered would be less noisy than the first. 
Returning to my room after a day of shopping and dinner, I could tell no difference in the odor. 
Another irritation....the onsite restaurant is one of our favorites in the city. One evening of our visit we made dinner reservations. Upon arriving for dinner, we were taken to a basement conference room that had been set up for dining. Apparantly they were in the midst of a liquor licensing issue and could not serve in the restaurant. The banquet room seating was TINY.,...bistro type tables, tiny uncomfortable chairs. After perusing the menu for a moment, we realized we were upset that no mention of the alternative dining arrangements had been made at the time of reservation and we were uncomfortable and unhappy. So we chose to leave.
Additionally....one day of our visit was a rainy one. There was ONE umbrella in the room. Upon departing for the day we requested a second from the front desk. We were told they were all given out and that we should purchase one after leaving the hotel..."it could be our NYCity souvenir"! ...not the answer i was looking for. 
So we stepped outside, two of us, under our one umbrella. We needed a cab and asked the doorman if he could hail one. His response was that we'd be better off walking to the corner of 6th and 46th as there was already a gentleman ahead of us and we'd just have better luck up there. 
In closing....i doubt we'll give this a third shot. I had considered the Westin Times Square and as well The Michelangelo. I believe we would have gotten better service at either of these locations. We visit NYCity at least twice a year. We usually stay at a Ritz property but thought we'd try something a little closer to the theater district this time. Bad choice !!!
